volume.h1:
SOUTH INDIAN INSCRIPTIONS
VOLUME XXIV
INSCRIPTIONS OF THE RAṄGANĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE, ŚRĪRAṄGAM.

inscription.head:
No. 1.
(A.R. No. 69 of 1892.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, RIGHT SIDE OF THE DOOR-JAMB AT THE ENTRANCE INTO THE
THRESHING FLOOR.
inscription.p:
Records a decision of the members of the Peruṅguṟisabhā of Śrīraṅgam
disqualifying persons who were not residents of the village from holding the
dēvadāna and other lands from the 5th year onwards of the king's reign and
stipulates a fine of 25 poṉ on the members of the paṇḍāravāriyam and the
karaṇattār who violated this decision.

The record is dated in the 4th year and 89th day of the reign of Rāja-
kēsarivarman who may be identified with Āditya I (A.D. 871-907) on palaeo-
graphical consideration.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. IV, No. 516.

inscription.head:
No. 2.
(A.R. No. 70 of 1892.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, RIGHT OF ENTRANCE INTO THE THRESHING FLOOR.
inscription.p:
Registers an endowment of 90 kaḻañju of śemmai gold by Puttaḍigaḷ, son of
Kārāṇai Viḻuppēraraiyar Aṟivāḷaḍigaḷ, towards the feeding of four Brāh-
maṇas. The sabhā of Tiruvaraṅgam (Śrīraṅgam) undertakes to supply 120
kalam of ripe (old) paddy annually measured out with the measure called
Śeṉṉaḍaikkāl for the interest on the endowed amount. The record is dated
in the 26th year and 181st day of the reign of Rājakēsarivarman who may be
identified with Āditya I (A.D. 871-907) on palaeographical consideration.
Published in the S.I.I., Vol. IV, No. 517.

inscription.head:
No. 3.
(A.R. No. 73 of 1892.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, LEFT OF ENTRANCE INTO THE THRESHING FLOOR.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of gold towards feeding a Brāhmaṇa well versed in the
Vēdas on the occasion of the midday service, by Puśaṁkaṉ Pirānterumāṉ
son of Dēvaṉār of Taḷikkoṭṭūr in Neṉmali-nāḍu. The record is dated in the
2nd year of Parakēsarivarman probably Parāntaka I.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. IV. No. 520.

l.ta: ñcu kaṟiyu māḻākku neyum tayir nā-

l.note: The rest of the inscription is completely damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 4.
(A.R. No. 72 of 1892.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, LEFT SIDE OF THE DOOR-JAMB AT THE ENTRANCE
INTO THE THRESHING FLOOR.
inscription.p:
Registers gift of a silver lamp with stand and gold towards its maintenance
for burning Bhīmasēni camphor by Śaṅkaraṉ Iraṇaśiṅgaṉ of Tiruvarai in
Teṉṉāḍu. The record is dated in the 17th year (923-24 A.D.) of Madiraikoṇḍa
Parakēsarivarman i.e., Parāntaka I. cf. No. 12 below.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. IV., No. 519.

inscription.head:
No. 5.
(A.R. No. 345 of 1918.)
STONE BUILT INTO THE FLOOR NEAR THE OTTAIKKĀL-MAṆḌAPAM
ATTACHED TO THE ŪÑJAL-MAṆḌAPAM.
inscription.p:
Damaged and worn out. Refers to the platform (tiṇṇai) raised for the
flag (tirukkoḍi) by Nārāyaṇaṉ ••• ••• •••• alias Teṉṉavaṉ-Brahmādhirā-
yaṉ, the Śrīkāryam of the temple. The record is dated in the [30]th year
(936-37 A.D.) of the king.

edition.l:
[3]0— āvatu śrī parakesari •• māruḷum ••
ṭṭa [olai] śrī kāryya-
[ti]ṇṇai [||]—

inscription.head:
No. 6.
(A.R. No. 71 of 1892.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, LEFT OF ENTRANCE INTO THE THRESHING FLOOR.
inscription.p:
Registers the gift of a vessel Sahasradhārā and 100 kaḻañju of gold
towards the sacred bath of the deity by ••• ••••• Pallavaraiyaṉ alias Gaṇattāṉ
•••• The record is dated in the 38th year (944-45 A.D.) of the king.
Published in the S.I.I., Vol. IV, No. 518.

l.note: This is the symbol for poṉ
This is the symbol for poṉ

inscription.head:
No. 7.
(A.R. No. 415 of 1961-62.)
PROPER RIGHT JAMB OF THE ENTRANCE INTO THE CHANDANA-
MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
Records a gift of land in Śāsanamaṅgalam in Viḷā-nāḍu for food offerings
on the occasion of the bathing ceremony of the god on the day of Rōhiṇī, the
day of tirumuḷaippuḻudi during the Paṅguṉi-Uttiram festival by Āchchiyaṉ-
Bhaṭṭaṉ alias Śrī Vāsudēvaṉ, a brahmādhirāja of Peruveṅgūr in Viḷā-nāḍu.
This record is dated in the 39th year (945-46 A.D.) of the reign of Parāntaka I.
cf. No. 9 below.

l.ta: nāṟpattu nāṟ kalam ivai śrī ko-

inscription.head:
No. 8.
(A.R. No. 417 of 1961-62.)
PROPER LEFT JAMB OF THE ENTRANCE INTO THE
CHANDANA-MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
This epigraph which is damaged seems to record the gift of some jewels
by Tāṉañjaṉ Nārāyaṇaṉ of Puḷḷamaṅgalam in Kalār-kūṟṟam.

It is dated in the reign of Parāntaka I. The date may be any year from
thirty-one to thirty-nine.

inscription.head:
No. 9.
(A.R. No. 95 of 1936-37.)
SLAB KEPT NEAR THE DĒVASTHĀNAM MUSEUM.
inscription.p:
Records a gift by Brahmādhirājaṉ alias Āchchiyaṉ-Bhaṭṭaṉ Śrī Vāsudēvaṉ
Chakrapāṇi of Peruveṅgūr (vide No. 7 above) of two pieces of land in Śāsana-
maṅgalam, a tax-free brahmadēya of his own in Viḷā-nāḍu, for sweet cake
offerings to the god on the day of Ēkādaśi, during the Paṅguṉi festival in the
temple. Mentions the measure called ‘Rājakēsari’. The record is dated
in the 41st year (948-49 A.D.) of the king. cf. No. 7 above.

l.ta: ḷ appap perun tiruvamutu ceytaruḷa eṉ
peru nāṉ kellaiyi lakappaṭṭa nilam eṭṭu mā-

inscription.head:
No. 10.
(A.R. No. 418 of 1961-62.)
PROPER LEFT JAMB OF THE ENTRANCE INTO THE CHANDANA-MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
Records a similar gift of jewels as recorded in No. 8 above by Teṉ[ṉavaṉ]
Brahmaśrīrājaṉ alias Nārāyaṇa ••• ••• raiyaṉ of Puḷḷamaṅgalam.

The record is dated in the reign of Parāntaka I. The regnal year may be
any year between fortyone to forty-eight, the latest known regnal year for
this king.

inscription.head:
No. 11.
(A.R. No. 416 of 1961-62.)
PROPER RIGHT JAMB OF THE ENTRANCE INTO THE CHANDANA-MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
Records the creation of an endowment of sixty kaḻañju of gold by Sōmadēva-
bhaṭṭaṉ of Pāppaṉachchēri in Tirukkaṇṇamaṅgalam in Tirumarugal-nāḍu for
feeding a brāhmaṇa well-versed in the vēdas.

The epigraph is in two sections, one in Sanskrit engraved in Grantha
and the other in Tamil characters of about the 10th century. The record
which is dated in the 4th year in the reign of a Rājakēsarivarmaṉ may be
assigned to Gaṇḍarāditya or Sundara-chōḷa on grounds of palaeography.

edition.l: svastiśrī raṅganāthasya pādapaṅka[ja] The first six lines appear to be a palimpsest.-

inscription.head:
No. 12.
(A.R. No. 65 of 1938-39.)
ROUND PILLAR SET UP IN THE CHANDANA-MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
Records that the members of the sabhā of Śrīraṅgam agreed to run the
service of burning a lamp in front of Tiruvaraṅgattu-Perumāṉaḍigaḷ with
Bhīmasēṉi-kaṟpūram (camphor) and ghee provided for, from the interest
on 71 kaḻañju of gold invested by Śrīdharaṉ Kumaraṉ, a Malaiyāḷaṉ of
Iravimaṅgalam in Vaḷḷuva-nāḍu of Malai-nāḍu. The record is dated in the
fifteenth regnal year of Uttama Chōḷa. cf. No. 4 above.

inscription.head:
No. 13.
(A.R. No. 343 of 1918.)
TIERS OF THE OTTAIKKĀL-MAṆḌAPAM ATTACHED TO THE
ŪÑJAL-MAṆḌAPAM.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Mentions Kuravaṉ •••• the commander (Sēnāpati)
of Rājarājadēva (I). The other details are lost. Contains a portion of the
praśasti of the king.

edition.l: -ṭaiyār śrīrājarājadēvar [ sēn] āpati kuravaṉ

inscription.head:
No. 14.
(A.R. No. 342 of 1918.)
TIERS OF THE OTTAIKKĀL-MAṆḌAPAM ATTACHED TO THE
ŪÑJAL-MAṆḌAPAM.
inscription.p:
Built in. Registers a gift of 40 kaḻañju for several offerings to the god.
The record belongs to Kōvirāja-Rājakēsari i.e. Rājarāja I. Another fragment
below this contains part of the praśasti of Rājēndrachōḷa I.

l.ta: ṭe ceḻiyarait tecu koḷ śrī kovirājarājake
rūpāy pon nāṟpatin kaḻañcuṉāl kaḻañciṉ vāttiṅ

edition.p: Another fragment in similar characters reads as follows:—

inscription.head:
No. 15.
(A.R. No. 327 of 1952-53.)
TIRUKKUṞAḶAPPAṈ (VĀMANA) SHRINE, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Seems to refer to some transactions of the Peruṅguṟi-
sabhā of Uttamaśīli-chaturvēdimaṅgalam. One of the fragments refers to
the sabhā of Nandivarmamaṅgalam. The record is dated in the 7th regnal
year (991-992 A.D.) of Rājarājakēsarivarman i.e., Rājarāja I. This and
the following two numbers also consist of fragments of records some of which
seem to have belonged to the temple at Tiruvāṉaikkā in Jambukēśvaram
(see No. 16 below).

inscription.head:
No. 16.
(A.R. No. 328 of 1952-53.)
SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
Fragment. One of the fragments cites the 7th year (991-92 A.D.) of reign
of Rājarājakēsarivarman (i.e., Rājarāja I) and mentions a certain Pallavaraiyaṉ
of Paḻuvūr from whom some lands seem to have been bought. Another
fragment refers to the deity Purushōttamattup-Perumāḷ. Two other frag-
ments mention the deity Āṉaik[kāvil-Embe]rumāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 17.
(A.R. No. 331 of 1952-53.)
SAME PLACE, TIERS OF THE WALL.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Mentions the sabhā of [Uttamaśīli]-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
and seems to refer to some lands in Vīranārāyaṇanallūr. The record is dated
in the 7th regnal year (991-92 A.D.) of Rājarājakēsarivarman i.e., Rājarāja I.

inscription.head:
No. 18.
(A.R. No. 344 (b) of 1918.)
TIERS OF THE OTTAIKKĀL-MAṆḌAPAM ATTACHED TO THE ŪÑJAL-MAṆḌAPAM.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Seems to record a gift of gold for certain offerings to god
in the 19th regnal year (1003-04 A.D.) of Rājarājadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 19.
(A.R. No. 370 of 1953-54.)
HEXAGONAL COLUMN OF A PILLAR LYING IN FRONT OF THE ANCIENT PADDY-
STORING ROOMS IN THE KOṬṬĀRAM.
inscription.p:
Slightly damaged in the beginning. Contains the praśasti commencing
with Tirumagaḷ-pōla etc., Registers a gift of a hundred cows for the daily
supply of four nāḻi of milk measured by the (liquid-measure called) śēṉāṉaḍai
for the pāl-amidu service to the god by Madurāntakaṉ Sundara-śōḻaṉ alias
Rājarāja-Īḷaṅgōvēḷār in the 32nd regnal year of Rājarāja (I).

The writing is in a spiral, starting at the top of the column and ending at
its bottom.

inscription.head:
No. 20.
(A.R. No. 341 of 1918.)
TIERS OF THE OTTAIKKĀL-MAṆḌAPAM ATTACHED TO THE ŪÑJAL-MAṆḌAPAM.
inscription.p:
The inscription is built in. Registers a gift of 14 kaḻañju of gold by the
assembly of Tiruvaraṅgam for offerings to the deity during the festivals in
the months of Paṅguṉi and Aippasi. Mentions Vēmbaṟṟūr alias Avaṉināraṇa
chaturvēdimaṅgalam. The record is dated in the 24th year of the king.

l.ta: tu nicatam iraṇṭu paruppup poṉakam paḻampaṭi amitu ceytaru ••• ••••

inscription.head:
No. 21.
(A.R. No. 344(a) of 1918.)
TIERS OF THE OTTAIKKĀL-MAṆḌAPAM ATTACHED TO THE ŪÑJAL-MAṆḌAPAM.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. The inscription is dated in the reign (year lost) of
Parakēsarivarman Rājēndrachōḷadēva (acc. 1012 A.D). Mentions Kuravaṉ
Karumāṇikkattār.

inscription.head:
No. 22.
(A.R. No. 19 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST OF NĀḺIKĒṬṬĀṈVĀŚAL.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Gives a list of names. The record is dated in the 12th regnal
year (1024 A.D.) of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 23.
(A.R. No. 333 of 1952-53.)
CHAKRATTĀḺVĀR SHRINE, PILLARS OF THE VERANDAH.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Commences with the praśasti Tiṅgaḷēr-taru etc. Seems to
record a gift of gold by a Mūvēndavēḷār. Mentions a certain Brahmamā-
rāyar. Some of the pillars on which the inscription seems to have been
engraved in continuation are built into the wall. The record is dated in
the 30th regnal year of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 24.
(A.R. No. 334 of 1952-53.)
CHAKRATTĀḺVĀR SHRINE, PILLARS OF THE VERANDAH.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Contains the praśasti commencing Tiṅgaḷēr-taru. Seems to
record an endowment of 130 kāśu towards the upkeep of some maṭha.

inscription.head:
No. 25.
(A.R. No. 181 of 1951-52.)
NĀḺI-KĒṬṬĀṈVĀŚAL, STRAY STONES BUILT INTO THE WALL OF THE
PASSAGE.
inscription.p:
Built in and fragmentary. Gives the praśasti of Adhirājēndra. Seems to
record a gift of land.

l.ta: cuṭar maṇi makuṭam cūṭi neṭu nila mannavar-

inscription.head:
No. 26.
(A.R. No. 56 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records, in Sanskrit written in grantha characters that Vāṇādhirāja,
minister of Jayadhara i.e., Kulōttuṅga I raised a prākāra in this temple. (?)

l.note: The ends of lines are lost

inscription.head:
No. 27.
(A.R. No. 112 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
Much damaged. Begins with the introduction Pugaḻ śūḻnda puṇari of the
king. Registers gift of land by Arumoḻi Rājādhirājaṉ alias Vāṇādhirājar for
rearing a flower garden.

inscription.head:
No. 28.
(A.R. No. 109 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Seems to record a gift of land in Viḷattūr-nāḍu to Chōḷa-Kēraḷanallūr-
uḍaiyāṉ Ādittaṉ Vēdavanamuḍaiyāṉ alias Nēriyaṉ Mūvēndavēḷār for a flower
garden (tirunandavaṉappuṟam) for supplying two hundred garlands and some
specified quantity of paddy to the temple at the instance of Nishadarājar,
the Śrīkāryam officer of the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 29.
(A.R. No. 110 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Partly built in. Records an assignment by sale of fallow land for pay-
ment of 10 kalam of paddy to the temple by the temple executives towards
the expenses of two festivals to be conducted every year in the temple. The
details of the recipients of the land are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 30.
(A.R. No. 117 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records a sale of dēvadāna land in Kīḻ-Pilāṟu in Pāchchil-kūṟṟam to Kāś-
yapan Kaṇṇan Nārāyaṇaṉ alias Vaiśravaṇa Bhaṭṭar of Tiruveḷḷaṟai, a Brah-
madēya in Vaḍavaḻi-nāḍu in Ulagamuḻuduḍaiyāḷ-nāḍu. Another similar
transaction by the temple executives to Aḻagiyarāja Mūvēndavēḷār is referred
to in lines 9-11 of the inscription. The land given seems to have been named
Gāṅgaiyarsurabhi-viḷāgam.

inscription.head:
No. 31.
(A.R. No. 131 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a sale of land by the temple executives to Poṉṉambalakkūt-
taṉ alias Kāliṅgarāyar.

puviyil ponnemiyaḷavun tannemi naṭappa

III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of land by sale to Śēndamaṅgalamuḍaiyāṉ Jayaṅgoṇḍ-
śōḻaṉ alias Sēnāpati Iḷaṅgōvēḷār for a garden to be named Kiḍāraṅgoṇḍa-
viḷāgam.

(A.R. No. 137 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Records a sale of land to Araiyaṉ Sēndaṉ alias Rājēndra-
chōḻa Adiyamāṉ of Poṉpaṟṟi in Tirumiḻalaik-kūṟṟam for a garden to supply
garlands of specified flowers to the god.

nantavanattukku veṇṭum paṇiceytu kamuku[m] palāvum uḷḷiṭṭa maram
iṭṭākki ceṇpatamum catapattiyum karumukaiyum uḷḷiṭṭa tiruppūmaramum
iṭṭākki nittamum Damaged and lost.tiruppaḷḷittāmam paṇimāṟakkaTavarkaḷAkavum ippaTi ittiru

No. 34.
(A.R. No. 2 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL, NORTH SIDE.
inscription.p:
Registers a sale of land by the temple executives to Āṭkoṇḍavilli of Āda-
ṉūr.

No. 35.
(A.R. No. 6 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.

Damaged. Sale of land to Āritaṉ Tiruvāykkulamuḍaiyāṉ Śī(Śrī)rāgha-
vaṉ by Aḻagiya Maṇavāḷa-Nambi and another.

inscription.head:
No. 36.
(A.R. No. 8 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Records a sale of land to the kaṉmis of Nambirāṭṭiyār Lōkamahādēviyār
free of all taxes for five years from the year of assignment and thereafter for
payment of a stipulated quantity of paddy into the temple treasury. The
sale was made at the instance of Nārāyaṇabhaṭṭar, the Śrīkāryam officer.

inscription.head:
No. 37.
(A.R. No. 9 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a sale of land for garden to Ādityadēva, details lost. A frag-
ment by the side of this record mentions Ādittaṉār alias Kāḍavarāyar and
seems to refer to a sale of land to him.

(A.R. No. 10 of 1948-49.)

inscription.head:
No. 45.
(A.R. No. 167 of 1951-52.)
NĀḺIKĒṬṬĀṈVĀŚAL, WEST AND SOUTH WALLS.
inscription.p:
Registers an agreement (sammātattīṭṭu) by the shepherd Āchchaṉ Gōvad-
dan and five others to supply ghee to the lamp endowed by Sēnāpatigaḷ
Irājanārāyaṇa Muṉaiyadaraiyar alias Koṭṭūruḍaiyāṉ Arayaṉ Rājēndrachōḷaṉ
with the 96 sheep endowed for the purpose by the same donor.

l.ta: ṭṭam vayirākarattu vāri ayir munai ko-
t toḷ vali kāṭṭip poṟpaṟi naṭāttik
nātu tiruniḻal veṇṇilāt tikaḻa orutani meru-

inscription.head:
No. 46.
(A.R. No. 168 of 1951-52.)
NĀḺIKĒṬṬĀṈVĀŚAL, TIERS.
inscription.p:
Partly covered by brass plates. Seems to refer to an enquiry into and
the settlement of arrears by Rājēndrachōḻa Mūvēndavēḷār, the arrears amounting
to 940 kāśu by some individuals to the temple treasuries. One of the individuals
(name lost) involved in the case seems to have served a period of imprison-
ment. A fragment apparently not connected with this record but on the same
tiers refers to the provisions made for observing the Pūśattirunāḷ of Kulōttuṅga
chōḻadēva every month.

l.note: Considerable portions of the inscribed tiers are covered up by brass plates.

inscription.head:
No. 47.
(A.R. No. 169 of 1951-52.)
NĀḺIKĒṬṬĀṈVĀŚAL, WEST AND SOUTH TIERS.
inscription.p:
Records gift of sheep by a number of individuals for supply of ghee for a
perpetual lamp in the temple. Mentions Vīra Vichchādira Mūvēndavēḷār as the
Śrīkāryam officer of the temple at whose instance the gift seems to have been
made.

inscription.head:
No. 48.
(A.R. No. 187 of 1951-52.)
NĀḺIKĒṬṬĀṈVĀŚAL, TIERS.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Seems to record a gift of 10,000 kalam of paddy for ser-
vices and offerings to the god.

No. 49.
(A.R. No. 12 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Records a sale of land to Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷap-Perumāḷ alias Irāva •••
Chōḻappallavaraiyaṉ under the orders of Śīriḷaṅgō Bhaṭṭar, the Śrīkāryam
officer. Details lost.

Fragmentary. Mentions the names Śilaiyaṉ-Kundavai and Chāmuṇḍaṉ
Gōvarddhaṉa-maṇavā[ḷaṉ].

inscription.head:
No. 51.
(A.R. No. 41 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers sale of land, details lost. The record is dated in the 10th regnal
year of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 52.
(A.R. No. 18 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers the repayment of a loan of 400 kaḻañju by sale of 6 vēli of tax-free
land by the sabhā of Chandralēkhai-chaturvēdimaṅgalam which had raised this
amount from God Ānantanārāyaṇasvāmi at Śrīraṅgam in the 10th year of
Madurai koṇḍa kōp-Parakēsari (Parāntaka I), having paid the interest thereon
regularly. The record is dated in the 10th year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 53.
(A.R. No. 31 of 1936-37).
IV PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
The record is stated to belong to Rājamahēndra-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.
States that as the temple of Mummuḍichōḻa-viṇṇagarāḻvār at Rājamahēndra-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Kāndāra (Gāndhāra)-nāḍu, a sub-
division of Nittavinōda-vaḷanāḍu, was unable to safeguard its property owing
to a burglary of its treasury during a conflict between the Right and Left hand
classes in the 2nd year of the king's reign the assembly of Rājamahēndra-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam received a lump-sum from the temple and exempted
certain lands belonging to it from payment of taxes. The record belongs to
the 11th year of the king's reign and is dated Karkaṭaka, ba. 15, Sunday,
Āyilyam. The details regularly correspond to A.D., 1080, July 19, Sunday.

inscription.head:
No. 54.
(A.R. No. 118 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers gift of land in Kaṟkuḍi by Sēnāpati Vīrarājēndra Adigaimāṉ
for a flower garden to the temple. The record is dated in the 13th regnal year
of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 55.
(A.R. No. 3 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Records assignment of two plots of land free of specified taxes by the temple
executives to Sēnāpatigaḷ Taḷiyil Madurāntakaṉ Rājēndrachōḻak-Kiḍāra
tarayar and his wife Rājakēsarivalli for raising two flower gardens in their names.
Mentions among the boundaries the Rājēndrachōḻa-vāṇādhirāja maṇṇilai.
The record is dated in the 13th regnal year of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 56.
(A.R. No. 132 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Seems to be a gift of gold by Sēnāpatigaḷ Rājēndrachōḻa Muṉaiyadaraiyar
towards garlands, ghee for lamp, etc. The wording in line 5 indicates the
regnal year of the king as either 14 or 16.

inscription.head:
No. 57.
(A.R. No. 61 of 1892.)
III PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers an agreement (kaittīṭṭu) by the temple executives (nivandakkārar)
made to Sēnāpatigaḷ Vīrachōḻa Muṉaiyadaraiyar alias Āyarkoḻundu Chakrapāṇi
of Koṭṭūr for 50 kaḻañju of gold paid towards purchase of lands to provide for the
recitation of the Tiruppaḷḷieḻuchchi and Tiruvāymoḻi, in the temple. Dated in
the 15th regnal year of the king.
[Published in S.I.I., Vol. IV, No. 508]

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷa[ṅ]ka jayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ vaḷara [ma]larmakaḷ
puṇara urimaiyiṟ ciṟa

inscription.head:
No. 58.
(A.R. No. 15 of 1936-37.)
III PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of land by purchase from the temple authorities by Rāja-
rājaṉ Madhurāntakaṉ alias Vatsarāja for worship and offerings to God Aḻagiya
maṇavāḷap-perumāḷ on the day of his natal star Mṛigaśīrsha and for feeding
Śrīvaishṇavas in the Madhurāntakadēvaṉ-maṭha during certain specified
festivals. The record is dated in the 15th year of the king.

l.ta:
puviyil ponnemiyaḷavun tan
nemi naṭappa viḷaṅku jeyamakaḷai iḷaṅkopparuvattu cakkarakoṭṭattu vikkiramat

ḷavaraicar tantaḷamiriya vāḷuṟai kaḻittut toḷvalikāṭṭip porp

inscription.head:
No. 59.
(A.R. No. 114 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records sale of land to Ādināthaṉ alias Kārāṇai Viḻupparaiyar (vide
No. 112 of 1947-48). Dated in the 15th year of the king's reign.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa nila[makaḷ nilava] ma[lai]makaḷ puṇara urimaiyiṟ

inscription.head:
No. 60.
(A.R. No. 120 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records an undertaking (kaittīṭṭu) by the nivandakkārar to the temple
executives for having purchased certain lands from Śrīraṅganātha-Kramavit-
taṉ and his brothers, of Rājakēsarichchēri, the 8th ward (chēri) in Rājāśraya-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Uṟaiyūrkūṟṟam and provision (nivandam) for reciting
Tiruppaḷḷieḻuchchi and Tiruvāymoḻi before god Anantanārāyaṇasvāmi, at
the instance of Āyarkoḻundu Chakrapāṇi alias Vīrachōḻa Muṉaiyadarayar of
Koṭṭūr in ••••• nāḍu in Arumoḻidēva-vaḷanāḍu. This is evidently con-
nected with No. 57 above where the gift of 50 kaḻañju of poṉ by the donor for
the purpose is recorded. The record is dated in the 15th year of the king's reign
and the details of the date viz., Kumbha śu. 4, Wednesday, Rēvati, correspond
to 1085, A.D., February 1, Saturday (not Wednesday) ·12; ·27.

inscription.head:
No. 61.
(A.R. No. 124 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a sale of land in Viḷattūr-nāḍu under orders of Nishadarājar,
the Śrīkāryam of the temple to Ulaguyyakkoṇḍaruḷiṉa •••••
ya Śembiyār alias Nēriyaṉ-Mādēvi, daughter of Pāṇḍiyaṉār for a garden
to be raised in her name and for payment of specified amount of paddy to the
temple from out of the produce. Dated in the 15th regnal year of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 62.
(A.R. No. 125 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of money for bringing land in Viḷānāḍu in Pāṇḍikulāśani-vaḷa
nāḍu under cultivation by Teṉṉavan-Mādēviyār, the Queen of Rājēndradēva,
(Kulōttuṅga I) towards provision for offerings to the god on the 5th day of Māśi.
Mentions Nēriyaṉ Mādēvi-viḷāgam as one of the boundaries. Dated in the
15th year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 63.
(A.R. No. 62 of 1892.)
III PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of 6(1/4) kāśu by Araiyaṉ Garuḍavāhaṉaṉ alias Kāliṅgarāyan
for offerings during specified festivals on the occasion of the recitation of
Tēṭṭaruntiṟal, a hymn composed by Kulaśēkhara-Āḻvār. The record is dated in
the [1]8th regnal year of the king.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. III, No. 70.

inscription.head:
No. 64.
(A.R. No. 113 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Seems to record a sale of tax-free land in Viḷā-nāḍu in Kēraḷāntaka-vaḷa-
nāḍu to Tuṟumūr-uḍaiyān Araiyaṉ ••••• alias Uḍaiyār Kārāṇai
Viḻupparaiyar stipulating payment of a specified quantity of paddy from the 5th
year of cultivation of the land. The record is dated in the 19th regnal year
of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 65.
(A.R. No. 118 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a sale of tax-free dēvadāna land in Viḷattūr-nāḍu in Kēraḷāntaka-
vaḷanāḍu to Poṉpaṟṟi-uḍaiyāṉ Araiyaṉ Sēñaṉ alias Rājēndrachōḻa Adiyamān
of Tirumiḻalaikkūṟṟam in Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu in Rājarājap-Pāṇḍināḍu by
the executives of the temple on the orders of Vīra Vichchādira-Mūvēndavēḷār
the Śrīkāryam officer of the temple, stipulating that the endowed land be named
Tirumallināḍi-viḷāgam and a specified quantity of paddy measured to the
temple. Dated in the 20th year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 66.
(A.R. No. 130 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers the gift of a chauri (fly whisk) called Āyiravaṉ with a golden
handle, to the god named as Ananta-Nārāyaṇasvāmi at Śrīraṅgam through
the assembly of the village, by Malaiyāḷar of both Perundanam and Śiṟudanam.
Mentions also the name of the person Śrīdānappiḷḷai-maṅgai-āḻvāṉ of the
southern section (kaṇḍa) in the 16th chēri (ward) of Rājāśraya-charuppēdi
maṅgalam appointed to serve the lord with the chauri. The record is dated
in the 21st regnal year of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 67.
(A.R. No. 121 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a sale of tax-free land to Uttamachōḻanallūr-uḍaiyāṉ alias
Nārāyaṇaṉ Malaikkiṉiya-niṉṟāṉ alias Mummuḍichōḻa Viḻupparaiyaṉ and
Padmanābhaṉ Vishṇumūrti, the servant (paṉimagaṉ) of the former under the
orders from Chōḻaśikhāmaṇi Mūvēndavēḷār, the Śrīkāryam of the temple
stipulating the payment of a specified quantity of paddy to the temple.
Dated in the year opposite the 21st i.e. the 22nd year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 68.
(A.R. No. 130 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Records a sale of land in Viḷattūr-nāḍu in Kēraḷāntaka-vaḷanāḍu
to Irumbuḷaiyuḍaiyāṉ Vēḷāṉ Tillaiyāṉ alias Maḻavarā ••••• under
orders of ••• ••• •••• Īśvara-kulakāla-Brahmārāyar for the purchase
of garden (tirunandavanappuṟam) to be named Uttamālayaṉ •••••
Dated in the 22nd year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 69.
(A.R. No. 122 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of land by Arigaṇḍadēvaṉ Āyarkoḻundinār alias Sēnāpatigaḷ
Gaṅgaikoṇḍaśōḻa Muṉaiyadaraiyar of Koṭṭūr in Arumoḻidēva-vaḷanāḍu for
a flower garden named after him and for offering on two festival days when the
god was taken to the Arigaṇḍadēvaṉ tirumaṇḍapam. The record is dated in
the 24th regnal year of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 70.
(A.R. No. 123 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers the provision made by the person mentioned in No. 69 above
for a lamp in the temple. Dated in the 24th regnal year of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 71.
(A.R. No. 132 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Partly built in States that as the original tenants of the dēvadāna village
(name lost) in Mīpilāṟu in Pāchchil-kūṟṟam, a sub-division of Kulōttuṅga-
chōḻa-vaḷanāḍu were unable to pay the taxes, this village was renamed
Rājanārāyaṇanallūr and redistributed among new tenants. Dated in the
24th regnal year of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 72.
(A.R. No. 112 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records a sale of land in Viḷattūr-nāḍu in Kēraḷāntaka-vaḷanāḍu to Guṇa
valli alias Peṇḍāṭṭi Kaḍavūruḍaiyāḷ, a resident of Tirukkaḍavūr in Ākūr-
nāḍu in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, towards charity, under orders of Rājēndra-
Mūvēndavēḷār. The inscription was ordered to be engraved on the wall of
the Rājamahēndraṉ-tiruchchuṟṟu. Mentions Vīra Vichchādhara Mū[vēnda ?]
vēḷār, the headman (kiḻavaṉ) of Pālaiyūr. The record is dated in the 25th
regnal year of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 73.
(A.R. No. 126 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Records an endowment of land by Rājarājaṉ Arumoḻiyār alias Teṉṉavaṉ
Mādēvi, queen of Rājēndradēva (Kulōttuṅga I) towards feeding (bhikshā) of
bhahavar (bhagavar). Also refers to a land endowment by Rājamāṇikkam, the
peṇḍāṭṭi of Kāḍuveṭṭigaḷ to provide rice for spreading under the Tiruppāñ-
jāḍi (?) on certain specified occasions. The inscription is dated in the 25th year
of the king's reign.

l.ta:
tevar nampi
rāṭṭiyār rājarājan arumoḻiyārāna tennavan māteviyār

tām munpu tiruttuvitta nilattukku vaṭak[kiṭai] nilam katili āḻvārkku iṟai
iṟutti iṟai mikuti koṇṭu

kaṭavārākavum iṅke kāṭu veṭṭikaḷ peṇṭāṭṭi rāja māṇikkam iraṇṭu tiru
nāḷilum eḻā nāḷaikku tiruppāñcāṭik kīḻp parappum
tai aricikku tiruttivitta nilam kālmā ••• ••• •••• iṟai mikuti

inscription.head:
No. 74.
(A.R. No. 182 of 1951-52.)
NĀḺIKĒṬṬAṈVĀŚAL, NICHE IN THE INNER WALL.
inscription.p:
Highly damaged and incomplete after the preamble. The record is dated
in the 27th (26th+1st) year of the king's reign.

edition.l: ḷiya kovirāja[ kesaritri]

inscription.p:
The inscription is in Kannaḍa. It is damaged and worn out in places
and records a provision made for burning two lamps in the temple of god Śrī-
raṅga by the Kannaḍasandhivigrahi and Daṇḍanāyaka (name lost) of king
Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI) in the 29th year (A.D. 1099-1100)
of the reign of Kulōttuṅgadēva (Kulōttuṅga I). Gives the names of seven Kōns
such as Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷa-kōn, Karuṇākara-kōn, Śrī-Narasiṅga-kōn, Tirumā-
liru[ñ]jōlai-kōn, etc., who were enjoined to supply the required quantity of
ghee for burning the lamps. The record seems also to refer to the plastering,
with lime mortar, caused to be done to the shrine of Sēnāpati (Vishvaksēna)
probably by the same donor.
Published in Epigraphia Indica, Vol. XXXVI, pp. 203 ff. and plate.

edition.l: • • • • • • • • • • [ya] • kōn [i] [·aḷa]geyamanavāḷakōn | tiru

inscription.head:
No. 76.
(A.R. No. 13 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Registers a sale of land to Vaḷavaṉ Mādēviyār by Śī(Śrī)
rāman Kēśavaṉ. The record is dated in the 29th year of the king. One of
the fragments refers to Sri-Magadai-kōṉ as a signatory.

pukaḻ mātu viḷaṅka puvimātuvirumpa nilamakaḷ vaḷara malarmakaḷ puṇara

inscription.head:
No. 77.
(A.R. No. 121 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers gift of land after purchase by Ādittaṉ-Tiruvaraṅgadēvaṉ alias
Virudarāja-bhayaṅkara Vijayapālaṉ for rearing a flower garden. The record
is dated in the 31st year of the king's reign.

No. 78.
(A.R. No. 36 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Registers a sale of land free of taxes to Tiruvaraṅgamuḍai-
yāṉ, an accountant of the sabhā of the temple. The details are lost. The
record is dated in the 32nd year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 79.
(A.R. No. 210 (A) of 1951-52.)
II PRĀKĀRA, INNER WALL OF THE MĒṬṬUNĀCHCHIYĀR SHRINE.
inscription.p:
Records an endowment to the sabhā for purchase of land for offerings to
god by Vaṇḍāḻañjēri Uḍaiyāṉ Veḷḷāḷaṉ Anai alias Vāṇakōvaraiyaṉ of Tiru-
naṟaiyūr (see No. 94 below), dated in the 32nd regnal year of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 80.
(A.R. No. 82 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA ON THE STEPS OF THE
CHANDRAPUSHKARAṆĪ.
inscription.p:
Records a gift of a lamp weighing 100 palams by a person named Śāttan-
dai Aiyyāṟaṉ Śaṅkaranārāyaṇaṉ. The inscription is dated in the 33rd year
of the king's reign.

edition.l: ṟku yāṇṭu 33- āva-

inscription.head:
No. 81.
(A.R. No. 129 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of land by the peruṅguṟi-sabhā of the Rājāśraya-chaturvē
dimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Uṟaiyūr-kūṟṟam, a sub-division of Rājamahēndra-
vaḷanāḍu, for a flower garden and repairs to the temple of Tiruvaraṅgadēva.
The sabhā is said to have been convened in the Rājēndraśōḻan maṇḍapa in
their village and the number of members present counted before beginning
its deliberations. Dated in the 34th regnal year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 82.
(A.R. No. 4 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Records a gift of land, details lost. The inscription is dated in the 34th
regnal year of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 83.
(A.R. No. 124 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of land by purchase, by Kiḷiyūruḍaiyār Nāḍaṟipugaḻaṉ
alias Kāliṅgarāyar for a flower garden. Since this piece of land was found
insufficient, another piece of land purchased by him in the 33rd year was also
added to this. The adhikāri at the time was Nishadharāja. The record is
dated in the [3]8th regnal year of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 84.
(A.R. No. 14 of 1936-37.)
III PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
The record is partly covered up by the floor. It seems to register a gift
of land to Āriyaṉ Vāsudēva Bhaṭṭaṉ alias Rājarāja Brahmārāyaṉ of Anishṭhā-
nam in Kāśmīradēśam, by the several executive bodies of the temple, in the
39th regnal year of the king. The Śrīkāryam of the temple was Neḍuñjērik-
kuḍaiyāṉ alias Bhuvaninārāyaṇa Mūvēndavēḷār.

inscription.head:
No. 85.
(A.R. No. 131 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH ENTRANCE INTO THE YĀGAŚĀLAI-MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of land by purchase by Kaṇṇagan Karumāṇikkam alias
Vaḷava-Vichchādira Pallavaraiyan for rearing a flower garden after his name,
to the god Tiruvaraṅgattāḻvār in Viḷānāḍu, a sub-division of Dīnachintāmaṇi-
vaḷanāḍu. The Śrīkaryam of the temple was adhikārigaḷ Bhuvaninārāyaṇa
Mūvēndavēḷār. The record is dated in the 39th regnal year of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 86.
(A.R. No. 127 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Begins with the introduction Pugaḻ śūḻnda puṇari etc. Registers a gift
of land after purchase by Paḷḷikoṇḍāṉ Kūttaṉār alias Viḻiñattaraiyar of
Śiṟṟāmūr in Tiruvaḻundūr-nāḍu, a sub-division of Rājanārāyaṇa-vaḷanāḍu
for supplying flowers to the deity on different occasions. In continuation
of this is engraved another record of the same king beginning with Pugaḻ-
mādu-viḷaṅga, a greater portion of which is however built in. The record
is dated in the 40th year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 87.
(A.R. No. 119 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records sale of tax-free (tirumaḍaippuṟam) land in Viḷānāḍu in Kēraḷān-
taka-vaḷanāḍu to Śīrāḷaṉ Tiruchiṟṟambalamuḍaiyāṉ alias Adhikārigaḷ
Vīra-Vichchādira Mūvēndavēḷār, for supply of paddy to the Śrībhaṇḍāram
and also for offerings to the god on the occasion of reciting the Tiruvāymoḻi.
The record is dated in the 40th regnal year of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 88.
(A.R. No. 138 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Records a gift of land under orders of Bhuvaninārāyaṇa Mūvēndavēḷāṉ
of Neḍuñjēri to Karumāṇikkam Ādittadēvaṉ alias Chēdirāyar for raising
a garden. The inscription is dated in the 40th year of the king's reign.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] pukaḻ cūḻnta puṇari akaḻ cūḻnta puviyil ponnemiyaḷavun tannemi
naṭappa viḷaṅku jayamakaḷai [iḷaṅkopparuvattuc cakkarak]oṭṭattu vikkiramat t[oḻilāl
putumaṇam puṇarntu matuva]raiyiṭṭam vayirākarattu [vāri] ayar muṉaik konta

villetir koṭā vikkalan kallata[r naṅkili tuṭaṅki maṇalūr naṭu]vena tuṅkapattirai-

inscription.head:
No. 89.
(A.R. No. 17 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Seems to record a sale of land to Tiruvaraṅgappichchar and Tirumōhūrp-
pichchar. The inscription is dated in the 40th year of king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 90.
(A.R. No. 26 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Seems to record a grant of tax-free land by purchase by the king who
bears the title Śuṅgantavirtta on the day of Pūśam, the day of his accession.
The record is dated in the 40th year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 91.
(A.R. No. 125 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of lands, by purchase for 200 drammas by Ādittaṉ Tiru-
varaṅgadēvaṉ alias Virudarājabhayaṅkara Vijaiyapālaṉ towards offerings
and a jewel Śrīpādasālam (Śrīpādasaram) to the god on specified occasions.
One of the lands, which was once endowed for the jewel mentioned above by
Vaḷavaṉ Viḻupparaiyaṉ was now to be reclaimed and its produce to be utilised
for the same purpose. The record is dated in the 41st year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 92.
(A.R. No. 106 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers the sale of fallow land belonging to the temple (tirumaḍap-
paḷḷipuṟam) in Viḷattūr-nāḍu in Rājamahēndra-vaḷanāḍu by the temple execu-
tives (Śrīvaishṇava-vāriyam) to •••• ṅguḍaiyāṉ Mādhavaṉ-Tiruvaraṅgattēvaṉ
alias Gaṅgaikoṇḍa Śōḻavijayavēḷār with the stipulation that 10 kalam of
pady be made over to the temple. The sale was made under the orders of
Pārthivēndra-brahmādirāja, the Śrīkāryam officer. Dated in the 41st year of
the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 93.
(A.R. No. 108 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers the sale 4 vēli of fallow land made tax-free and belonging to the
temple in Viḷattūrnāḍu in Rājamahēndra-vaḷanāḍu by the Śrīvaishṇavavāriyam
to Kaḍuvaṅguḍaiyāṉ Ādittaṉ Tiruvaraṅgadēvaṉ alias Virudarāja-bhayaṅkara
Vijayapālaṉ stipulating payment of 32 kalam of paddy at the rate of 8 kalam
per vēli to the temple for 220 diramam which was utilised towards a jewel to the
god in the name of Paṉaiyūruḍaiyāṉ Vaḷavaṉ Viḻupparaiyaṉ. The transaction
was made on Tuesday, the 352nd day in the month of Āṉi in the 41st year of
the king's reign when the nakshatra was Rōhiṇī. The details of date probably
correspond to A.D. 1111, June 6, Tuesday; ·63. (Same as No. 91 above).

inscription.head:
No. 94.
(A.R. No. 115 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. The details after the praśasti are completely lost. The
inscription is dated in the 41st year of the king's reign.

No. 95.
(A.R. No. 129 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Records sale of land in Rājamahēndra-vaḷanāḍu to Mādhavaṉ Tiruvaraṅ-
gadēvaṉ alias Adaḷaiyūrnāḍāḻvāṉ of Kaḍuvaṅguḍi by the temple executives
for supply of specified quantity of paddy to the temple. The record is
dated in the 41st year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 96.
(A.R. No. 5 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Records sale of land to Ādityaṉ of Tiruviñjalūr-nāḍu, under
orders of Pārthivēndra Brahmādhirāja. The record is dated in the 42nd year
of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 97.
(A.R. No. 7 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Damaged and fragmentary. Seems to refer to a sale of land. The ins-
cription is dated in the 42nd year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 98.
(A.R. No. 210 of 1951-52.)
II PRĀKĀRA, INNER WALL OF THE NĀCHCHIYĀR SHRINE.
inscription.p:
Seems to record the remission of certain taxes on some lands in lieu of a
lump sum of money received by the Peruṅguṟi sabhā of Aṟindigaichaturvēdi-
maṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Uṟaiyūrkūṟṟam in Rājamahēndra-vaḷanāḍu
from Vaṇḍāḻañchēri-uḍaiyān Veḷḷāḷan Āṉai ••• alias Vāṇa-
kōvaraiyaṉ of Tirunaṟaiyūr for offerings and baths on Ēkādaśi days to the
god. The sabhā is stated to have met at the temple of Tirukkuṟuṅguḍiyāḻvāṉ.
The record is dated in the 42nd regnal year of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 99.
(A.R. No. 126 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of land by purchase, by the Śrīkāryam officer, Talaich-
chaṅgāṭṭu Śīriḷaṅgō-bhaṭṭar, for a flower garden to the temple. His full name
is given as Talaichchaṅgāṭṭu Āttiraiyaṉ Picchaṉ Śīriḷaṅgō-bhaṭṭan and he is
stated to be a resident of Rājarāja-chēri in Rājanārāyaṇa-chaturvēdi
maṅgalam, a brahmadēya of Kuṟukkai-nāḍu, a sub-division of Virudarāja-
bhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu. The record is dated in the 43rd year of the king's
reign.

inscription.head:
No. 100.
(A.R. No. 123 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records a sale of land to Tirumaḻavāḍi-uḍaiyāṉ alias Rājavallabha-Palla-
varaiyaṉ under orders from Adhikārigaḷ Nittaviṉōdamuḍai ••••• for
payment of a specified quantity of paddy to the temple. The inscription is
dated in the 44th regnal year of the king.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] pukaḻ mātu viḷa[ṅ*]ka jayamā[tu*] virumpa nilamakaḷ nilava malaimakaḷ

inscription.head:
No. 101.
(A.R. No. 22 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records the order (niyōgam) of the Peruṅguṟi-mahā-sabhā of the village
••••• maṅgalam to re-impose the iṟaiyili tenure in lieu of a sum of
three kaḻañju and •• mañjāḍi of gold on certain cultivable lands which were
under iṟaikāval from the 2nd to the 40th year of the king's reign. The record
is dated in the 44th year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 102.
(A.R. No. 32 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records a sale of tirumaḍaippaḷḷippuṟam land in Viḷattūr-nāḍu to Ara-
yaṉ Tirumaḻapāḍi-uḍaiyāṉ alias Rājavallavap-Pallavaraiyaṉ of Koṟṟamaṅga-
lam in Eyil-nāḍu for a flower garden. The record is dated in the 44th
regnal year of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 103.
(A.R. No. 111 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Begins with the praśasti Pugaḻ mādu. Records an assignment of land
by sale by the temple executives to Tirunīlakaṇṭaṉ Kaḷappāḷaṉ Aḻagiya ••
Mūvēndavēḷār of Ūṟṟukkāṭṭuk-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam for pay-
ment of specified quantity of paddy to the temple. Refers to the garden
land of Poṉpaṟṟi Uḍaiyāṉ alias Rājēndrachōḻa Adigaimāṉār. The record is
dated in the 45th regnal year of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 104.
(A.R. No. 15 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers an endowment of land by sale by Śiṟiyāṇḍāḷśāni, the daughter
of Ātrēyaṉ Dāmōdaraṉ Nārāyaṇaṉ and the wife of Tāya Nambi Pirāṉ, to the
Śrīvaishṇavas in-charge of the flower gardens. The record is dated in the
45th year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 105.
(A.R. No. 20 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records a sale of land, to the Śrīvaishṇavas by Śēndaṉ Śivaṉ and
another. The inscription is dated in the 45th year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 106.
(A.R. No. 21 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records sale of land by Āttirayaṉ Śēndaṉ Śivaṉ to the Śrīvaishṇavas of
the temple (vide No. 105 above). Engraved in continuation of No. 105. The
record is dated in the 45th year of the king's reign.

III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.

No. 108.
(A.R. No. 116 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a sale of land to Śī (Śrī)[vīra] Kāladāsan of Pallapuram. Men-
tions the officer Rājēndrasiṅga Pallavaraiyan. The inscription is dated in the
48th year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 109.
(A.R. No. 27 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Commences with the praśasti Pūmālai miḍaindu etc. of the
king. All other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 110.
(A.R. No. 37 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p: Records a gift of 100 sheep for supply of ghee to the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 111.
(A.R. No. 127 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers an assignment of tax-free land to Rāmadēvaṉ Śrīrāma Bhaṭṭaṉ,
the tārkkāṇi of Iḷavēḷūr for providing offerings on specified occasions; the land
endowed was to be called Śrīrāmaviḷāgam. The record is dated in the 3rd year
of the king's reign.

l.ta:
pūmālai miṭaintu pon mālai tikaḻtarap pāmālai malinta parumaṇit
tiraḷ puyat tirunilamaṭantaiyoṭu jeyamakaḷiruppat tanvarai mārvan tanatenap-
peṟṟu tirumakaḷorutani iruppak kalaimakaḷ coṟṟiṟam-

puṇarnta kaṟpiṉaḷāki viruppoṭu nāvakattiruppa ticaitorun tikiṟiyoṭu ceṅkol
naṭappa akilapuvaṉamuḻutuṅ kavippator putumatipol veṇkuṭai mimicai niḻaṟṟak
karuṅkali oḷittu van pilattiṭaik kiṭappak kuḷattiṭai-

t telu[ṅ*]kaviman vilaṅkal mimicai eṟavuṅ kaliṅka pūmiyaik kaṉaleri parukavum

inscription.head:
No. 112.
(A.R. No. 128 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Records another assignment of tax-free land by sale to Tirumaḻapāḍi
Durga •••• Bhaṭṭan alias Śrī Śaḍagōpanambi Aḻagiya-maṇavāḷap-
perumāḷ, a tārkkāṇi of Iḷavēḷūr, in Kavijanakaṟpagachchēri in Gaṇḍarāditta-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Poygai-nāḍu in Tyāgavalli-vaḷanāḍu,
for supply of garlands to the temple. The endowed land was to be named
Śaḍagōpaviḷāgam, after the assignee. Mentions Śrīkāridēvaviḷāgam endowed
by Nīlagaṅgaraiyar, probably as one of the boundaries of the gift land. The
record is dated in the 3rd year of the king's reign.

l.ta:
pūmālai miṭantu pon mālai tikaḻtara pāmālai malinta [paṉmaṇit
tiraḷpuyat tirunilamaṭantaiyoṭu] jayamakaḷḷiruppa tan[varai]mārpan tanateṉap
peṟṟut tirumakaḷ ḷorutani iruppa-

kaṟpinaḷāki viruppoṭu nāvakat tiruppa ticaitoṟum
tikiriyoṭu ceṅkol naṭappa akilapuvanamuḻutuṅ kavippator putumatipol veṇ
kuṭai mimicai[niḻa]-

ṟṟa karuṅkali oḷittu vanpilattiṭaik kiṭappa kuḷattiṭait teluṅkavīman vilaṅka
mimicai yeṟavum kaliṅka pūmiyaik kanaleri parukavum aympaṭaip paruvattu vem
paṭai tā[ṅ]-

ṅkini tiruntum vaṭaticai yaṭippapaṭuttaruḷit tenticait
tarumamum tavamum tānamun taḻaippa vetamum nitiyum meymeyum ātiyukam
pola talai ciṟappa vanta[ru]-

ḷi velaṅkaḷum poṟpuliyāṇai pārttivar cūṭa niṟai maṇimakuṭam muṟaimaiyiṟ
cūṭa mannuyiṟ kellām innuyiṟ tāy pol tannoḷi parappi tanittani [parattu
maṇmuḻutu kaḷippa] manuneṟivaḷa-

rttu tan koyiṟ koṟṟavācar pu[ṟa*]ttu maṇināvoṭuṅka muṟacukaḷ mu[ḻa*]ṅka
vicaiyamum pukaḻum mel mel oṅka ceḻiyar veñcuram puka ceralar kaṭal puka
aḻitaku [ci]ṅkaṇarañci ne[ñ*]cala[ma]rak kaṅkar tiṟaiyiṭak kannaṭar venniṭak
koṅkarotu-

ṅka koṅkaṇar cāya maṟteṟaticai mannarum tan tamakkaraṇena tirumalaṟ cevaṭi
urimaiyiliṟaiñci tollai eḻulakun toḻuteḻat tonṟiya mullai vāṇakaik
koṭi mukko(ḷa)kkiḻānaṭi umaiyoṭu caṅkaran imaiyac cimai-

yattiruntanaṉena porutti uṭaniruppa āṅkavan makuḻuṅ kaṅkaiyoppākiya tari
vayar tilatam tiyākapatākai purikuḻal maṭantai punita kuṇavanitai tiripuvanamuṭai
yāḷ [a]vaṉ tiruvuḷḷattaruḷ muḻutuṭaiyāḷena irup-

kopparakecaripanmarāna tiripuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī vikkiramacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu

yar evaṟpaṭi āḻvār[kanmikaḷil] śrīvaiyiṣṇavāriyam kuṇṭu[r*] paḷḷikoṇṭā
nampiyum perumpaṟṟappuliyūr ci caṭakopatā

nampiyum tiruvāyppāṭi nampiyum śrīpaṇṭāravāriyam āritan ārāvamutu puṇṭa
rikanum pāratāyan cirāman ārāva[mu*]tum āritan cirākavanum cikāri-

inscription.head:
No. 113.
(A.R. No. 38 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records a sale of land at the instance of Vaḷavanārāyaṇa Mūvēndavēḷār,
the Śrīkāryam officer of the temple to Puravaṅguḍaiyāṉ Araiyaṉ Ādittadēvaṉ
alias Ēnādi Araiyaṉ of Puḷiyaṅguḍi in Kiḷiyūr-nāḍu for a flower garden to be
named Nidiyābharaṇaṉ-tirunandavanam. The record is dated in the 8th year
of the king's reign and the details of the date viz., Makara 25, ba. 8, Monday,
Viśākham regularly correspond to A.D. 1126, January 18, Monday, ·30; ·46.

inscription.head:
No. 114.
(A.R. No. 39 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records the sale of 3(1/2) mā of land to Ēnādi Araiyaṉ (vide No. 106 above)
for 8 kāśu, by Śāndūr Magiḻalaṅkāradāsaṉ and his younger brother who obtained
this from Periyakōyilpriyar in exchange for the garden land called Kōdai Āṇḍāḷ
tirunandavanam. The record is dated in the 8th year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 115.
(A.R. No. 40 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records the sale deed of the transaction referred to in No. 114 above.
The inscription is dated in the 8th year of the king's reign.

l.note: This is written in continuation of No. 114.

l.ta:

inscription.head:
No. 116.
(A.R. No. 28 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a sale of land (tirumaḍaippaḷḷipuṟam) to a certain Brahmamārājar.
The record is dated Kaṭaka, Śu. 3, Wednesday, Maghā in the 10th year of the
king's reign, corresponding to A.D. 1127, July 13, Wednesday, f.d.t. ·20; ·51.

l.ta:
vatu kaṭakanāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu tiritikai
yum putankiḻamaiyum peṟṟa makattu nāḷ tiruvaraṅkattāḻvār koyil ••• ••• ••••

inscription.head:
No. 117.
(A.R. No. 437 of 1954-55.)
II PRĀKĀRA, INNER WALL OF THE SANDAL-PASTE ROOM.
inscription.p:
Commences with the praśasti Pūmādu puṇara, etc. Registers a gift of 3 mā
of land bounded by the Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻaṉ-tirunandavanam on the west,
Rājādhirājaṉ-tirunandavanam on the north, Āḷappiṟandān-tirunandavanam
on the east and Kāvēri on the south by Tillaiyaḻagiyār the mother of queen
Araiyaṉulaguḍaiyār alias Olōga (Lōka)mahādēviyār to Kandāḍai Tiruva
raṅga-nārayāṇaṉ Śrī Śaḍagōpaṉ for a flower garden in her name and for her
own welfare, at the instance of Kalivalam-uḍaiyāṉ Tiruvāyikkuḷam-uḍaiyāṉ
alias Vaḷavanārāyaṇa-mūvēndavēḷāṉ, the Śrīkāryam of the temple. The
record is dated in the 10th year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 118.
(A.R. No. 438 of 1954-55.)
II PRĀKĀRA, INNER WALL OF THE SILVER PALANQUIN ROOM.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Commences with the praśasti Pūmālai miḍaindu etc. Records
the sale of one vēli of land by the Śrīvaishṇavas of the temple to Nambi Nilaiyaṉ
Tiruchchiṟṟambalamuḍaiyāṉ alias Muḍikoṇḍachōḻa-mūvēndavēḷār of Poṟkala-
kkuḍi in Marugal-nāḍu in Gēyamāṇikka-vaḷanāḍu, who probably donated the
same to the temple at the instance of Rājavichchādira Brahmamārāyar, the
Śrīkāryam, of the temple, for a flower garden.

The record is dated in the 12th year of the king's reign, Siṁha, ba. 8,
Thursday, Kārttikai (Kṛittikā), which corresponds to 1129 A.D., August, 8,
f.d.t. ·33. f.d.n. ·69.

inscription.head:
No. 119.
(A.R. No. 340 of 1952-53.)
III PRĀKĀRA, PAVITRA-MAṆḌAPA, INNER WALL, RIGHT OF THE ĀRYABHAṬṬĀḶ-
VĀŚAL.
inscription.p:
Commences with the same praśasti as No. 114 above. Registers a sale of
one vēli of 2,000 kuḻi of 28-2-340 kōl of sand-silted fallow land in Teṉkarai-
Vaḷattūr-nāḍu for 2 kāśu for a stipulated quantity of paddy to be given into
the temple granary by the temple officials at the instance of Rāja Vijjādira
Birumma-mārāyar (Vidyādhara Brahma-mahārāyar), the Śrīkāryam of the
temple, to Vijayarāyar alias Tiruvēṅgaḍavaṉ-Dēvaṉ of Paṉṟimaṅgalam in
Nallūr-nāḍu in Nittavinōda-vaḷanāḍu, for being reclaimed and made into a
flower garden under the name Kēśavanviḷāgam by Perumbuliyūr Svāmibhaktan.
The record is dated in the 13th year of reign of the king, the details of the
date being Vṛiśchika. śu. 13 (wrong for 3) Wednesday, Mūla, corresponding
regularly to A.D. 1130, November 5, Wednesday; ·98; ·78.

inscription.head:
No. 120.
(A.R. No. 339 of 1952-53.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
Commences with the introduction Pūmālai miḍaindu etc. Registers a
gift of land by sale, by the Śrīvaishṇava-kanmis of the temple headed by Aṇiya-
raṅgadāsar and inclusive of the temple accountant Tiruvaraṅgappiriyaṉ for a
flower garden called Avirōdiśīlaṉ for the god to Kāṅgaiyaṉ alias Paṭṭamuḍaiyāṉ
Mūvāyirattoṟuvaṉ of Paṭṭam in Āvūrkūṟṟam in Nittavinōda-vaḷanāḍu, at the
instance of Śrīnara-vichchādira Brahma-mārāyar, the Śrīkāryam of the temple.
The record is dated in the 15th regnal year of the king and furnishes details viz.,
Mīna, ba. 10, Wednesday, Uttirāḍam, which correspond to A.D. 1113, March,
3, Friday (not Wednesday) ·49; ·53.

inscription.head:
No. 121.
(A.R. No. 31 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a sale of land for a flower garden under orders of the Śrīkāryam
officer Tiruvālanāṭṭu-Mānāḍuḍaiyār to Kōṭṭūr-uḍaiyāṉ Tiruvikramaṉ.
Mentions the land endowed by Uḍaiyāṉ Vēḷāṉ Karuṇākaraṉ alias Toṇḍaimāṉār.
The inscription is dated in the 15th year of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 122.
(A.R. No. 33 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers gift of land by purchase, by Śīr-Iḷaṅkōṉ Tirunāḍuḍaiyāṉ for
feeding Śrīvaishṇava pilgrims on new moon days and ten Malayāṇa(ḷa) Brāh-
maṇas during the Paṅguṉi festival. This gift is placed under the protection of
the Abhimānabhūshaṇar of the three maṇḍalas. The record is dated in the
16th regnal year of the king.

edition.l:

tiraḷ puyattirunilamaṭantaiyoṭu jayamakaḷiruppat tan varai mārvan taṉateṉap

[vantaruḷi velaṟkarum porppuliyāṉai] pār[t*]tivar cūṭa niṟai maṇi makuṭam
muṟaimayiṟ cūṭi maṉṉuyiṟ kellām miṉṉuyir[t*]tāy poṟṟaṇ oḷi parap
pit taṉittaṉi parattu maṇmuḻutu kaḷippa maṉu neṟi vaḷarttu taṉ koyiṟ
koṟṟa vācaṟ-

[puṟattu maṇi nāvoṭuṅka muracukaḷ muḻaṅka vijaiya]mum pukaḻum meṉmeloṅka
vāḻi vāḻi mānilaṅ kākkat tirumaṇi poṟ ṟeṭṭeḻutu pattāṇṭu varumuṟai
munne maṉṉavar cumantu tiṟai niraittuc corinta cempoṟk kaiyāl tan
kulanāya-

kaṉ tāṇṭavam puriyum cempoṉ ampalañcūḻ tirumāḷikaiyum kopura vācalil
kūṭa cāḷamum ulaku valaṅ koṇṭoḷi viḷaṅku nemi kulavarai utaiyakuṉṟa
moṭu [ni]ṉṟenap pacumpoṉm meyntu pala vaḷar piṭamum vicum-

poḷit taḻaippa viḷaṅkupoṉ mentu irunilan taḻaippa imaiyavar kaḷippap periya
tirunāḷ perum per viḻāvennum uyar puraṭṭāti uttiraṭṭātiyil ampala
niṟaitta aṟputak kūttar inpar vāḻa eḻuntaruḷuvataṟkut tirutterk koyil
cemponimayattu parutiraḷ muttiṉ payilvaṭam parappi niṟaimaṇi māḷikai neṭun
terviti tiruvaḷar perāl ceytu camaittaruḷi paimpo-

ṟ kuvitta parikalamutalāl cempoṟ kaṟpakattoṭu paricciṉṉamum aḷavillātaṉa
oḷipeṟa vamaittup pattāmāṇṭu cittirait tiṅkaḷattam peṟṟa ātittavārat
tut tiru

pakkattu iḷanaṉa palavum initu camaittaruḷit taṉ
oru kuṭaik kiḻ taraṇi muḻutum taḻaippa ceḻiyar veñcuram puka ceralar kaṭal
puka vaḻitaru ciṅkaṇarañci neñcalamara kaṅkar tiṟaiyiṭa kaṇṇaṭar

ventiṭa koṅka rotuṅka koṅkaṇar cāya maṟṟetticai maṉnarun tantamak
karaṇen tirumalar ceppi urimaiyilaṟaiñci āṅkavar cūḻuṅ kaṅkai yoppākiya
terivaiyar tila[tam*] tiyāka patākai purikuḻal maṭappiṭi-

puṉita kuṇavaṉitai puvaṉamuḻuvatuṭaiyāḷivaṉ tiruvuḷḷat taruḷu muḻutuṭai
yāḷeṉa uṭaniruppa neṭumālākattu piriyāteṉṟum mātar maṭamayil pūtalat tarun
tati araṇiya kaṟpiṟ taraṇimuḻutuṭaiyāḷivaṉ tirumaṇi mārvattaruḷuṭaniruppa cempo-

edition.l:
n virasiṅhāsanattu puvaṉamuḻutuṭaiyāḷoṭum viṟṟiruntaruḷiya kopparakecari
panmarāna tiripuvanacakkaravattikaḷ śrī vikkiramacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu 16- vatu [||]
pāṇṭikulācani vaḷanāṭṭu viḷānāṭṭu akaram brammateyam śrī-

l.ta:
mummuṭic coḻac caruppetimaṅkalattu piṭākai allūr viracoḻavatikku kiḻakku
virarācentira vākkālukku teṟku eṭṭāṅkaṇṇāṟṟu mutal tuṇṭattu kiḻ panaṅ
kāṭṭu nari-

vanattukkuk kiḻakku allūr [kiṇaṟum] peruvaḻikku
vaṭakku centiṟattu iḷaiyanārāyaṇa paṭṭan mākāṇikku meṟku tiruvuṭkuṭi teva
tāna[m] tevimacakkalu[kku] teṟ-

ku naṭuvuṭpaṭṭa nilamākāṇiyum aḻakiya maṇavāḷap perumāḷaṭiyāril ciriḷaṅkon
tirunāṭuṭaiyānen ittevaṟku patinmunṟāvatum patinālāvatum ivvūr muṟic
ceṭṭu māta-

vapaṭṭan pakkalum puḷḷimaṅkalattu ciṅkappirānāna caṭakopa paṭṭan pakkalum
emmilicainta vilaipporuḷanṟ[ā]ṭu naṟkācu patinañcu ikkācu patinañcu iṭṭu
tanmārttamā-

ṇarai[yum*] amutu ceyvikka[ka*]ṭavatākavum paṅkunit
tirunāḷ aḻakiya maṇavāḷap perumā-

ṇar nittam
pattu śrī vayiṣṇavar amutu ceyyak kaṭavatākavum ipparicu ivviraṇṭu tanmamum
catirātitta[val*] cellakkaṭavi-

tākavum ittanmañ cellumiṭattu innilam mākāṇikkum ūr māvintavari
iṟuttu itil mikutikoṇṭu muṟicceṭṭu mātavapaṭṭarai amu[tu*] ceyvikka
kaṭavarāka ippaṭi

ṭṭuvittukkuṭutten aḻakiyamaṇavāḷap perumāḷaṭiyāril ciriḷaṅkon tirunāṭuṭai
yānen itil mikitik kuṟaivuḷḷana aḻakiyamaṇavāḷap perumāḷaṭiyā[r]k
kaykkoḷare ārāyntu kuṭukkakkaṭavarākavum itu munṟuma-

edition.l: ṇṭalattu apimāna bhūṣaṇar irakṣai—

inscription.head:
No. 123.
(A.R. No. 56 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Commences with the introduction Pūmaṉṉupadumam. Purports to be
an order issued by the deity leasing the temple lands to the kōvaṇavar who
were to plant cocoanut and areca palms thereon and to pay a specified portion
of the yield to the temple year after year. The inscription is dated Kuṁbha,
ba. 5, Saturday, Svātī, in the 7th year of the king's reign and the details work
out regularly to A.D. 1140, February 10, Saturday; ·14; ·35.

l.ta:
pūmantu patuvam pūtta eḻulakun tāmuṉ ceytavattāl paruti vaḻit
tonṟiya neṭumālivaneṉac cuṭarmuṭi cūṭi irunilamakaḷai urimaiyil puṇarntu
tirumakaḷ paṇaimulaic ceñcāntaṇaintu parumaṇimārvam paruvarai nikaṟpac jayamakaḷ
ceḻuñcantaṉac cuvaṭṭāl puyamirukayilaip poruppeṉat toṉṟa nāmakaḷ tānum
meṅkomakan cevvāy pavalac ceyyoḷi paṭaittaṉa niyānenat-

tavaḷananneṟit tanittoṭaiyiteṉap pukaḻmakaḷ cintai makiḻnāḷinum orukuṭai nilavum
porupaṭaittikiri veyilenak karuṅkali iruḷiṉait turappa niṭupallūḻi eḻkaṭaṟ
puṟattiṉuṅ koṭāc centanikko linitulāva minamuñcilaiyum citaintu vāṉuyar
poṉṉeṭu meruviṟ puli viṟṟiruppa umpariyāṉai ōrroṭṭiṉukkum tampameṉṉa
tanittani ticaitoṟum
mpam niṟ(p)pa paci pakaiyāṉatu tituniṅki mannuyir

taḻaippa manuvāṟu viḷaṅka mātavar tavamum maṅkaiyar kaṟpum ātiyantaṇar
ākutikkaṉalum miyteḻu koṇṭal vicu taṇ puṉalum nitiyil va[ru]m cāti oḻukkamum
nitiyaṟamum piṟaḻātu nikaḻp pāvum paḻaṉapparappum paṇaikkaimāvu mallatu vanṟaṉaip
paṭutal kaṉaviluṅ kāṇṭaṟkariteṉa varunetip puṭaiyilum palveṟu puḷḷiṉa mallatu
ciṟaiyeṉap paṭutaliṉṟi niṟai peruñ ce[l]vamoṭa-

vani vāḻp pallavar teluṅkar māḷuvar kaliṅkar koṅkalar kanṉaṭar kaṭāra[r*] tenṉa[r]
ceralar cetiyar tirikarttar vaṅka raṅkar vattirar mattira kaṅkar coṉakar kaiykaiyar
jinakar enṟa aṟaikaḻal ventarum allā araicarum muṟaimaiyil vantiṟai
kuṇarntiṟaiñca tirumalaṟ celvi cempiyan kiḻānaṭi orumaruṅ kuṭanamarntiruppa aruḷ
poruḷ cimaiyap poṟ koṭṭimaiyappāvaiyum civanum pola vira

ṉattu
puvaṉamuḻutuṭaiyāḷoṭum viṟṟiruntaruḷiya kovirācakecaripanmarāna tiripuvanaccakkara-
vattikaḷ śrīkulottuṅkacoḻa tevaṟku yāṇṭu ēḻāvatu—kumpanāyaṟṟu aparapakṣattu
pañcamiyum caṉikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa cotināḷ tiruvaraṅkat tiruppatiyil śrīvai-

yiṣṇavakaḷom tirukkuṟai kamukum teṅku[m] vaiyttu tiruccolaiyākka veṇume[ṉ*]
ṟum itu ākku miṭattu •• kamuku vaittāl ikkamuku śrīpaṇṭārattukku uṭa[lā]ka
kaṭava pākku oruva ••••• niccayittu vaiykka veṇumenṟu pāttu ippaṭi aḻakiyamaṇa
vāḷap perumāḷukku viṇṇappañceyya aḻakiyamaṇavāḷapperumāḷ varicai tiruvāy

l.gr:
2 ētat trailōkya
nirmmāṇatrāṇa saṅhāra kāraṇam | śrīmat śrīraṅganāthasya śāsanam śāśvatam
param ||
l.ta: nam viṭṭu kuṭavaruṅ ko[va*]ṇavarkaḷukkum nam ka

kaḷukkum
nam variyilārkkum nam pu ••••• ṅkaḷil kamu[ku*]n teṅkum vaikkalān
taṟai atto ••••• kil naṅ koṇavarkaḷe ka-

mukum vaikkak kaṭavarkaḷākavum kamukunteṅkum ākkik kaṭamai koḷḷumiṭattu
akavāṟṟil pākku koḷa pukka vāṇṭu talaiyāl nūṟu pākkāka vanta pākku
iraṇṭāmāṇṭu talaiyāl irunūṟu pākkāka vanta pākkum muvvāmāṇṭu talaiyāl
munnūṟu pākkāka vanta pākkum nālāmāṇṭu mutal talaiyāl nā[ṉūṟu] pākkāka
vanta pākkum kollai-

ttaṟaiyil kamukku toppukku vāṇṭu talaiyāl eṇpatu pākku iraṇṭāmāṇṭu
talaiyāl nūṟṟirupatu pākkāka vanta pākkum muvāmāṇṭu talaiyāl irunūṟṟu
nāṟpatu pākkāka vanta pākkum nālāmāṇṭu mutal talaiyāl munnūṟu pākkāka
vanta pākku eṇṇakkaṭavatākavum [|*] ippaṭi akavāṟṟup paṟṟukku talaiyāl
nānūṟu ••• kollait taṟaiyil niṉṟa kamuku-

kku talaiyāl munnūṟu pākkāka vanta pākku eṇṇumiṭattu ava[r] tuccam
āyirattiṉ vāy patinañcu pākkāka eṟṟi pākkāle nam paṇṭārattile eṇṇak
kaṭavarkaḷākavum [|*] teṅku talai nirampik kāccāl teṅku[kku kā]cu oru māvākavum
mā[vāl] oru[mā]k kācākavum vanta kāce koḷḷak kaṭavatākavum connom
[|*] ippaṭikku nam āṇai iṭṭuk kallilum cempilum veṭṭi kamukun teṅkum
vaikka-

lān taṟai ellām vaittākkip paṇṇuka [|*] tiruvāy moḻintaruḷa ivai
tiruvaraṅkappiriyan eḻuttu [|*] ivai māṅkuṭit ta[lai]makaṉukku mokkum [|*]
ippaṭikku mel koḷvār aḻakiyamaṇavāḷapperumāḷ śrī ••••• ṇai

inscription.head:
No. 124.
(A.R. No. 55 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers an endowment of land after purchase, for a flower garden to the
temple, by three private individuals who also made additional gifts of money
for the maintainance of five gardeners. The land is said to have been purchased
by the donors from the temple itself. The details of the date viz. 11th year,
Siṁha, ba. 14 Wednesday, Āyilyam correspond to A.D., 1143, August 11,
Wednesday; ·57; ·45.

l.ta:
pūmanṉu patumam pūtta veḻulakun tāmuṉ ceytavattāṟ paruti •••••
neṭumālivaneṉac cuṭarmuṭicūṭi irunilamakaḷai urimaiyiṟ puṇarntu tirumakaḷ
paṇaimulai ceñcāntaḷaintu parumaṇimārvum paruvarai nikaṟpa jayamakaḷ ceḻuñ
cāntaṇac cuvaṭṭāṟ puyamiru kayilaip poruppenat toṉṟi nāmakaḷ tāṉuṅ
komakaṉ ce-

vvāyp pavaḷac ceyoḷi paṭaittana niyāneṉa tavaḷa naṉ niṟat tiruttuṭaiy
yenavun pukaḻ makaḷ cintaiyai makiḻunāḷilum oru kuṭai veyilinuṅ karuṅkali
iruniḷait turappa niṭu pallūḻi eḻkaṭal puṟattiṉuṅ koṭāc centanik kolinitulāva
miṉamuñ cilaiyum citaintu vāṉuyarp poṉṉeṭu meruviṟ poli viṟṟiruppa umpar
yāṉaiyor reṭṭinukkum

mpa ni[ṟ]pa paci pakaiyānatu titu
niṅka mannuyir taḻaippa manuvā[ṟu] viḷaṅka mātavar tavamum maṅkaiyar kaṟpum āti

ṇar āku[ti*]kkanalum [miyteḻu] koṇṭal vicun taṇ puṉalum metini vaḷaruñ
cāti yoḻukkamum nīti yaṟamum piṟaḻātu nikaḻap puravum paḻana[ṟ]parappumallatu vaṉṟaḷaip
paṭutal kaṉaviluṅ-

kāṇṭaṟ kariteṉa varunati puṭaiyilum palveṟu puḷḷiṉa mallatu ciraiyena[p]
paṭutali[ṉ*]ṟi niṟaiperuñ celva moṭavaṉi vāḻ pallavar teliṅkar māḷavar
kaliṅka kocalar kaṉṉaṭar teṉnar koṅkar [ce]tiyar tirikarttar vaṅkaraṅkar mattirar
mattirakaṅkar conakar kaiykaiyar cinaren ṟaṟai kaḻal ventarumellā varai
carum muṟaimaiyil vantu tiṟai kuṇantiṟaiñca

edition.l:
ampoṉ malaṟkoṭi cempiyaṉ kiḻāṉaṭi orumaruṅkuṭaṉamarntiṉi tiruppa a[ruḷ
peruccir[ā]maiy poṟkoṭi yimaiyap pāvaiyuñ civaṉum polac cempoṉ virasi
ṅhāsanattu puvaṉamuḻutuṭaiyāḷoṭum viṟṟiruntaruḷi kovirājakecari
panmarāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīkulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu 11- vatu
ciṅka nāyaṟṟu a-

l.ta:
yum putankiḻamaiyum peṟṟa [āyiliyattu] nāḷ tiru
varaṅkan tiruppatiyil tiruvāy moḻintaruḷi śrīvayiṣṇavaril parakecariceri •••••
kārimāṟa nampiyum toṭittāṇṭānum kantāṭai tirumaṅkai
āḷinampiyum tirukka ••• ṇṭa nampiyum iṭavai nārāyaṇa nampiyum perum
puliyūr arumāritā

rum paḷḷikoṇṭān nampi uḷḷiṭṭa ivvaṉaivom caṅkāḻ
vānāṉa [ciraṅka nāta]ṉukkum perumāṭi tevaṉukkum ivarkaḷ mātākka ••• ••• ••••
tirunaṉtavanattukku icainta kiḻpāṟkellai tirumārviṭaṅ
koṇṭāṉ tirunaṉtavanattukku meṟku tenpāṟkellai tennāṟṟukku vaṭakku
melpāṟkellai irā[ca] nārāyaṇa ntiruna

kkum vaṭapāṟkellai tiruveṭṭaik keḻuntaruḷun tiruvitikkut teṟkum icainta
perunāṉkellai yuḷḷakappaṭṭa nilam koyiṟkolāl kuḻi āyiram ikkuḻi
āyiramum tiruna

vanañ ceykaikku viṟṟukkuṭuttom innilam viṟṟukkuṭuttuk
koḷvatāka anṟāṭu naṟponnukku ciraṅkavattanin pakkalil koṇṭa pon
irupatu ivvirupatiṉ kaḻañ-

cum ivarkaḷ mātākkaḷ pakkal koṇṭa pon patin kaḻañcum āka ivarkaḷ pakkal
koṇṭa pon ••••• poṉ muppatin kaḻañcuṅ koṇṭu śrī paṇ
ṭāratte oṭukki ivarkaḷukku viṟṟuk kuṭut[tom] tevar tiruvaraṅkan tiruppatiyil
tiruvāy moḻintaruḷiṉa śrīvayiṣṇavarkaḷom [|*] ivarkaḷukku viṟṟuk
kuṭutta tirunaṉtavanam koyil

kolāl kuḻi āyiram ivvāyirattukkum ituve vilaiyāvatākavum ituvallatu
veṟu poru[ḷ*] māvaṟutip poruṭ cilavolai kāṭṭak kaṭavatallātatākavum
ipparicu vilaikkaṟa viṟṟu poruḷaṟak koṇṭu vilaiyāvaṇañ ceytu kuṭuttom
caṅkāḻvārāṉa ciraṅka[nātaṉukkum] perumāṭi tevanukkum ivarkaḷ mātāk-

kaḷukkum tiruvaraṅkan tiruppatiyil tiruvāy moḻintaruḷina śrīvayiṣṇavarkaḷom caṅ
kāḻvānāṉa ciraṅka ••••• yyum śrīvayiṣṇavar per [munṟu]kku
jivanattukku cantirātittaval palicaiyāl śrī paṇṭāratte cellakkaṭavatāka śrī paṇ
ṭārattu oṭukkina pon [eṇpatin kaḻañcu] ippon eṇpatin kaḻañcum perumāṭi
tevaṉ tirunantavaṉañ ceyyum śrīvaiyiṣṇavar oruvarkku jīvitam cantirā
[titta*]val śrī paṇ-

ippoṉ muppatin kaḻañcum
ivarkaḷ mātākkaḷ ••••• vayiṣṇavar oruvarkku jivanattukku
cantirā[titta*]vaṟ cellakkaṭavatānamaikku śrīpaṇṭāratte oṭukkina pon

ippon
muppatin kaḻañcum āka tiru na

vaṉañ ceyyum per ayvarkku jivanam cantirātittavaṟ
palicaiyāl celvatāka śrī paṇṭārattu oṭukkiṉa pon

ippon nūṟṟu nāṟpatin kaḻañcum oṭukka ••• koṇṭu ittirunaṉta
vanañ ceyyap paṇṇi ••••• t tiruvaraṅkat tiruppatiyil tiruvāy
moḻintaruḷina śrī vayiṣṇavarkaḷom [|*] ippaṭikku ivai koyiṟ kaṇakku
tiruvaraṅkappiriyaṉ eḻuttu [|*] ippaṭikku ivai aṭikaḷ

yātanamaikku
ivai tiruvāymoḻi araiyaneḻuttu [|*] itai tiruk kiḻkaṇṭa

inscription.head:
No. 125.
(A.R. No. 68 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of a golden lamp-stand set with one ruby and an endow-
ment of money for maintaining the lamp and for the supply of camphor, by
Kōdai Ravivarmaṉ of Vēṇāḍu in Malai-nāḍu. The details of date, viz.,
11th year, Vṛiśchika, śu. 5, Monday, Tiruvōṇam regularly correspond to A.D.
1156, November 19, Monday, ·63; ·58.

l.ta:
pūmaruviya tirumātum puvimātu jeyamātum nāmaruviya kalaimātum
pukaḻmātu nayantu pulka arumaṟai vitineṟi aṉaittun taḻaippa varumuṟai urimai
yaṇimuṭicūṭit tiṅkaḷ veṇkuṭait ticaik kaḷiṟeṭṭun taṅku taṉikkūṭan-

tāṉeṉa viḷaṅkap porukalip paṭṭikaic ceṅkol turappa voru katirāḻi puvi
yaḷantuṭaṉvara villavar yiraṭṭar mi[na]var ciṅkaḷar pallavar nutaliyar pāttivar
paṇiya eṇṇaruṅ kaṟpil maṇṇaka[m] puṇa[r]ntu cempoṉ

ṉattup puvaṉimuḻutuṭaiyara(ḷ)ḷoṭum viṟṟiruntaruḷiya kopparakecari
paṟmarāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ

tevarkku yāṇṭu patiṉonṟāvatu
miriccika nāyaṟṟu pū

pakṣattup pañcamiyum tiṅkaḷ kiḻamaiyum [pe]ṟṟa tiru[vo]ṇattiṉ nāḷ tiru
varaṅkam periya koyilil tiruvaṭi vaḷavañ ceykiṟa tiruvaraṅkatā

cerit
tiruvaraṅkavaḷḷalārum śrīvaiṣṇava vāriyañ ceykiṟa nālūṟ ciṅkappirā nampiyum
vaṅkippuṟattu tiruvā[y*]kkulattu āḻvāṉum viranārāya-

inscription.head:
No. 126.
(A.R. No. 122 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records an agreement given by Kāśyapaṉ Tiru ••• ttavaṉ perumāḷ
alias Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷa Brahmārāyaṟ, his brother •••• perumāḷ alias
Vīrachōḻa Brahmārāyar and Śiṅgapirāṉ Chakrapāṇi to Poṉ-parappiṉār alias
Vaṅgāra Muttaraiyar to cultivate the 2 mā of garden land purchased from Śrī
Gōvinda-perumāḷ alias Pagaivali Aṇukka Brahmārāyar and Tiruvāykkulam
Uḍaiyāṉ Uttamanambi for 17,000 kāśu. The inscription is dated in the
11th regnal year of Tribhuvaṉachakravartigaḷ Rājarāja who may be identified
with Rājarāja II on palaeographical considerations.

The portion containing the details of the date is partly damaged on the stone
the last letter of the tithi mi along with the other details viz., the weekday
(Saturday) and the nakshatra (Svāti) being clear. If the intended tithi is ba.
ashṭami, then the details given viz., 11th year, Saturday and Svātī regularly
correspond to A.D. 1157, January 5, Saturday, f.d.t. ·13; f.d.n. ·24. The
month was Makara.

nampimāril kā

inscription.head:
No. 127.
(A.R. No. 63 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records an endowment in money for a lamp in the temple by Periya
Tiruśuṭṭi-śāttiṉa Perumāḷ alias Rājarāja Uttama-śeṭṭi of Kuraṭṭippaṭṭaṉam
situated in Kaivāra-nāḍu, a sub-division of Poyśaḷa-nāḍu. The record is dated
in the 9th regnal year of the king Tribhuvaṉachchakravartigaḷ Rājādhirāja-
dēva who may be identified with Rājādhirājadēva II on palaeographical consi-
derations.

inscription.head:
No. 128.
(A.R. No. 73 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Commences with the praśasti Kaḍal-śūḻnda pārmādarum, etc. Records an
endowment of 1000 kāśu by Vīṟṟirundāṉ śēmaṉ alias Tirukkuṟaivaḷa[r*]tta
Akaḷaṅka-Nāḍāḻvāṉ of Tiruttavattuṟai for conducting certain special festivals
in the temple. (vide Nos. 129-131 below). The record is dated in the 9th year
of the king's reign.

inscription.head:
No. 129.
(A.R. No. 267 of 1930.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, OPPOSITE THE UḌAIYAVAR-SANNIDHI.
inscription.p:
Records an oath of fealty taken by Nāyaṉ alias Aḻagiya Maṇavāḷa Mārāyaṉ,
a Kaikkōḷa-Mudali of Tiruvaraṅgam, to serve unto death, as a vēḷaikkāṟaṉ of
Vīṟṟirundāṉ śēmaṉ alias Akaḷaṅka-Nāḍāḷvāṉ. The record is in characters of
the 12th century A.D. (Vide No. 128 above.)

l.ta: ṟku tiruvaraṅkattu kaikkoḷa mutalikaḷil nāyanāṉa aḻakiya ma-
ṇavāḷa mārāyanen ivaṟku pinpu cāvāte iruntenākil en

inscription.head:
No. 130.
(A.R. No. 268 of 1930.)
IV PRĀKĀRA WALL, OPPOSITE THE UḌAIYAVAR-SANNIDHI.
inscription.p:
Records an oath of fealty as in No. 129 above, by Ariyāṉ alias Kiḍāratta-
raiyaṉ.

n v[e*]ḷaiyākac cāvakkaṭavenākavum ivaṟku pinpu cāvāte irun-

inscription.head:
No. 131.
(A.R. No. 269 of 1930.)
IV PRĀKĀRA WALL, OPPOSITE THE UḌAIYAVAR-SANNIDHI.
inscription.p:
Records an oath of fealty taken as in No. 129 above, by Ulagaṉ alias
Aḻagāṉaichchōḻa-Mārāyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 132.
(A.R. No. 50 of 1948-49.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Records a gift of 4,000 kāśu probably for purchase of some lands,
by Kēśuvaṉ Sundarattōḷuḍaiyāṉ alias Śiṅgapperumāḷ-dāsaṉ of Vēmbaṟṟūr in
Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam for conducting a service on the day of Uttiram, the natal star
of the donor. The record is dated in the reign (year lost) of Tribhuvaṉachchakra-
vartigaḷ Kulōttuṅgachōḻadēva ‘who was pleased to take Madurai, Īḻam and the
crowned head of the Pāṇḍya.’ i.e., Kulōttuṅga III.

tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyum īḻamum karuvūrum pāṇṭiyan muṭit
talaiyuṅ koṇṭu vīr(ā)a

piḷḷai ciṅkapperumāḷ

inscription.head:
No. 133.
(A.R. No. 89 of 1936-37.)
VEḶḶAIGŌPURAM, INNER WALL.
inscription.p:
States that the worship of the god who was the tutelary deity of the king,
and the repairs to the prākāra wall called Magadēśan alias Aḍaiyavaḷaindāṉ-
tirumāḷigai were left in charge of Tāyilumnallāṉ alias Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-
Vāṇakōvaraiyar.

l.ta: kulatanamāy varukiṟa koyilil tiruvārātaṉamum makatecaṉāna a-

inscription.head:
No. 134.
(A.R. No. 120 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of land by Kōdaikaṇi Śeṭṭiyār of Kuḷamukkil for rearing a
flower garden called Perundēvi for the god. Veṅgūr Śrīrāmabhaṭṭa-Vājapē
[ya*]-yājiyār was the Śrīkāryam of the temple. (See No. 138 below). The date
is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 135.
(A.R. No. 75 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Begins with the praśasti Puyal vāyttu. The record is incomplete. Seems
to record an endowment by •• kaṉ Sēnāpati Āḻvāṉ Aḻagiya-maṇavāḷapperumāḷ
for the sacred bath and offerings to the deity in the name of Śi(Śrī) Puṇḍarīkaṉ
on special festival days in the months of Aippaśi and Paṅguṉi. The record is
dated in the third regnal year of the king.

l.ta:
puyalvāyttu vaḷam perukap poyyāta nāṉmaṟaiyiṉ ceyal
vāytta tiru[makaḷum]

ceyamakaḷuñ ciṟaṉtu vāḻa veṇmatipol kuṭai viḷaṅka velventaraṭi vaṇaṅka
maṇmaṭantai maṉaṅkaḷippa ma-

puvikākka ••• t tārāka [kayaltāṅki]p poṟpamai
tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrī kulottuṅkacoḻa tevaṟku yāṇṭu

tirumañcaṉam paṇṇiyaruḷi [tiruvār]ātaṉaṅ koṇṭaruḷi amutu ceytārum

inscription.head:
No. 136.
(A.R. No. 61 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of the village Tiruvāyppāḍinallūr, made tax free, for special
worship and offerings to the god on the day of the Daivattarayaṉ festival by the
gōpālas who had proprietory rights over Vaḷḷuvappāḍi-nāḍu in Karikālakaṇṇa-
vaḷanāḍu. Mentions the Śrīkāryam officer Kavaittalai-uḍaiyār. The record
is dated in the 6th year of śrī Vīrarājēndra which is a surname of Kulōttuṅga
III with whose praśasti the record begins. The details of date viz. 6th year,
Mīṉa, ba. 6, Monday, Kēṭṭai, regularly correspond to A.D. 1184, March 5,
Monday; ·58; f.d.n. ·10.

maikku ivvūrkku koyiṟṟa

ūrkaḷile ēṟiṭṭu viḻukkāṭu iṭṭu viḻukkāṭṭu

kaṭamai iṉṉā •••
••• mutalikaḷukku nāṅkaḷe iṟukka kaṭavomāvum [|*] ippaṭiye cantirātittavaṟ
cellak kaṭavatākaccammatittut tiruvāyppāṭi nallūr tiruviṭaiyāṭṭamāka
maikkuk kalveṭṭik kuṭuttom vaḷḷuvappāṭi nāṭu kā-

ṇi uṭaiya śrī kopālarom [|*] iṉṉāṭṭurkaḷukkuc camainta tuṟaiyūruṭaiyāṉ
caṅkaraṉ celvaṉāṉa amarakoṉum ottaṉ kūṭṭeriyāṉa aḷakaikkoṉum
periñcalūruṭaiyāṉ ārāvamutu kāvaṉāṉa kovattarāyaṉum maṇavāḷa nampi
yāṉa pala maṇṭalak koṉum [t]eṟ kūṭalāṉ puttāṟṟaṅkaraiyuṭaiyāṉ cuntaṉ
celvaṉāṉa vaḷḷuvappāṭi nampiyum vaṭukaṉ nam

piyāṉa karikāla kaṉṉavaḷanāṭṭuk koṉum pirampil uṭaiyāṉ muṭikoṇṭāṉ
ulakaḷanta coḻakkoṉum maṇavāḷaṉ teṟṟiyāṉa āyira vallavak koṉum
kuṉṟaṇi uṭaiyāṉ naṉta uṭaiyāṉ uyyavantā(ṉ)ṉum vā[ḻ]kai tiruppe •
•• coṟaṉ ta[ṇi]kaṉum murukuṭaiyāṉ paṭṭaṉ piḷḷaiyum peruvallamuṭaiyāṉ
iḷaiyāṉ comaṉum eḻuvāṉ coṟaṉum kaṇṇaṉ ūruṭaiyāṉ maṇavāḷaṉ

c[e]lvak koṉum kaṭuṅkarai uṭaiyāṉ koḻuntaṉ kopālapiḷḷaiyum aru
moḻi nallūruṭaiyāṉmpala nampiyāna āṭkoṇṭakoṉṉum tirumaḻapāṭi
cuṉtaṉum na • nattamuṭaiyāṉ kū[t]tan maṇavāḷak koṉum nirutaṉ
pirampaṉum nāḻaluṭaiyāṉa teṉntoḻiyāṉa nālūr nā[ṭ]ṭuk koṉum
koṭṭaiyūruṭaiyān periyāḷ tiruvaraṅkakoṉum tūṅkāṉai uṭaiyāṉ cemaṉ puku
vāyaṉāṉa pakaiyāta kaṇṭakkoṉum nampi aṟappaḷḷiyum

yāṉa tiruvaṉ kaliyaṉum cirampa •• yāṉa piccaṉ iḷaiyāṉāṉa vaṇṭuvarāpatik
ko • ṉum māyilaṅkai [yu]ṭaiyāṉ kallaṉ atikaiyarāyaṉum veṇmaṇip
pāṭiyuṭaiyāṉ vempaṉ tiruvaṉum piṟaiyom[pi] yuṭaiyāṉa celvaṉ nakkaṉum
toṟkalaiyuṭaiyāṉ nāṭṭāṉ iḷaiyāṉum pullāliyuṭaiyāṉ teṉ atikai
yarāyaṉum maṇiyaṉ kuṟicciyuṭaiyāṉ cuṟṟipaṭṭāṉum alappi[li]yuṭaiyāṉ
ottaṉ cūṟṟiyum teṉṉūruṭaiyāṉ veṉkāṭaṉ te-

ṟṟiyum pakavati maṅkalamuṭaiyā[ṉ] ••••• aṟaṅkāttāṉṉum avaṉimaṅkala-
muṭaiyāṉ aṭippirampaṉum ūti ma[n]tai uṭaiyāṉ nicaṉ teṟṟiyum varampiyuṭai-
yāṉ aracaṉ peṟṟāṉṉum karumpaṉuṭaiyāṉ ampalavaṉ tiruvaṉum celamuṭaiyāṉ
coṟṟaṉ [ka]ṇak[ka]ṉum taṇṭuṟai uṭaiyāṉ koḻuṉtaṉ kovintarāyaṉum
maruttūruṭaiyāṉ araṅkaṉ virāṉtakoṉum pullaṅkuṭaiyāṉ maṟavaṉ ciṅkaḷānta
kkoṉum ciṟu natta muṭaiyāṉ malai tirumaḻapāṭiyum

cāttaṉuṭaiyāṉ kaṇakkaṉ ••••• kaṇappāṭiyuṭaiyāṉ periyāṉ teṟṟiyum
kuṇamaṅkalamuṭaiyāṉ kecavaṉ nampiyum pūṉtuṟaiyuṭaiyāṉ ciṅkaṉ celvaṉāṉa
kulottuṅkakoṉum māyilaṅkaiyuṭaiyāṉ malai tiruvaṉum nāvalūruṭaiyāṉ
periyāḷ ukavātiyum kaḷḷinattamuṭaiyāṉ kumpiṭaṉ kāḷiyum vallamuṭai
yāṉ ceṉtaṉ cuṉtaṉāṉa tiruvāḷakkoṉum eḻuvaṉ iḷamaik koṉṉum

kuruviyūruṭaiyāṉ vaṭukaṉ maṇṭalak koṉum tillaivāṇa nallūruṭaiyāṉ celvaṉ
cuntara coḻakkoṉum nampivaṭukaṉum kūttāṭu nallūruṭaiyāṉ kallaṉ
viḻumiyatevakkoṉum tevaṉ aṟaṅkāttāṉum piraṇṭai nallūruṭaiyāṉ
koṟṟaṉ cāttaṉum muttāṉ paṭṭaṉum karikālanallūruṭaiyāṉ coṟaṉ oḷiyan
ṉum ciṟukarikāli uṭaiyāṉ tirumaḻapāṭi uṭaiyāṉ centāmarai ka[ṇ*]ṇaṉum

kaḷattuṭaiyāṉ tiruvaṉ uyyavantāṉum naṭukalūruṭaiyāṉ mummuṭi coḻakkoṉum
turattūruṭaiyāṉ ārā amutukāvaṉum ciṟu kaṇṇaṉūruṭaiyāṉ tevaṉ cuṉtaṉum
kaḷḷikkuṭiyuṭaiyāṉ uṭaiyāṉ tiruvāyppāṭik koṉum celvaṉ aracanāṉa
tiruvāyk kulakkoṉum tiruvaraṅkamāṉi puliyaṉum aiyyāṟaṉ yirācāciriyakkoṉum
puliyūruṭaiyāṉ teṟṟi irāmaṉum

ciṟuvayallūruṭaiyāṉ catti tāmotirakkoṉum pulvāykuṭiyuṭaiyāṉ aracu
teṟṟiyum iṭaikkuṭi uṭaiyāṉ viracoḻakkoṉum ayaṅkayamuṭaiyāṉ piccaṉ
iraṇṭāyirakkoṉum mātevi tāḻiyūr uṭaiyāṉ caṅkaraṉ celvaṉum putteṟṟaṅ

karaiyuṭaiyāṉ malaitirumaḻapāṭiyum periyāṉ tirumaḻapāṭiyum mātevi uṭaiyāṉ
kāṭeri coṟaṉum varakūruṭaiyāṉ vaḷḷuvappāṭikoṉum

va[lai] uṭaippuṭaiyāṉ utaiyaṉ tiruviṅkaikkoṉum ārācciuṭaiyāṉ a ••
uṭaiyāṉum •• ṭam piṟaiyuṭaiyāṉ coti tiruveṇṇāval koṉum ceṉṉa
maṅkalamuṭaiyāṉ vempaṉ meḻukiyakkoṉu maṉṉamaṅkalamuṭaiyāṉ cā[t*]taṉ
mativāraṇamum ciṟukaḷḷikkuṭaiyāṉ vicaiyapālakkoṉum kiḷiyūruṭaiyāṉ ulakaṉ
nāyaṉnāṉ aṇṭarāyaṉum tiruvāyppāṭi nellūruṭaiyāṉ kāru ili

l araṅkaṉāṉa tiruvāyppāṭi konṉum kallaṉ toṇṭaṉāṉa kovattarāyaṉum
arumoḻi nallūruṭaiyāṉ (m) celvaṉ ṉilaṉum nampi vaṭukaṉum peruṅkaṇṇa
ṉūruṭaiyāṉ aḻakiya maṇavāḷakkoṉum cirākavakkoṉum irāja

māḷum kallaiyāṉ vaṇṭuvarāpatikkoṉum ciṅkaḷāntakaṉallūruṭaiya maṟavaṉ paṭṭaṉum

inscription.head:
No. 137.
(A.R. No. 76 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records an endowment of money for the merit of a person by name
Vāgalarkōḍāli alias ••• nātha-Pallavaraiyaṉ, by his wife and daughter
for conducting a special festival to god Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷa-perumāḷ in the temple
on the day of Rōhiṇī which was his natal star. Mentions the Śrīkāryam officer
Kavattalaiyuḍaiyāṉ Viḻumiyadēvaṉ Perumāṉ (see No. 136 above). The details of
date viz., 7th year, Mithuṉa, ba. 12, Tuesday, Kārttigai, regularly correspond
to A.D. 1185, May 28, Tuesday; ·10; f.d.n. ·08.

inscription.head:
No. 138.
(A.R. No. 119 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of land by a resident of Malaināḍu (name lost) for rearing
a flower-garden called Śūḍikkoḍuttāḷ for the god, when Veṅgūr Śrīrāmabhaṭṭa-
Vājapēya[yā]jiyār was the Śrīkāryam of the temple. (Vide No. 134 above).

inscription.head:
No. 139.
(A.R. No. 63 of 1892.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers the gift of a bronze lamp and 9000 kāśu towards supply of ghee
for the same at the rate of 1 nāḻi per day to Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷapperumāḷ by
Mādhavaṉ Ayirkkāḍaṉ of Muruṇōttamaṅgalam in Malaimaṇḍalam. The
record is dated in the 18th regnal year of the king.
Published in the S.I.I., Vol. IV, No. 510.

āvatu malai

perumāḷukku oru ti[runa]ṉtāviḷakkeriyak[ka]ṭa[vatā]ka nāḷ oṉṟukku tiruvaraṅka
tevanāl nāḻi [ney] [eri]kkak ka[ṭava]tāka oṭukkina kācu

ikkācu onpatiṉāyiramum oṭukki itu eriya oṭukkiṉa veṇkala tirukkutti viḷakku
oṉṟu [i]ppaṭi oṭukku koṇṭom śrī ka

inscription.head:
No. 140.
(A.R. No. 66 of 1892.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL, LEFT OF THE ENTRANCE.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Gives the praśasti of the king and records an order issued
to the revenue officers, the contents of which are lost.

The record is dated in the 19th year, Vṛiśchika, ba. 5, Tuesday, Pūśam
corresponding to A.D. 1196, November 12, Tuesday, ·56, ·57.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. III, No. 88.

l.ta:
puyal vāyttu maṇ vaḷara puli[yā]ṇaiyum cakkaramuñ
ceyal vā[yt]ta manu nūluñ ceṅkolun ticai naṭa[k]ka[k] ko[ṟ]ṟava[nu]ca[ṉ]

nam kuṟaipaṟṟil pū The rest of the inscription is lost.-

inscription.head:
No. 141.
(A.R. No. 67 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of 12 Bhujabala-māḍai for a brass lamp with tripod stand
in the temple by Nūṅkama-Mahādēvi, wife of Madhurāntakap-Pottappichchōḻan
alias Siddharaśaṉ. The inscription is dated in the 19th year of the king's reign.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] tiripuvanaca[k]karavattikaḷ maturaiyum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭi[t*]talaiyum koṇṭa
(a)ruḷiya śrī kulottuṅka coḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu 19- [vatu*] maturāṉtaka pottap
piccoḻanāna citta aracaṉ

l.ta:
viyār aḻakiya maṇavāḷap perumāḷukku tiru
nuntāviḷakku erikaikku ca

r kaiyil varak
kāṭṭi cipaṇṭārattu oṭu-

paṉniraṇṭunāl vanta policaiyāl oru tiruviḷakku
celuttakkaṭavom ānom tiruppati śrī vaiyiṣṇavarkaḷom [|*] it tiru
viḷakku eriya i-

inscription.head:
No. 142.
(A.R. No. 113 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
Refers to the erosion of the river into the lands belonging to the temples
at Śrīraṅgam and Tiruvāṉaikkā and the resettlement of the proper boundaries
under arbitration by Aṇṇavāyiluḍaiyār Gāṅgēyarāyar in the 20th year and
213th day of the King, evidently Kulōttuṅga Chōḷa III. After the necessary
adjustments, the āḻi (disc) and the śūla (trident) stones were planted demarcating
the boundaries.

inscription.head:
No. 143.
(A.R. No. 364 of 1953-54.)
KOṬṬĀRAM, WALL INSIDE THE RICE STORAGE ROOM.
inscription.p:
Beginning built in and damaged and intercepted by walls. Records a gift
of buffaloes by a number of Kōṉs (members of the shepherd community) such
as Śrīraṅga-kkōṉ, Sēṉāpati Śrī Kulaśēkara-kkōṉ, Aḻagiyamaṇavāla-kōṉ, etc.,
for supply of ghee towards some service introduced by Kāśyapaṉ •••••
Perumāḷ alias Akaḷaṅka Brahmārāyar, one of the Śrīpādam-tāṅgum nambimār.
The inscription is dated in the reign of Tribhuvanachakravarti Kulōttuṅga-chōḷa
who was pleased to conquer Madurai, Īḻam, Karuvūr and the crowned head of
the Pāṇḍya (i.e. Kulōttuṅga III). The regnal year quoted in the record
is damaged, only the digit 1 in the unit's place being visible. It may be
assigned either to the 21st or the 31st year of reign as the wording of the
record indicates.

inscription.head:
No. 144.
(A.R. No. 146 of 1938-39.)
V PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL, TO THE RIGHT OF CHOKKAPPĀṈAI GŌPURAM.
inscription.p:
Registers gift of lands in Nagaikku-Māṅguḍi alias Rājēndraśōḻapuram
and three other neighbouring villages clubbing them together under the name
Ayaṇḍambākkiḻāṉ-brahmadēyam by Tiruvaṉantamūtti(mūrti) Aruḷālaṉ
alias Vīrarājēndra-Pallavaraiyaṉ, the Kiḻāṉ of Ayaṇḍambākkam in
Āguḍi-nāḍu, a subdivision of Puḻaṟkōṭṭam alias Vikramaśōḻa-
vaḷanāḍu, a district of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻamaṇḍalam, to some
Brāhmaṇas settled by him in the Rājamahēndran-tiruvīdi at Tiruva-
raṅgam. Tribhuvaṉachakravartin Kōnēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ in whose 24th year and
the 225th day the record is dated, may be identical with Kulōttuṅga Chōḷa III
whose surname Vīrarājēndra was adopted by the donor. Mention is made of the
Piḍāri Aiyapoḻil Mākāḷi.

inscription.head:
No. 145.
(A.R. No. 335 of 1952-53.)
V PRĀKĀRA, OUTER WALL, TO THE RIGHT OF THE PATH LEADING TO THE
CHAKRATTĀḺVĀR SHRINE.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to the same transaction as in the previous record
(No. 144) and mentions among the signatories Kāliṅgarāyar and Vāṇādhirājar.
