inscription.head:
No. 146.
(A.R. No. 18 of 1938-39.)
II PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers gift of lands after purchase from the temple authorities by Ōlai-
maṅgalam-uḍaiyāṉ Tiruveṇkāḍuḍaiyāṉ Śiṟiyāṇḍāṉ, a merchant of Kulōttuṅ-
gachōḻapaṭṭaṇam in Śellūr-nāḍu in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu for offerings to god during
the śandhi instituted in the temple in the name of the donor. The record is
dated in the 32nd regnal year of the king. The agreement was engraved north of
Śrīpāda which is to the east of Tiruvaṇukkaṉ Tirumāḷigai (central shrine) as
stated in the inscription. The details of the date viz. 32nd year, Dhanus,
śu. 1, Sunday and Mūla regularly correspond to A.D. 1209., November 29,
Sunday, ·96, ·76.

inscription.head:
No. 147.
(A.R. No. 32 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers an order of Gāṅgēyarāyar fixing the apportionment of the produce
from several classes of temple lands, between the temple and its tenants. The
record is dated in the 34th regnal year of the king.

muṭittalaiyuṅ koṇṭu virā

inscription.head:
No. 148.
(A.R. No. 17 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
The inscription dated in the 35th year of the king seems to be incomplete.
Records that certain lands were purchased and given as tirunāmattukkāṇi for
worship and offerings to god Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷa-Perumāḷ by Ambalavaṉ-
Kōyilāḻvār, a lady who was under the support (mudugaṇ) of Ambalavaṉ
Periya-Perumāṉ alias Jagadēkavīra-Āchārya of Rājarājapuram. The details
of the date viz., Tulā, śu. 13, Wednesday, Rēvati, correspond to A.D. 1212,
October 10, Wednesday, ·25; f.d.n. ·10.

inscription.head:
No. 149.
(A.R. No. 51 of 1948-49.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Records gift of money and 1 vēli of land in the village Pēyāṭṭi in Kulaśēkhara-
śaruppēdimaṅgalam by Kēśuvaṉ Sundarattōḷuḍaiyāṉ alias Piḷḷai Śiṅgap-
perumāḷ Kūttaṉ for offerings to the goddess in his name on his birthday every
year. The record is dated in the 36th year of the king's reign.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyum ••••• karuvūrum pā[ṇ]ṭiyaṉ
[muṭittalai]yum [ko]ṇṭu vir(ā) apiṣekamu[m vic]aiy(ā) apiṣekamum pa[ṇṇi]

paṟṟūrk kecuvaṉ cuṉtara[t*]toḷuṭaiyāṉāṉa piḷḷai ciṅkapperumāḷ [kūt]

inscription.head:
No. 150.
(A.R. No. 34 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of land by Uyyakkoṇḍāḷśāṉi, wife of Kunikkumpirāṉ-
bhaṭṭaṉ of Rājakēsari-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Nallūr-nāḍu, a
subdivision of Nittavinōda-vaḷanāḍu, as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam to god Aḻagiya-
maṇavāḷa-Perumāḷ. The gift was made in the 37th regnal year of the king.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyum īḻamuṅ karuvūrum pāṇṭiyaṉ
muṭittalaiyuṅ koṇṭu vīrā

mmaṇi uyyakkoṇṭāḷ
cāṉiyeṉ eṉ makaṉ vetanāyaka paṭṭaṉai mutukaṇṇākak ko-

ṇṭu nāṉ aḻakiyamaṇavāḷap perumāḷukkut tiruviṭaiyāṭṭamāka viṭṭa nilamāvatu
[|*] ivvūr piṭākai tirucceyalūr naratoṅkavatikku meṟku virarājentira
vāykkālukkut teṟku eḻāṅkaṇṇāṟṟu iraṇṭāñ catirattu vaṭakkaṭaiya eṉ

vittu varukiṟa nilam kal
veṭṭic cantirātittavarai ippaṭi tiruviṭaiyāṭṭamākak kaṭavatāṉa

inscription.head:
No. 151.
(A.R. No. 71 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Seems to record a gift of money to provide for
an endowment in land to the temple by a person (named Nakhilāṇḍai) for the
merit of her son ••• pōlāḻvāṉ-Daṇḍanāyakkaṉ. The details of the date are
incomplete.

l.ta:
ṇṭanāyakkaṉu[k*]ku naṉṟāka ivaṉ mātā nakilāṇṭai a •••••
•• tanta pon

inscription.head:
No. 152.
(A.R. No. 128 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers gift of land by purchase by Araiyaṉ Viradamuḍittāṉ alias
Kurukularāyar of Śevvirukkai Chakrapāṇi-nallūr in Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam, for
rearing a flower garden. The details of the date viz., 9th (8+1st) year, Siṁha,
ba, 6, Wednesday, Bharaṇi regularly correspond to A.D. 1224, August 7,
Wednesday, ·21. ·62.

l.ta:
vaḷanāṭṭu viḷānāṭṭut tiruvaraṅkat tiruppatiyil śrīvaiṣṇa(ṣṇa)varkaḷil tūppil civilli
puttūr

ṉum ivviruvomum pāṇṭimaṇṭala
ttu cevvirukkai nāṭṭu cakkarapāṇinal-

lūr araiyan viratamuṭittāṉāṉa kurukularāyaṟku icaivu tiṭṭukkuṭutta paricāvatu [|*]
tirukkuṟaippaṟṟil paḻaitāka ivaṟkuc ceytu varukiṟa tirunantavaṉattukkut tam
pe[rāl ivvūr] maṇimaṅkalattut tirumāliruñ-

colai nampi pakkal tirukkuṟaippaṟṟil vaṭavoḻukil kācu patiṉāyirattu aññūṟṟukku
vilaikoṇṭu tanta nilam yirumāvarai innilam irumāvaraiyum aḻakiyamaṇavāḷap
perumāḷukkuk kaṭamai yi-

ṟuttu iṟai mikutikoṇṭu ittirunantavaṉam cantirātittavaṟ ce[l]vomākavum
[|*] ittirunantavaṉattile nittat tiruppaḷḷittāma[m*] paṟittup paṇimāṟak
kaṭavomākavum [|*] tiruvaṭinilai-

kottuppuṟampe eḻuntaruḷiṉāl tirumālaiyum eṭuttut tirukkāṭciyuṅ kāṭṭak kaṭavo
mākavum [|*] ippaṭi cantirātittavaṟ celvatākac cammatittuk kallilum cempilum
veṭṭikku-

inscription.head:
No. 153.
(A.R. No. 72 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records an endowment of 100,000 kāśu to the temple by Dēviyār Sōmala-
dēviyār for a lamp and a flower-garden. 50,000 kāśu out of the endowment is
said to have been contributed from the treasury (bhaṇḍāram) of the queen
Sōmaladēviyār and the balance by Padineṇvishaya-Brahmachakravarttigaḷ of
Vaṅgippuṟam and Taṇṇantuḻāydāsar, a dāsa-nambi, in equal shares of 25,000
kāśu. The former amount was set apart for the supply of ghee for the lamps
(kaṟpūra-maṇiviḷakku) and out of the latter, two plots of land 8 mā in extent
for a flower garden for 20,000 kāśu and 3 vēli for 30,000 kāśu for maintaining four
gardeners for the upkeep of the garden were purchased. Dated in the 21st
year of the king's reign.

l.ta:
viyār aḻakiyamaṇavāḷap
perumāḷukku tiruppaḷḷittāmañ cāttit tirunantavaṉaṅ koḷḷavum tirunanta
vaṉañ ceyyuñ śrīvaiṣṇavarkaḷukku jivaṉattukku [nila]ṅ koḷḷavum

viyār paṇṭārattil
niṉṟum puṟappaṭṭa kācu nūṟāyira mitil tiruviḷakkukku

yamāka śrīpaṇṭārattu
oṭukkiṉa kācu aimpatiṉāyirattukkup policaiyāṟ kāciṭṭu nāḷoṉṟukkut tiruva
raṅkatevanāḻiyāle nāḻiney tirinuntā viḷakkāka

kaṟpūramaṇiviḷakkile erikkak kaṭavatākavum tirunantavaṉaṅkaḷ koḷḷavun tirunanta
vaṉañ ceyyum śrī vayiṣṇavarkaḷukku jivaṉattukku nilaṅkoḷḷavum puṟappaṭṭa kācu
aimpatiṉāyiramum iraṇṭu perāy tirunantavaṉañ ceyya veṇṭukaiyil tiruppati
śrīvayiṣṇavarkaḷil vaṅkippuṟattup patiṉeṇvi

r vacam puṟappaṭṭa kācu irupattaiyyāyiramum ākak kācu
aimpatiṉāyirattukkut tirukkuṟaippaṟṟut teṉṉoḻukil meṟkaṭaiya vaṅkippuṟattup
patiṉeṇvi

tirunantavaṉattukku vilaikoṇṭa kuḻi nānūṟṟiṉāl nilam nālumāvum ivvoḻukil
kiḻakkaṭaiyat taṇṇantuḻāy

r tirunantavaṉattil vilaikoṇṭa kuḻi nānūṟṟiṉāl
nilam nālumāvum ākat tirunantavaṉam iraṇṭiṉāl kuḻi eṇṇūṟṟināl nilam eṭṭu
māvukku vilai

ppaṭi kācu irupatināyiramum ittirunantavaṉaṅkaḷ ceyyuñ śrīvaiṣṇavarkaḷ per
nālvaṟkup peroṉṟukku nāḷoṉṟukkuc cāṉṟāpaṭi marakkālāle mukkuṟuṇi
āka nāḷoṉṟukku nellukkalattukku vilaikoṇṭa nilam [muṉṟu] velikku
vilaiyppaṭi

kācu muppatināyiramum kaikkoṇṭu ippaṭi jivaṉampeṟṟu ittirunantavaṉaṅkaḷil
tiruttuḻāy mallikai yiruvācci karumukai ceṇpakam tamanakam kūvaḷai nantiyā
vatta muḷḷiṭṭa tiruppaḷḷittāmaṅkaḷun teṅku māvum pilāvum vāḻaiyu
muḷḷiṭṭa maraṅka(ḷ)ḷum vai-

ttākki nittappaṭiyilum eḻuntaruḷun tirunāḷkaḷilum nāḷo[ṉ*]ṟukku nālu
tiruttoḷmālaiyum i[vvaṟ]kaṅkaḷil tiruppaḷḷittāmamum eṭuttuc cāttiyaruḷap
paṇṇavum ippaṭi i

edition.l: paṭi śrīvayiṣṇavarkaḷ rakṣai ||—

inscription.head:
No. 154.
(A.R. No. 52 of 1948-49.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of 1(1/2) mā of land in Tirukkuṟaippaṟṟu as Jīvaṉaśēsham to
Eḍuttakai-Aḻagiyāṉ alias Tirukkuṟuṅguḍi-Nambi by Mārattammaṉ, in the
service of Bōgaya Daṇḍanāyaka (vide No. 157 below), for supplying a flower
garland every day for the merit of the latter. The record is dated in the 21st
year of the king.

l.ta: ttammaṉeṉ iṉakku nanṟāka aḻakiya maṇavāḷap perumāḷukku

edition.l: tirumālai eṭuppatāka ittiruppati śrīvaiṣṇavarkaḷil peru[ macca] liyar

l.ta: ivaṟku naṉṟāka nittamoru tirumālai cāttak kaṭavaṉākavum itu śrī[v*]aiṣṇavarkaḷ

inscription.head:
No. 155.
(A.R. No. 363 of 1953-54.)
KOṬṬĀRAM; WALL INSIDE THE GRANARY FOR STORING RICE.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of a thousand two-hundred and two kuḻi of land in the
brahmadēya village Pūdanūr by its owner Iḷai-Āḻvāṉ-Bhaṭṭaṉ, son of [Mattaṅgi]
Vāsudēva-Bhaṭṭar of Tribhuvanavīrach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam to the shrine of
Sēnāpati Āḻvār for a lamp. The record is dated in the 22nd year of reign of
the king and the details of the date viz., Ṛishabha, ba. 5, Wednesday, Uttirāḍam
correspond regularly to A.D. 1238, May 5, Wednesday, on which day both the
tithi and the nakshatra were current throughout the day.

l.ta:
yum putaṉkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa uttirāṭattu nāḷ aḻakiyamaṇavāḷap
perumāḷ koyil śrī[ce]-

ṉāpati āḻvār śrīvaiṣṇava kaṇkāṇi ceyvārkaḷukku [mu]tal karaṇattāṟkum uyyak
koṇṭār vaḷanā-
ṭṭu puram nāṭṭu akaram śrītiripuvaṉaviraccaruppetimaṅkalattu mattaṅki

naṅ kuṭutta paricāvatu ivvūr piṭākai •••
māṇikka[vaḷa] ••• ••• ••••

[e]llaikku vaṭakkum me-

lpāṟkellai oṭampoku niroṭukālukkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai ne[ṭṭi]ṭai-
kkāl ellaikku teṟkum i-

puvaṉavīraccaruppetimaṅkalattu meṟkkaṭṭu

kaiviḷakkukku

inscription.head:
No. 156.
(A.R. No. 156 of 1951-52.)
III PRĀKĀRA, ĀRYABHAṬṬĀḶ-VĀŚAL, NICHES OF THE INNER WALL, PROPER
LEFT OF ENTRANCE.
inscription.p:
Records grant of 2 mā of land by purchase by Goppaṇaṉ, a sēṉaibōga of
Valaya-Daṇḍanāyakar for providing flower garlands to the deity for his own
merit. The land was made over to Periyakōyil-Nambi. The details of the
date viz., 23rd year, Mīna, ba. 11, Pūśam, Sunday, are irregular.

inscription.head:
No. 157.
(A.R. No. 158 of 1951-52.)
III PRĀKĀRA, ARYABHAṬṬĀḶ-VĀŚAL, NICHES IN THE INNER WALL,
PROPER LEFT OF ENTRANCE, FIRST NICHC.
inscription.p:
Gift of land to Śī(Śrī)rāmapirāṉ Bhaṭṭaṉ alias Periyakōyil Nambi by
Chaṭṭayaṉ, a sēṉaibōga of Bōgaya-Daṇḍanāyakar-Vallaiya Daṇḍanāyaka (vide
No. 154 above) for his well-being. The details of the date viz., 23rd (22+1)
year, Karkaṭaka, śu. 14, Mūla, Sunday regularly correspond to A.D. 1238,
June 27, Sunday ·91; ·48.

edition.l: svasti śrī tribhuvanaca[ kravattikaḷ]
śrīrājarājadēvaṟku yāṇṭu [ 22 āva
tu] etirāmāṇṭu kaṟkaṭakanā[yaṟṟu]
l nampikku dēvaṉ sō[ mīśvaradēvaṉ]

inscription.head:
No. 158.
(A.R. No. 157 of 1951-52.)
III PRĀKĀRA, ĀRYABHAṬṬĀL-VĀŚAL; NICHES IN THE INNER WALL, PROPER
LEFT OF ENTRANCE, SECOND NICHE.
inscription.p:
Records a gift of land by purchase from Śēnāvaruchchāṇi by Irāmayaṉ
for the daily offering of a garland to the deity for his (donor's) own well-being.
The date of the record viz., 23rd year of reign, Kuṁbha, śu. 3. Sunday, Pūśam
is irregular.

edition.l: nāya[ṟṟu] pū[ rvapakṣa]

inscription.head:
No. 159.
(A.R. No. 30 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of land by purchase, by Viṭṭaya, a Sēṉaibōya of Mahā-
pradhāni Sandhivigrahi Bhīmaṇṇa-Daṇḍanāyaka, a member of Śrīkaraṇam
of Sōmēśvaradēva, for the supply of garlands to the god Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷa-
Perumāḷ. The record is dated in the 24th year of the king's reign.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī rājarājadēvaṟku yāṇṭu 24- vatu
dēvaṉ sōmisvaradēvaṉ śrīkaraṇattāril mahāpradhāṉikaḷil santi

l.ta:
ṇṭaṉāyakkar ceṉaipoharil viṭṭayaṉeṉ eṉakku naṉṟāka
aḻakiyamaṇavāḷap perumāḷukkut tirumālai yeṭukkak[ka]-
ṭavatāka ittiruppati śrī vaiṣṇavarkaḷil perumpuliyūr tiruvāymoḻi

ṟku ivar
koḻunti iṉṉaḷa vantac cāṉi pakkalt tirukkuṟai
ppaṟṟil vaṭavoḻukil vilaikoṇṭu viṭṭa nilam oru māvaraiyum jivaṉa

inscription.head:
No. 160.
(A.R. No. 35 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of land by purchase by Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷa-Perumāḷ, son of
Irāyāṉ Koṇḍanambi-śeṭṭi of Kuḷamukku in Malaimaṇḍalam, to Tiruvaraṅga-
māḷi-Nambi of Nālūr, a Śrīvaishṇava of Tiruvaraṅgam, for a flower-garden
to god Śrīraṅganātha. The record is dated in the 26th regnal year of the king
and the details viz., Dhanus, śu. 3, Saturday, Tiruvōṇam regularly correspond to
A.D. 1241, December 7, Saturday; ·28; ·76.

l.ta:
tiyaiyum caṉikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa tiruvoṇattu
nāḷ malai maṇṭalattu kuḷamukku yirāyāṉ koṇṭanampi ceṭṭi-

yār makaṉ aḻakiyamaṇavāḷap perumāḷeṉ iṉakku naṉṟākat tiruppati
śrī [v*]aiṣṇavarkaḷil nālūṟ tiruvaraṅkamāḷi nampi

nātaṉukkut tiru
nantavaṉañ ceytu tirumālai eṭu[ppa]tāka yivaṟku nāluṟ cirāmanampi pakkal
koṇṭu viṭṭa tirunantavaṉa-

māvatu [||*] tirukkuṟaippaṟṟu vaṭavoḻukkil tiruccolaippiccar tirunantavaṉat
tukku meṟku iccirāmanampi tirunantavaṉattukku naṭuvuḷpaṭṭa tirunantavaṉaṅ
kiḻ mel kol

vaṉattu iṭṭu tiruppaḷḷittāmam vaittākki nāḷtoṟu moru tirumālai
cāttak kaṭavatākavum pa[ḷḷa](m)mum viḷainilamum paṭukaiyum payiṟ ceytu
koṇṭu koyiṟ kaṭamaiyiṟuttu mikuti jivaṉa ceṣamākak koḷḷak
kaṭavatākavum mittammañ cantirātittavaṟ-

yittu ittirunantavaṉaṅ koliraṇṭum ivaṟkuk kāṇiyākak koṇṭu
viṭṭu ippaṭikkuk kalveṭṭik kuṭutten nālūṟ tiruvaraṅkamāḷi nampikku ikkuḷa
mukku yirāyāṉ koṇṭa nampi ceṭṭiyār makaṉ aḻakiya maṇavāḷap peru-

inscription.head:
No. 161.
(A.R. No. 133 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers the gift of a garden by purchase for 20 poṉ (araimaṉai-mariyādi)
as per the prevailing rates to Iḍavai Tiruvaraṅgadāsar by Sōmakkaṉ, son of
Māchchavai, an attendant (tādi) of Sōmaladēviyār in order to serve the lord
with garlands for his own merit and for the merit of his parents. The record
is dated in the 29th (28+1) year of the king's reign.

l.ta:
ṟkun tantaikku naṉṟāka śrīraṅkanātaṉukku tiruṉantavaṉañceytu tirumālai
eṭukka iṭavai irājamahentira ••• pakkal koṇṭa tiruṉan •••••

vaṉattukku meṟkum vicaiyarāyar vāykkālukku
vaṭakku vaṇṭuvarāvati ṉampi tiruṉa

ṉokkip pokiṟapperuvaḻikku teṟku ṉaṭuvuḷppaṭṭa nilattu [p(pa)paraviṉa] maranilai
oḻi[ya*] nikki uḷḷa pūmi ta

inscription.head:
No. 162.
(A.R. No. 102 of 1938-39.)
II PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL OF THE PAGALPATTU-MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
Registers the gift of 840,000 kāśu equivalent to 1200 Varāha-gajjāṇam
by Chaṅgadēvaṇṇaṅgaḷ, a Mahāpradhāni of Sōmēśvara for offerings during
service (sandhi) instituted in the name of his son, Siṅgaṇṇa-Daṇḍanāyakkar.
The record is dated in the 31st year of the king's reign and the details of the
date viz., Makara, śu. 13, Sunday, Puṇarpūśam correspond regularly to A.D.
1247, 20th January, Sunday ·76; ·83.

ṣṇavakkaṇkāṇi ceyvārkaḷum mutal karaṇattomum kalveṭṭik kuṭuttapaṭi [|*]

inscription.head:
No. 163.
(A.R. No. 30 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Records an endowment of money, by Periyapirāṭṭi, the wife of Rāyappaḷḷi
Kaṇḍaṉ-Gōvinda-cheṭṭiyār the Ōlaikkārar of the Śrīvaishṇavas of the Periya-
kōyil in return for her maintenance out of offerings to god. The endowed
amount is stated to be her share of gold left by her husband as her jīvaṉa-
śēsha, his own share of the amount of gold having already been endowed by
Gōvinda-cheṭṭiyār himself to the deity for an ornament. The record is dated
in the 31st year of the king's reign.

l.ta:
varkaḷ ōlaikāra rāyappaḷḷik kaṇṭaṉ kovinta ceṭṭiyār akamuṭaiyāḷ periyapirāṭṭi
kaṇṭu

taṅkaḷ mutaliyār ikkovinta ceṭṭiyār tammutāna cavaṭippūṇunūl oṉṟiṉāl
pattaraimāṟ[ṟu*]

accuttūkkam poṉ nūṟṟu oru kaḻañce mukkāle mañcāṭiyil oṉṟu pāti poṉ

tantom ippaṭi iṉṉāḷ mutal peṟuvate ||

inscription.head:
No. 164.
(A.R. No. 134 of 1938-39.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SLAB IN THE VĒDAPĀRĀYAṆA-MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
Damaged and ends of lines lost. Seems to register a gift (details lost) by
Chiṅga-Daṇḍēśa (Siṅgaṇṇa Daṇḍanāyaka), minister of Hoysaḷa Sōmēśvara,
in the 32nd regnal year of the king. The details of the date viz., •••••, śu •••
śī, Sunday and Uttiraṭṭādi probably corresponds to A.D. 1247, October 13,
Sunday, (Trayōdaśī) ·98; ·63. The month was Tulā.

inscription.head:
No. 165.
(A.R. No. 147 of 1938-39.)
V PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL, RIGHT OF ENTRANCE.
inscription.p:
Registers the gift of 15 kaḻañju of gold also referred to as Varāha-gajjāṇam
to Varamtarumperumāḷdāsar towards a garland to be offered daily to god for
the merit of Kāmadēva, son of Tikkāṉai Nāchchiyār, a queen of Vīra Sōmēśvara.
The details of the date viz., the 32nd regnal year, Tulā, ba. 7, Monday, Pūśam
correspond to A.D. 1247, October 22, Tuesday (not Monday); ·20, ·1.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī irājarājadēvaṟku yāṇṭu 32— āvatu
tulānāyaṟṟu aparapa-

yum tiṅkaḷkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa pūcattu nāḷ aḻakiyamaṇavāḷap peru
māḷukkuc cāttiya-

kaṭavatākavum niccai-

inscription.head:
No. 166.
(A.R. No. 148 of 1938-39.)
V PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL, RIGHT OF ENTRANCE.
inscription.p:
Registers the gift of a garden by purchase towards the daily offering of a
big garland to Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷapperumāḷ for the merit of Iḷaiya-Perumāḷ,
son of Śiṟusiṅgāṇḍai of Āḻvārkaṇmaṭṭam, who was the son of Tiruppāṟkaḍal-
dāsar, a Śrīvaishṇava. Dated in the 4th regnal year of the king. The details
of the date viz., Karkaṭaka, śu. 5, Friday, Hasta, correspond regularly to A.D.
1249, July 16, Friday, ·24, ·61.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] [tiripuvaṉa]ccakkaravattikaḷ śrī irācentiracoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu
4- vatu ka[ ṟkaṭaka]-

rakṣai [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 167.
(A.R. No. 64 of 1892.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of two vēli of land lying uncultivated on the banks of the
river (Kāvērī) by the Śrīvaishṇavas of Periyakōyil to Ōlaimaṅgalamuḍaiyāṉ
Poṉṉambalakkūttaṉ Tiruveṇkāḍuḍaiyār and Aruḷāḷaṉ Aḻagiya Maṇavāḷap-
perumāḷ, merchants of Kulōttuṅga-chōḻapaṭṭaṉam, for supply of aval-amudu
to the god while seated on Chēra-Pāṇḍiyaṉ tambirāṉ-tiruppaḷḷik-kaṭṭil under
the Pugaḻābharaṇaṉ canopy in the Vikramachōḻaṉ-periyatirumaṇḍapam on the
occasion of Uttarāyaṇa. The inscription commences with the praśasti Samasta-
jagadēkavīra, etc. The details of the date viz., 7th year, Makara, ba. 8, Wednes-
day, Chittirai correspond to A.D. 1253, January 22, Wednesday, Shashṭhī, ·65
(not Ashṭami), ·34.
Published in the S.I.I., Vol. IV, No. 511.

l.gr:
svasti [||*] samastajagadēkavīra khaṭgaikavīra vīrarākṣasa vikhyātimaduktaralam
kārāma manukulasamuddharaṇa pracaṇḍaraṇamukhasammukharāja pracaṇḍamāruta
cōḷakulaparibhavanirākaraṇa vikrama trivarṣadhārita makuṭadvaya rājarājasarvē

inscription.head:
No. 168.
(A.R. No. 65 of 1892.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers the gift of two pieces of land one of 6(1/2) kōl and the other of 3(1/2) kōl
to Tiruppāṟkaḍaldāsar alias Tirunāḍuḍaiyāḻvār for maintaining a garden in
the former piece and using the latter for his own livelihood and to offer nine
garlands for the merit of Narasiṁha-Nāyaka, son of Lāḍadēvaya-Nāyaka, a
Mahāvaḍḍavyāpāri (merchant) and other members of the family including
his parents. The record is dated in the year opposite the 7th of Rājēndra Chōḻa
who is given the epithet Māma-Sōmīśvarapratikūla-kāladaṇḍa. The details
of the date viz., Vriśchika, śu. 5, Monday correspond to A.D. 1254, November
16, Monday, 0.85, the nakshatra was Uttirāḍam. ·11 and not Aśvati.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. IV, No. 512.

ṉukkut
tirumālai cātti aruḷat tirumālaippuṟamākat taṉ

m iṭṭu vi[lai]koṇṭa

inscription.head:
No. 169.
(A.R. No. 317 of 1952-53.)
TIRUKKUṞAḶAPPAṈ (VĀMANA) SHRINE NEAR THE VIṬṬHALARĀYAN
GŌPURAM, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Damaged and peeled off. Records an assignment of fallow land to the
Śrīvaishṇavas for being re-claimed and cultivated for offerings to god on
certain specified occasions. The record quotes the 12th regnal year of Vīra-
rājēndradēva.

l.ta: •• mevai nikki ikkoyilp parikaramāṉārkku nivantaṅkaḷukkum tiruva

inscription.head:
No. 170.
(A.R. No. 368 of 1953-54.)
KOṬṬĀRAM, WALL OF THE PADDY STORAGE ROOM.
inscription.p:
Damaged and partly built in. Refers to the redemption of some
mortgage by payment of 52 achchu and seems to record a gift of paddy by
Tīraṉ Paramēśvaraṉ of ••• ••• •••• puṟam in Malaimaṇḍalam probably for
supply of Śeṅgaḻunīr garlands. Also mentions the brahmadēya village Tirut-
taṅgāval and refers to a transanction made during the reign of Māṟavarmaṉ
Sundarapāṇḍya (I). Rājēndra of the record is evidently Rājēndra III.

l.ta:
nāṉ kuṭutta muṉṉāḷ śrī ko[māṟa]parmarāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīcu
•••••

ivvūr kālāl nellu āyira[m] kalattukkum oṟṟikoṇṭa pu[ṇai] rūpāy 2 ••
•••••

ttiraṇṭum nellu muṉṉūṟṟo[ru]pattu nāṟkalamum nikki aḻa[kiya]maṇavā •
•••••

cātti aruḷavu[m*] inilamuṭai[yā]r [maṇa]ppiḷḷai oṟṟimiṇṭa •• l śrīva •
••• ••• ••••

inscription.head:
No. 171.
(A.R. No. 94 of 1936-37.)
STONE BUILT INTO THE KŪRATTĀḺVĀR SHRINE.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Mentions the temple of Purushōttamattu-Emberumān
at Uttamaśīli-chaturvēdimaṅgalam. In characters of the 11th century A.D.
See No. 185 below.

edition.l: rvēdimaṅkalattu peruṅkuṟi sa[ bhai]

l.note: Fragmentary. The rest of the inscription is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 172.
(A.R. No. 111 of 1937-38.)
RIM OF THE SLAB CALLED MĀLAIKAṬṬIKKAL IN THE TĀYĀR SHRINE.
inscription.p:
States that this slab for flowers (pūppalagai) was the gift of Śōḻakula-
tuṅga Mūvēndavēḷār.
In characters of the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 173.
(A.R. No. 133 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL, TIERS OF THE BASE, EAST END.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary and damaged. Seems to refer to a grant of land by pur-
chase. In characters of the 11th century A.D.

l.note:
The inscription apparently commences on the tiers of the east wall which have now
been covered over by the granite slabs of the floor.
l.ta:

inscription.head:
No. 174.
(A.R. No. 134 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL, TIERS OF THE BASE, EAST SIDE.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to refer to a sale of land by the temple executives towards
certain provisions to the god. In characters of the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 175.
(A.R. No. 135 of 1947-48.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL, TIERS OF THE BASE, EAST SIDE.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Seems to refer to some grant of land for raising a flower-
garden. In characters of the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 176.
(A.R. No. 29 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL, WEST OF THE NĀḺIKĒṬṬĀṈVĀŚAL.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Mentions Rājakēsarivarmaṉ. Seems to record a provision
made for payment of a specified quantity of paddy as jīvitam for certain per-
sons (names lost). In characters of about the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 177.
(A.R. No. 33 of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to a restoration of land to the legitimate (?) lessees
who were directed to remit the specified amount of taxes. Gives the 14th
regnal year of the king. In characters of the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 178.
(A.R. No. 53 of 1948-49.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL, WEST OF THE SVARGAVĀŚAL.
inscription.p:
Seems to eulogise in verse Poṉ-Irāśadēvaṉ Āṉaikāttāṉ alias •••••
Nambi. In characters of about the 13th century. The writing is erased in
several places.

inscription.head:
No. 179.
(A.R. No. 166 of 1951-52.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NĀḺIKĒṬṬĀṈ VĀŚAL, PROPER RIGHT OF ENTRANCE, WALL
FACING WEST.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Forms the concluding portions of the record. Seems
to refer to the sale of a piece of land for a flower garden to the deity. States
that the record was ordered to be engraved on the wall called the Rājamahēn-
draṉ-tiruchchuṟṟumāḷigai.

edition.l: namum irājamahe[ ntra] ntiruccu-

inscription.head:
No. 180.
(A.R. No. 172 of 1951-52.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NĀḺIKĒṬṬĀṈVĀŚAL SOUTH AND WEST WALLS OF THE PROPER
RIGHT NICHE IN THE PASSAGE, EARLIER WRITING OF THE PALIMPSEST.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Seems to refer to some gift of land. Mentions Paḷḷi-
koṇḍamarakkāl and mañjāḍi. In characters of the 10th-11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 181.
(A.R. No. 174 of 1951-52.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NĀḺIKĒṬṬĀṈVĀŚAL, EAST WALL OF THE NICHE TO THE
PROPER LEFT IN THE PASSAGE.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Mentions a number of temple officials such as Āḷi-Nambi,
Tiruvāyikkulamuḍaiyā-Nambi, etc. In characters of about the 11th century
A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 182.
(A.R. No. 178 of 1951-52.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NĀḺIKĒṬṬĀṈVĀŚAL, WALL NORTH OF THE STONE JAMB BEARING
THE GAṆḌABHĒRUṆḌA CREST.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Gives a list of provisions made for the worship of the
deity, among which is one for the reading of the Śrīpurāṇam. The date of
the record is lost. In characters of about the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 183.
(A.R. No. 180 of 1951-52.)
NĀḺIKĒṬṬĀṈVĀŚAL, INNER PASSAGE, PROPER RIGHT.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. One of the fragments assignable palaeographically to
about the 10th century records the 5th year of a king and mentions Parān-
takap-Pallavaraiya •••• Another fragment mentions Rājēndrachōḷadēva and
Rājādhirājadēva, and a third seems to record some provision made for playing
music on the lute (paṇṇiyāḻ) and for Jalapavitram.

l.ta: m paḻa arici kuṟuṇi[yinā]l tolākka i •••••—

inscription.head:
No. 184.
(A.R. No. 329 of 1952-53.)
KŪRATTĀḺVĀR SHRINE, FRAGMENTS ON THE SOUTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Fragments. Seem to refer to a gift by purchase, of land by Mūtta
Gōvindakramavittaṉ and his brothers to the god. One of the fragments
refers to endowment to Purushōttamattu-Emberumāṉ by the Peruṅguṟi-
sabhā of Uttamaśīli-chaturvēdimaṅgalam and another mentions Śrīkaṇṭha-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 185.
(A.R. No. 330 of 1952-53.)
SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
Fragments. One of the fragments belongs to a Parakēsarivarman and
mentions [Uttamaśīl]i-chaturvēdimaṅgalam; another cites the 31st regnal
year of a king and a third refers to a vaijya (vaidya)bhōga.

inscription.head:
No. 186.
(A.R. No. 342 of 1952-53.)
TIERS OF THE OTTAIKKĀL-MAṆḌAPAM.
inscription.p:
Fragments. The first fragment cites the 16th regnal year of Rājarāja I
and the second [6]th regnal year of a Rājakēsarivarman; the third fragment
mentions Sēnāpati Kuṟavaṉ Ulagaḷandāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 187.
(A.R. No. 365 of 1953-54.)
KOṬṬĀRAM, WALL NEAR THE GRINDING MILL STONE.
inscription.p:
Partly built in. Seems to record a provision made for supply of ghee
for maintaining a perpetual lamp in the temple. Mentions Karuṇākarakkōṉ.
The details of the date viz. Śaka •••••, [Dha]nus, ba. 14, Wednesday, [Anu]sham
are insufficient for calculation. In characters of about the 13th century A.D.

yum putankiḻamai

inscription.head:
No. 188.
(A.R. No. 441 of 1954-55.)
KOṬṬĀRAM, WALL OF THE PADDY-STORAGE ROOM; ON FIVE STRAY STONES
FIXED INTO THE WALL.
inscription.p:
One of the fragments refers to an endowment of a hundred and odd (••
ttara-śata) nishkas, another to a gift of sheep, the third to a gift of buffaloes
and the fourth to a gift of land. The fragments are in characters of about
the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 189.
(A.R. No. 442 of 1954-55.)
KOṬṬĀRAM, LION PILLAR LYING IN THE COURTYARD.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Records provisions made for the offerings (peruntiruvamudu),
[bhīma]sēni-kaṟpūram, etc. In characters of the 11th century.

edition.l: ••• sēnikaṟpūram 7- kku ••• ••• ••••

inscription.head:
No. 190.
(A.R. No. 367 of 1953-54.)
KOṬṬĀRAM, INNER WALL OF THE STORE-ROOM.
inscription.p:
The inscription is in Sanskrit verse written in Grantha and Tamil. Regis-
ters a gift of the village MuṇombUTTi alias ŚrIragganallUr in Paṭṭaiya-nāḍu
(mentioned as Maṇōmaṇi in PaṇTarāshTra in the Sanskrit verse) to god Ragga-
nātha by Nallasidharasa alias Madhurāntaka Pottappich-Chōḷa as tiruviḍai-
yāTTam in Śaka 1126 (rasa-kara-śaśi-bhU 1204-5 A.D.) The genealogy of the
donor is delineated as follows in the Sanskrit verse: ŚrIkānta (i.e. Vishṇu),
(from him) Kamalāsana (i.e. Brahma), (from him) the Sun, (then) Manu,
(then) RAghava, (in their lineage) Kalikāla-Chōḷa, and in his lineage Dāya-
Bhīma, his son Petta; to him Eṟa-Siddhi, and then Tammu-Siddhi (the donor),
the younger brother of Manma-Siddhi, born of ŚrIdēvī to Eṟa-Siddhi. At
the end of the record it is stated that (a copy of) the record is to be engraved
at the entrance into the courtyard (tirumuṟṟam) of Tiruppāṟkaḍal (i.e. the
temple of Vishṇu) at Nellūr. There is an exact copy of this inscription in the Raṅganāyakulu Temple at Nellore
(N.D.I. Nellore, No. 75.)

l.gr: svastiśrīḥ [||*] śrī kāntaḥ kamalāsanastimirabhiddēvō manū rāghavō
siddhistataḥ [||*] § svastiśrī śśākavarṣē rasakara śaśibhūsammitē tammusiddhikṣō-

l.ta: raṅkanātaṟkku tiruviṭaiyāṭṭamāka viṭṭa paṭṭaiyanāṭṭu muṇompūṭṭiyāna ciraṅ-
kanallūr ittaṉmattai iṟakkiṉāruṇṭākil kaṅkaik karaiyil nūṟāyiram ko
rai vatitta pāpam koḷvān ippaṭikku nellūril tiruppāṟkaṭal tirumuṟṟattu

inscription.head:
No. 191.
(A.R. No. 117 of 1937-38.)
V PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL.
inscription.p:
Commences with the praśasti, Samasta-bhuvana-vikhyāta, etc., and seems
to describe Mānābharaṇadēva, a Pāṇḍya king with the epithets Sōmakula-
tilaka, Paramabhaṭṭāraka, Chōḷakulāntaka, etc. Seems to record a gift of land
made to the temple of Raṅganātha by Kīttidēvaṉ and Paṭṭamahādēvi for pro-
viding two garlands (vaṉamālai) to the deity. The record which is badly
damaged is engraved in characters of about the 12th century A.D.
Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XXXVIII, pp. 225 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 192.
(A.R. No. 53 of 1892.)
EAST WALL OF THE SECOND PRĀKĀRA.
inscription.p:
Records that when the temple managers colluded with the Oṭṭar in mis-
appropriating the temple funds a large assembly comprising of all the adminis-
trative bodies met at the West Tiruvīdi of Rājamahēndraṉ Tiruvīdi and re-
solved that the executive functions in the administration of the temple must
be entrusted to individuals carefully selected, excluding the defaulters res-
ponsible for the misappropriation of temple funds, by rotation. Each indi-
vidual or body may perform the function for one year. The individual must
be selected on the occasion of Śrīpushpayāgam. The record is dated in the
9th regnal year of Māṟavarmaṉ Tribhuvaṉachakravartin Sundara Pāṇḍya ‘who
gave back Śōṇāḍu’ and quotes the details Mēsha, ba 3, Friday, Viśākhā, which
regularly correspond to 1225 A.D., March 28, Friday, ·88, ·41.
(Published in S.I.I., Vol. IV, No. 500.)

coṇāṭu va[ḻa]ṅki aruḷiya śrīcuntarapāṇṭiya tevar[k*]ku yāṇṭu oṉpatāvatu

tiyaiyum veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa vicākattunāḷ
jīyar nārāyaṇa
rum koyil kūṟu uṭaiyār aḻakiya coḻa

rāyarum periya tiruppati vaiṣṇava[r*]kaḷum tiruppaṇi ceyyum jaṉaṅkaḷum śrī pākavata
nampimārum tiruvaraṅkattu

tāṅkum nampimārum tiruvācal āriyaruḷḷiṭṭa palapaṇi nimantakkāṟarum koyila
karattu

nāḷ cevikka naṭanta [patiṇeṉ] maṇṭalattu śrīvaiṣṇavarkaḷum rājamahentiraṉ
tiruviti melait tiruvitiyil kuṟaivaṟak kūṭi iruntu

karāy patiṉamuceytu ponta pattupperumāka avaravar kālattu oṭṭaroṭe kūṭi
niṉṟu koyililum nimantakkāṟar pakkalilum oṭṭukkācu kūṭṭikkuṭukkaiyālum nilai
yāḷ eṉṟu kūṭṭi tiruviṭaiyā-

ṭṭaṅkaḷil nellai eṭuttu oṭṭar[k*]ku kuṭukkaiyālum maṟṟum palavaḻikaḷāle tiru
viṭaiyāṭṭattil uṭalkaḷai aḻikkaiyāle iraṇṭu

ḷ tiruvārātaṉam orunāḷppaṭi ko[ṇ]ṭu pala nāḷ paṭi celutta ippaṭi nāḷ
viḻukaiyālum pala maṇṭalaṅkaḷil tiruviṭaiyāṭṭaṅkaḷ taṅkaḷukku paṟṟāka kūṟiṭṭu
aṭaittuk koṇṭu taṅkaḷ āḷka-

ḷ pokaviṭṭu taṇṭivanta uṭalkaḷ tāṅkaḷum oṭṭarumāka koḷkaiyālum ippaṭi
tiruvārātaṉan taṭṭuppaṭṭateṉṟum śrīpaṇṭāra uṭalkaḷ ippaṭi aḻiyāniṉṟa
teṉṟum māṟakaṅ kotittuk kūppiṭṭu tiriya-

c ceyte [iva][r*]kaḷ tāṅkaḷ [va]rumaipaṭattirikaiyālum ippotu o[ṭṭa]ṟku kā
maṉṟiye

yamāy nañ cāmantaṉāre koyilai kaikkoṇṭu iraṇṭu karai

ṉe kūṭi niṉṟu aṉṉiyāyakārikaḷāy ponta ivarkaḷ neṭunāḷpaṭa kāṇi
pole ceytu porukaiyāle ippaṭi aṉṉiyāya-
ṅkaḷ piṟantatu ippaṭi ceytu ponta ivarkaḷai tavirntu

ñ camaintavāṟe patiṉeṭṭu maṇṭalamum kūṭi iruntu
ittiruppati kottile ā-

aricaraṇālaya[ppiri]yaṉeḻuttu ippaṭi iraṇṭāyiram eḻuttiṭṭu viṭṭatu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 193.
(A.R. No. 77 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL, RIGHT OF ENTRANCE, WEST END.
inscription.p:
Registers the uḷvari issued by the king at the instance of Aiyyaṉ Maḻa-
varāyaṉ for the tax-free village Kumaranambinallūr newly formed and granted
to the temple by Nāvāyaṉ Koṇḍanambi of Kuḷamūkku, a horse-dealer (kudirai-
chcheṭṭi) of Malaimaṇḍalam, for worship and offerings. Mentions Iraṭṭakula-
kālapuram from which a portion of the land was separated to form the new
village.

Aiyyaṉ Maḻavarāyaṉ at whose instance the lands in question were
exempted from tax and Araiyaṉ Viratamuḍittāṉ alias Pallavarāyaṉ of
Chakrapāṇinallūr in Śevvirukkai-nāḍu are well-known to be high officers
who served under Māṟavarmaṉ Sundarapāṇḍya II (A.D. 1232-49) (S.I.I.,
Vol. V, No. 448) and therefore this inscription which is dated in the third
year opposite to the eleventh (A.D. 1245-46) may be assigned to him.

inscription.head:
No. 194.
(A.R. No. 45 of 1891.)
NORTH WALL OF THE SECOND PRĀKĀRA.
inscription.p:
Records the victory of Sundara-Pāṇḍya over Hoysaḷa Sōmēśvara and
the conquest of Śrīraṅgam and refers to the gifts made to the temple there.
(Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. III, pp. 7 ff.)

l.gr:
m svasti śrīḥ—yēnāsau karuṇāmanīyata daśām śrīraṅgapatmākaraḥ
kṛtvā tam bhuvanāntara praṇayinam karṇṇāṭadōṣākaram [|*] bhūyōpyatra
jagattrayībahumatām lakṣmīmavasthāpayandēvassundarapāṇḍyabhānurudayam lēbhē

layandēva śśrimadhurāmahēndranṛpatirjjāmbūnadairnnirmmamē [|*] asyō
ttuṅgabhujādribhāsurayaśaścandrōdayavya

cchādya rājatapanaḥ kanakairvvimānam raṅgē harē sta[da]padā
nabhuvā svanāmnā [|*] asthāpayat parammāpraṇakhāt suvaṇṇa smin pumām
sammalē manasīva yōgī || [3*] kōśādāttām kṣitipatiraviḥ kāṭhakakṣōṇi

tviṣā dinamaṇē[ri]va tārakāṇām śāntāruciśśayana-

raṅgabharttuśśayanamuraga
rājam śārṅgiṇō rājasūryyaḥ [|*] vilasati viharantyā yassamantēna vatyā
kavacita iva lakṣmyāḥ kōṅkumai

[|*] yasminnakta
mudīrṇṇabhāsuramahastōmē muhūrttam gataḥ paryyaptākṛtirēva
bhāskaramiva prāptaśśaśīdṛśyatē || [7*] ghōradvairathakhinnakāṭhakapuri
sampatsamākarṣiṇā dēvēnēndukulōdvahēna

vidadhē sarvvōttarakṣmābhṛtā [|*] viṣvaksēnavimānamakṣatanabhassañcāravēlō
ntiṣadrōcissa[ñcaya]va[ñci]tadyumaṇinā hēmnātibhūmnā vṛtam || [8*] kṛtasya
nṛpabhā[svat]ā ripukirīṭahēmōccayai ranēkamaṇiśōbhinō makara
tōraṇasyāntarē [|*]

sundarasya vidadhē vēśma
kṣi[ti]ndrāryyamā kōśāgāravilu[ṇṭhi]taiḥ kṣitibhujām kōṭīrahē
mōccayaiḥ [|*] adyāpya[nti]kabhūramuṣya baha[ḷai]-

rutsaṅgasthavidīrṇṇadaityarudhiravyāptēva vidyōtatē
[||] [10*] raṅgēśvarāya kṣitipālasuryyaḥ prālambamuktāsrajamunnataśrīḥ
[|*] bhītēna śakrēṇa kirīṭabhaṅgāt suradrumandattamivōpaninyē || [11*]
mūrttam yaśassvamiva sundarapāṇḍya-

dēvō muktāmayam vyadhita raṅgapatērvvitānam [|*] yasya prabhābhiranuyāti
tadīyamaulirbhāgīrathīparigatam makuṭam purārēḥ || [12*] kramukapa
nasamōcānāndikērāmramukhyaiḥ kanakanicayakaraptai

marakatapṛthivībhṛtsambhavā bhaktivallī || [13*] nṛpatiravirudārassañcaranmēru
[tu]ṅgam vyaracayadadhiraṅgam śātakumbham śatāṅgam [|*] taṭida

bhisaraṇa
śrīrvyō[mni]yasya prabhābhiśśaradavataraṇēpi prāpyatē vārivāhaiḥ
|| [14*] sarvvōttīrṇṇa mahipatirmmadhuripōrddēvasya jētā dviṣām

sphuritairmma
yūkhavisaraisandhyēva yā śamsati || [15*] raṅgāspadapraṇayinaḥ para
masya pumsō haimam suparṇṇamakarōt kṣitipālasūryyaḥ [|*]
yasy[ā*]-

grajanmana iva prabhayā sphurantyā sampadyatē jagati śaśvadaharmmukhaśrīḥ ||
[16*] āsajñayannṛparaviśśayitasya raṅgē kamsadviṣō papuṣi kāñcanamanta
rīyam [|*] yasyāmśubhiḥ kavacitēna hiraṇyagarbhē bhūyōniṣaṇṇamiva
nābhigatēna dhātrā || [17*] uccaiḥ prabhāvalayam

ūrjjitakānti haimam prādatta raṅgapatayē narapālasūryyaḥ [|*]
ābhāti yattadupakaṇṭhagatam praphulpti

nṛpatapanavitirṇṇāmāsthitō haimapīṭhīm vividhamaṇivicitrām bibhra
duccaiḥ prabhāñca [|*] jayatidhṛtamarutvatkārmmukam

raṅganāthaḥ kanakaśi[khara]śṛṅgasthāyinam kāḷamēgham || [19*] caitrē māsi
cakāra rājatapanō yātrōtsavam raṅginaśślāghyam kāntimayaistathāt
[bhu]ta

mayaissampanmayairvvāsaraiḥ [|*] nandantyēva hi tēna vismayapadam
yē bibhratē cētanāmācaitanyabhṛtōpi

yatra dadhatē vṛkṣā vikāsam param || [20*] karttum raṅ[ga]nivāsinaḥ
kamalayā sākam vihārōtsavam hēmnā sundarapāṇḍyadēvanṛpa[ti]
rnnāvannavā[nni]rmmamē [|*] yadrō[ci]ḥ khacitaiḥ kavēratanayā rōdhō
ruhaiśśāvibhirmmandāradrumaṣaṇḍama[ṇḍi]ta taṭāmanvēti mandākinīm || [21*]
śauryyā-

sālam [|*] vēlācakram vijayajanuṣā tastarē yassvadhāmnā
skandhāvārikṛta

vasumatīsīmasālāyamānam || [22*] klaptam sundarapāṇḍya
bhūmipa[ti]nā yadraṅgabharttuḥ purō dēva-

syāhyavahāramaṇḍapamabhū dvistīrṇṇamāyātavat [|*] tēnaiva pratipāditasya
haratā pṛūthvīpatīnāndhanam sauvarṇṇasya paricchadasya tadalannāsīdavasthā
panē || [23*] āpādacūḍamakarōnnarapāḷa sūryyō raṅgēśiturvva
puṣi ratnavibhūṣaṇāni [|*] yairasya vakṣasi

ratnākaram bhavati bhūya iva praviṣṭā || [24*]
dharmmadviṣassamiti sundarapāṇḍyadēvē nirmmathya pālayiti

viśvamadhijyacāpē
[|*] viṣṇōrvvibhūṣaṇamabhūdamunā vitirṇṇam raṅgālayasyatapanīya[ma]ya
ntanutram || [25*] icphām sundarapāṇḍya unnatimatim bibhratsvayā

samjñayā [ni] ṣpādyābhyavahāravārayugaḷa [nni]ṣkampasampattikam [|*] sam
pūrṇam vidadhē gabhīramudaram raṅgēśituśśārṅgiṇō yasyābhūt
bhu

masamśayamabhaṅkṣyata mērurartthē tatkētulakṣaṇamadhāsyata cēnna mīnam || [27*]
mahīpatimahaḥ patiḥ pratinarēndralōkādiva pratāparavimaṇḍalānyapahṛ
tāni dōrvvikramaiḥ [|*] suvarṇaparikalpitānyatimahānti pātrāṇya
dāt kavēragirikanyakāpuḷina[śā]yinē

rnnirmmāya samāhṛtairmmana iva svam prāmśu siṅhā
sana[m] [|*] bhūbhṛtbhā

nuradatta raṅgapatayē yat prāpya lakṣmīsakha
ssōyammērutaṭījuṣassataṭitō

hēmācchādanarāja ityadhigataprakhyātanāmnō harērhaimasyōragasūda
nasya śayanāgārasya cādhiśriyaḥ [|*] [triṇi] svāma[yāni]

inscription.head:
No. 195.
(A.R. No. 60 of 1892.)
EAST FACES OF THE FOUR PILLARS OF THE CHANDANA-
MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
inscription.p:
Records, in Sanskrit verse written in Grantha characters, the various
gifts to the deity and the gilding of several parts of the temple from out of
the gold of the second Tulābhāra ceremony performed by the king Sundara
Pāṇḍya and alludes to his victories over Siṁhaṇa and Rāma of the Hoysaḷa
dynasty, the Chēras and the Chōḷas. The king may be identified with Jaṭā-
varmaṉ Sundara Pāṇḍya I who bore the epithet Poṉmēynda-Perumāḷ or Hēmā-
chchādanarāja.
(Published in S.I.I., Vol. IV, No. 507)

inscription.head:
No. 196.
(A.R. No. 99 of 1936-37.)
SLAB SET UP IN THE MAṆAVĀḶA-MAṬHA IN THE SOUTH
UTTIRA-STREET.
inscription.p:
States that the Sundarapāṇḍyaṉ-maṭha was built for the welfare of Perumāḷ
Sundara-Pāṇḍya by Varantaruvāṉ Eḍuttakkaiaḻagiyāṉ alias Pallavarāyar of
TuñjalUr in Naḍuviṟ-kUṟṟam, a sub-division of TirumiḻalaikUṟṟa-nāḍu, for
the use of the Tridaṇḍi-sanyāsis. King Sundara-Pāṇḍya may be identified
with JaTAvarman Sundara-Pāṇḍya I of accession A.D. 1251 who bore the
distinguishing epithet Perumāḷ. A.R.E.p., 1936-37, part II, para 39.

inscription.head:
No. 197.
(A.R. No. 81 of 1938-39.)
SOUTH WALL OF CHANDANA-MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
States that Sundara-Pāṇḍya defeated the Kēraḷa and Hoysaḷa kings and
made his younger brother Vīra-Pāṇḍya, the lord of Koṅkaṇa-rājya. Refers
also to the king's having performed the Tulābhāra of gold and gilded the vimāna
of the temple. The inscription is in Sanskrit verse written in Grantha cha-
racters. The last verse of the record refers to the king as the elder brother
of Kulaśēkhara (vide, A.R.Ep., 1914, Part II, para. 19). Mentions Pārtha-
sārathi alias Kōyil-Poṉmēynda-Perumāḷ-dāsaṉ. (See No. 199 below).

edition.l: Peeled off

inscription.head:
No. 198.
(A.R. No. 82 of 1938-39.)
SOUTH WALL OF THE CHANDANA-MAṆḌAPA, RIGHT
OF GARUḌA-SANNIDHI.
inscription.p:
Seems to contain Sanskrit and Tamil verses in praise of a king, evidently
Jaṭāvarman Sundara-Pāṇḍya, who defeated the Chōḷa and Kshēma. Engraved
in Grantha and Tamil characters of the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 199.
(A.R. No. 83 of 1938-39.)
SAME WALL, BELOW No. 198.
inscription.p:
Much damaged. Seems to contain a verse in praise of king Mīṉavaṉ
(evidently Jaṭāvarmaṉ Sundara-Pāṇḍya). A section of the record however,
mentions the name Poṉmēynda-Perumāḷ-dāsaṉ alias Pārthasārathi (See No. 197
above). Engraved in characters of 13th century A.D.

l.note:
The following two lines are engraved on a section disconnected with the above—
1 pārtthasārati āṉa koyil poṉmey-
2 nta perumāḷ dāsaṉ §

inscription.head:
No. 200.
(A.R. No. 43 of 1948-49.)
SAME WALL, BY THE SIDE OF No. 44.
inscription.p:
Much damaged. Seems to record a provision made for conducting
certain festivals in the Perumāḷdēvaṉ-tirumaṇḍapam to god Lakshmī-Varāha-
nāyaṉār and the goddess Nāchchiyār instituted by Kulaśēkhara-Kāliṅga-
rāyar of Aricharaṇālaya-nallūr. The record is dated in the 8th year of Jaṭā-
varmaṉ who bears the epithet Emmaṇḍala[muṅkoṇḍaruḷiya]. The king may
be identified with Jaṭāvarmaṉ Sundara-Pāṇḍya I.

inscription.head:
No. 201.
(A.R. No. 338 of 1952-53.)
PAVITRA-MAṆḌAPAM, INNER WALL, RIGHT OF THE ĀRYA-
BHAṬṬĀḶ-VĀŚAL.
inscription.p:
Damaged and built in. Seems to record the assignment of a number of
plots of land by Perumbuṟakkaḍal-Bhaṭṭaṉ of Rājakēsari-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
during his last days, evidently as provision for worship of Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷap
perumāḷ. Among the places mentioned in describing the boundaries of
the plots are Bhuvanamuḻuduḍaiya-nallūr, Ulagamādēvi-nallūr, Neṟiyuḍaiya
perumāḷ-nallūr, Nāvuḍaipperumāḷ-vāykkāl, Chaṇḍadaṇḍa-vadi, Āditya-vadi,
Vañji-vaḻi, etc.

The inscription is dated in the 8th year of reign of Jaṭāvarmaṉ Tribhu-
vanachakravartin Sundara-Pāṇḍya and the details viz., Mīna. śu. 15, Wednes-
day, Hasta correspond to A.D. 1258, March 20, Wednesday, f.d.t. ·7, ·89.

inscription.head:
No. 202.
(A.R. No. 84 of 1938-39.)
SOUTH WALL OF THE CHANDANA-MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
Records the order of Perumāḷ Sundara-Pāṇḍya issued to Vāṇādarāya that
the management of the temple which was hitherto vested with one group (kottu)
of officials should now be extended to members of the other groups and that
the Āriyar and uḷḷūrār be appointed to guard the treasury (poṟkāval) of the
temple from the month of Āvaṇi in the 10th (A.D. 1261) year of the king, who
may be identified with Jaṭāvarmaṉ Sundara Pāṇḍya I.

l.ta: ka koṇṭu ippaṭi ceytu potavum pa-

inscription.head:
No. 203.
(A.R. No. 89 of 1938-39.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
The inscription commences with the historical introduction Samasta
jagadādhāra etc. and purports to be an order issued at the request of the king
by the god. States that the administration of the temple was hitherto con-
ducted by a body of ten persons belonging to the Kōvaṇavar kottu. (King)
Sundara-Pāṇḍya is said to have defined the activities of the temple, made
gifts as aḍukkaḷaippuṟam for maintenance of the kitchen, instituted services,
gilded the vimānas like the Śrīraṅga-vimāna as also Sundara-Pāṇḍiyaṉ-madiḷ
and the gōpura, made duplicate sets of ornaments for the god, constructed a
palanquin (śivigai) and presented several other articles made of gold. A
representative committee from all the groups (kottus) was constituted, two from
Kōvaṇavar, two from Śrīraṅgamaṟaiyōr, one from Tōdavattut-tūmaṟaiyōr, two
from Taḻaiyiḍuvār, one from Vāśal-āriyar and two from Aṟaṭṭamukki-aṇukkar
for managing these benefactions. The inscription is attested by the Royal
Secretary Kaṇṇuḍaiyāṉ Pallavaṉ Viḻupparaiyaṉ of Puduchchēri and is said
to have been engraved at the instance of Śrīraṅganārāyaṇadāsan Māttūr-
kiḻavaṉ Kurukulattaṟaiyaṉ, the manager of the temple, the Kōvaṇavar and
the Kuḍavar.

The record bears the same regnal year as the previous one but is dated
a month later.

l.ta: kottil patimu ceytu ponta itu aṉa
ṇṭu perum vācal āriyar kottile oru

inscription.head:
No. 204.
(A.R. No. 7 of 1936-37.)
EAST WALL OF THE ROOM IN THE SOUTH-WEST CORNER
OF THE THIRD PRĀKĀRA.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of land by purchase, by Matituṅgaṉ Taṉiniṉṟuveṉṟa-
perumāḷ alias Āriyachakravarti of Chakravartinallūr in Śevvirukkai-nāḍu
for supplying garlands to the god on the day of his natal star Tiruvādirai and
on certain other specified festival days. The record belonging to Māṟavarmaṉ
alias Tribhuvanachakravartin Kulaśēkharadēva I is dated in the 10th
regnal year, Dhanus, śu. 10. Monday, Aśvati, regularly corresponding to
A.D. 1277, December 6, Monday, ·53; ·81.

l.ta: ppuṟamāka tiruppaṇi ceyyum janaṅkaḷil nampūr aḻakiyamaṇavāḷap perumā-

nam nam piṟanta nāḷ tiru
vātirai i-

vaiyiṟṟukku tirumālai cātti aruḷap paṇṇavum nāḷtoṟum tirumālaiyum cātti
tiru

inscription.head:
No. 205.
(A.R. No. 6 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Records a royal order making provision for the maintainance of a per-
petual lamp to the god Kōyilpoṉmēynda Perumāḷ from the taxes due from the
shepherds. The inscription is dated in the 2nd year and the 124th day of Tri-
bhuvanachakravartin Kōnērinmaikoṇḍāṉ Sundara-Pāṇḍyadēva. The record may
be assigned to Jaṭāvarmaṉ Sundara Pāṇḍya II who ascended the throne in
1276 A.D. The regnal year quoted for the king in the record viz. 2nd year,
is too early for Jaṭāvarmaṉ Sundara Pāṇḍya I who seems to have acquired
the title Poṉ-mēynda-perumāḷ sometime after 1253 A.D., (vide A.R. Ep.,
1936-37, Part II, para 39). The date of the record will correspond to A.D.
1278.

edition.l: nāḷ 124- ṉāl nāyaṉār koyil poṉmenta perumāḷu-

l.ta: kku ney amutu tiruviḷakkukku āṭu erumai pacup pāṭṭam tirukkoṭṭakāra-

inscription.head:
No. 206.
(A.R. No. 44 of 1948-49.)
THIRD PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL OF THE DORAI-MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
Records a gift of tax-free land capable of yielding 2,000 kalam of paddy
and a revenue of 163 poṉ in the village Aṉbil in Irā[ja*]rā[ja*]-vaḷanāḍu
as maḍappuṟam to the Śrīvaishṇavas of the Avaṉivēndarāmaṉ-maṭha esta-
blished after the name of the king, by Dēvapirāṉ Aḻagiyaperumāḷ alias Sundara-
Pāṇḍya Kāliṅgarāyar of Viḷattūr in Miḻalaik-kūṟṟam. The gift seems to have
been made in the 3rd year of the king Jaṭāvarmaṉ Tribhuvaṉachakravartin
Kōnēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ. The king is evidently identical with a Sundarapāṇḍya
who is known to have been called Avanivēndarāmaṉ and who is also known
to have been born on the day of the asterism Uttiraṭṭādi (Nos. 299 and 300
of 1943-44). Since Jaṭāvarmaṉ Sundarapāṇḍya I and III were born in the
asterisms Mūla and Pushya respectively this king may be identified with Sun-
dara Pāṇḍya II who ascended the throne in 1276 A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 207.
(A.R. No. 53 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a grant of house-sites for the bhaṭṭas and for the deity apparently
Vēdanārāyaṇa-perumāḷ, free of taxes in the agaram (name not given) formed
in the king's name in Tirunārāyaṇapuram. The record is engraved below
No. 220 and is damaged. The king probably is Sundara Pāṇḍya, brother
of Jaṭāvarmaṉ Vīra-Pāṇḍya (Vide Nos. 219 and 220 below).

inscription.head:
No. 208.
(A.R. No. 12 of 1936-37.)
NORTH WALL OF THE THIRD PRĀKĀRA (INSIDE), TO THE WEST OF THE
SVARGA-VĀŚAL-GŌPURA.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of land by purchase, for rearing a flower-garden for
supplying flowers to god Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷa-Perumāḷ, by Āsūri Śrī Lakshma-
bhaṭṭa-Sōmayāji of Kaṇṇuḍai-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Pāchchil-kūṟṟam, a sub-
division of Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, one of the bhaṭṭas of the Śrīpādasēva group
of (appointed by ?) Perumāḷ Sundara-Pāṇḍyadēva. The record is dated in the
15th year, on the 21st day of Āvaṇi, of Jaṭāvarmaṉ Tribhuvanachakravartin
Sundara Pāṇḍyadēva who may be identified with Jaṭāvarmaṉ Sundara
Pāṇḍya II, whose 15th year of reign corresponds to A.D. 1290-91.

l.ta:
koccaṭaiyapaṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī cuntarapāṇṭiya
tevaṟkku yāṇṭu

ṭṭakaḷil irā[ja*]rā[ja*] vaḷanāṭṭu pāccil kūṟṟattu kaṇṇuṭaic-
caruppetimaṅkalattu

māka cātti aruḷuvatāka i[n*]nāṭṭu tiruvuṭaiyāṭṭam aḻakiyamaṇavāḷac-

āṟṟaṅkaraikku vaṭakku

inscription.head:
No. 209.
(A.R. No. 44 of 1936-37.)
EAST WALL OF THE FOURTH PRĀKĀRA.
inscription.p:
Records an exchange of land between Malaikkiṉiyaniṉṟāṉ-bhaṭṭaṉ and
Gōmaḍattu Nārāyaṇa-bhaṭṭaṉ of Vikramaśōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in
connection with certain endowment to the colony formed by Kāliṅgarāya
in the name of Perumāḷ i.e. Jaṭāvarmaṉ Tribhuvanachakravartin Vīra Pāṇḍya in
the 10th year of his reign. A portion of the land so exchanged is said to
have belonged formerly to a certain Kaṇḍiyadēvar. Among the boundaries of
the land is mentioned a village by name Tuppanāyakkar-agaram. The
details of date viz., Kuṁbha, ba. 13, Wednesday, Uttirāḍam, correspond to A.D.
1307, February 1; ·80; ·51, if meant for the regnal year 10, given in l. no. 7
of the inscription.

ṉeṟkuppaiyuṭaiyāṉ kaṇakku koyil pi[ḷ*]ḷai eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 210.
(A.R. No. 50 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a sale of land including the land acquired by exchange (vide No.
209 ante) by Gōmaḍattu Nārāyaṇa-Bhaṭṭaṉ and his brother Vaiṉatēya Bhaṭṭaṉ
to Araiyaṉ Periya-Perumāḷ alias Kāliṅgarāyar of Kaṭṭikkuṟuchchi, for the
formation of a colony, (vide No. 211 below). The record bears the same date as
the previous inscription (No. 209) viz., 10th year, Kuṁbha, ba. 13, Wednes-
day, Uttirāḍam.

l.ta:

inscription.head:
No. 211.
(A.R. No. 47 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers the sale deed of the lands purchased by Periyaperumāḷ alias
Kāliṅgarāyar from Gōmaḍattu-Nārāyaṇa-Bhaṭṭar and his brother (vide Nos.
209 and 210) for the formation of the colony Kaliyugarāma-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
named after the king. The details of date are the same as in No. 210.

l.ta:
koccaṭapaṉmarāṉa tirupuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīvirapāṇṭiyatevarkku yāṇṭu

yum putaṉkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa
uttirāṭattu nāḷ vaṭakarai

ending with ‘ivviruvom ma[ṉ] ceyvi[va]’

inscription.head:
No. 212.
(A.R. No. 49 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Purports to be an order issued by the deity while seated on the throne
called the ‘Sundara-Pāṇḍya’ under the pearl canopy called ‘Sundara-Pāṇḍya’,
in the abhishēka-maṇḍapa in the temple on the day of Kārttigai festival, in
respect of house-sites granted to the 32 bhaṭṭas in the newly formed colony
the order to take effect from the month of Chittirai in the 10th year of the king.

l.gr:
ētat trai
lōkya nirmmāṇatrāṇa samhārakāraṇam śrīmat śrīraṅganāthasya śāsanam
śāśvatamparam 2

inscription.head:
No. 213.
(A.R. No. 42 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Records the ratification of the order of the deity (in the previous record)
by the temple authorities assigning the house-sites (vide No. 212), pathways,
etc., out of the lands carved out of Vikramachōḷa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam for
the formation of Kaliyugarāma-chaturvēdimaṅgalam by Kāliṅgarāyar alias
Periyaperumāḷ, stipulating that should an occasion arise for the donees to sell
the lands so assigned to them they should confine such transaction among
themselves or the Bhāgavatas and persons of the same tenets as themselves.
The bhaṭṭas were required to measure out ghee for a lamp as tax on their house-
sites.

l.gr:

varggasya śāsanam [|*]

inscription.head:
No. 214.
(A.R. No. 45 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers the gift of the land referred to in Nos. 212 and 213 above
having been made tax-free by Valaivīśuvāṉ Periyaperumāḷ Kāliṅgarāyar of
Kaṭṭikkuṟichchi, a hamlet of Parāntakanallūr in Naḍuvil-kūṟṟam, a sub-
division of Miḻalai-kūṟṟam in Pāṇḍimaṇḍalam, to the bhaṭṭas of Kaliyugarāma-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

l.ta:
puvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ koṉerimel koṇṭāṉ pāṇṭimaṇṭalattu
miḻalaikkūṟṟattu(ṭṭu) naṭuvilk kūṟṟattu

inscription.head:
No. 215.
(A.R. No. 46 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
States that some lands which had been granted in exchange for a pathway
to Kāvērī from Maṉṟachchanallūr, Uppūr, Nochchiyam, Attāṇi, Nelkuppai
and others and included in the new colony of Kaliyugarāma-chaturvēdimaṅ-
galam were made tax-free from the 10th year of the king's reign.

nocciyam attāṇi nelkkuppai uḷḷiṭṭa ūrkaḷil ūrārkku [|*] kāverik

inscription.head:
No. 216.
(A.R. No. 48 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Records the sale of land by Gōmaḍattu Nārāyaṇa Bhaṭṭa and his brother to
Kāliṅgarāyar who in turn gave it over to the 32 bhaṭṭas of the agaram (Kaliyuga-
rāma-chaturvēdimaṅgalam). The details of date viz. 10th year, Kuṁbha
ba. 13, Wednesday, Uttirāḍam are the same as in No. 209. The land
is stated to have been sold in order to form part of the pathway to Kāvēri.

l.ta:
kaḷ śrī vīrapāṇṭiyatevarkku yāṇṭu
[pat]tāvatu kumpa nāyaṟṟu

yum putaṉkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa

inscription.head:
No. 217.
(A.R. No. 43 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of land by Gōmaḍattu Nārāyaṇa-bhaṭṭa of Vikramaśōḻa-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam to the bhaṭṭas of Kāliṅgarāyar-agaram, which was
separated from the former village. The record is engraved in continuation of
No. 213 and is dated in the 12th year, Tulā, ba. 10, Wednesday, Maghā,
corresponding to A.D. 1308, October 9, Wednesday, f.d.t. ·07; f.d.n. ·19.

l.note: Engraved in continuation of the previous inscription.

tirupuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī virapāṇṭiya tevarkku yāṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 218.
(A.R. No. 98 of 1936-37.)
FOUR INNER JAMBS OF THE GŌPURA IN THE CHITTIRAI STREET.
inscription.p:
Four labels, one on each jamb reading ‘Kaliyugarāman’ in Grantha
characters of the 13th Century A.D. Below each label is sculptured in relief the
figure of a Gaṇḍabhēruṇḍa, with a human body and two birds heads
facing in opposite directions.

inscription.head:
No. 219.
(A.R. No. 51 of 1936-37.)
EAST WALL OF THE FOURTH PRĀKĀRA.
inscription.p:
Registers gift of tax-free land to the temple of Vēdanārāyaṇa-perumāḷ
at Tirunārāyaṇapuram in Allakaṟai-nāḍu. Mentions Sundarapāṇḍya-chatur-
vēdimaṅgalam formed in the name of the king's elder brother (aṇṇāḻvi). The
Tribhuvanachakravartin Kōnērinmaikoṇḍāṉ of this record may be identi-
fied with Jaṭāvarmaṉ Vīra-Pāṇḍya of the initial year A.D. 1296-97,
in which case aṇṇāḻvi Sundarapāṇḍya may be identified with Jaṭāvarmaṉ
Sundarapāṇḍya who ascended the throne in 1276 A.D.

l.ta:
vanaccakkaravattikaḷ koṉerimelkoṇṭā ṉallakaṟaināṭṭu [tiru]
nārāyaṇapurattu vetanārāyaṇapperumāḷ koyil

inscription.head:
No. 220.
(A.R. No. 52 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This record, also attributable to Jaṭāvarmaṉ of the previous inscription
connected with and engraved below No. 219 and registers a remission
of taxes on lands in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, for money received
by the bhaṭṭas of Sundarapāṇḍya-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a hamlet of Tirunārā-
yaṇapuram in Alakaṟai-nāḍu.

l.ta:
vaṉaccakkaravattika[ḷ] konerimel koṇ(ṭa)ṭāṉ alakaṟai
nāṭṭu tirun[ā*]

inscription.head:
No. 221.
(A.R. No. 149 of 1938-39.)
SOUTH WALL OF THE CHAKRATTĀḺVĀR SHRINE.
inscription.p:
Registers the confirmation by the king of the tax-free grant of the village
Chittiravalli alias Kēraḷa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Kōṭṭaikarai-nāḍu, made by
his father (Ayyaṉ) Kulaśēkhara, for the expenses of conducting the service
called Kulaśēkhara-sandi instituted in his name in the shrine of Tiruvāḻi-Āḻvār.
Quotes the 21st year and 290th day of the king's reign. Kōṉēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ
of the record may be identified with Jaṭāvarmaṉ Vīra-Pāṇḍya, a half-brother
of Jaṭāvarmaṉ Sundara Pāṇḍya II. Ayyaṉ Kulaśēkhara evidently refers
to Māṟavarmaṉ Kulaśēkhara I.

l.ta:
vatti koṉeriṉmai koṇṭāṉ nāyaṉār tiruvāḻi
āḻvār

varkaḷukku [|*] taṅkaḷ koyil nāyaṉār tiruvāḻi
āḻvāṟku
ayyan tirunāmattāl ayyaṉ kaṇṭaruḷiṉa kulacekaraṉ canti[kku] [amutu]paṭi cāttu-
ppaṭi uḷḷiṭṭa nitta nimantaṅkaḷukkum tiruppaṇikkum koṭṭaikkarai nāṭṭuc-
cey puṉcey nattam nirnilai kuḷamum kuḷappāḻum uḷḷiṭṭa nilaṅkaḷum in-
nilattu varuṅkaṭamai kuṭimai antarāyam poṉvari iṉavari
īḻam poṉ cey cekkiṟai taṟi iṟai taṭṭoli taṭṭārppāṭṭam
oḻukku nirppāṭṭam uḷḷiṭṭa āyavaṟkamum [uṭpaṭa pa]la viṉiyokam
ilāñcaṉai viṉiyokam kāttikaip paccai cantatippaccai uḷḷiṭṭa-
tantu ippaṭikku variyilār kaṇakkiluṅ kaḻikkac coṉṉom [|*] ippaṭik-
laiyum tiruvāḻik kallu nāṭṭik koḷka [|*] ivai olaimaṅkalamuṭaiyāṉ

inscription.head:
No. 222.
(A.R. No. 40 of 1936-37.)
EAST WALL OF THE FOURTH PRĀKĀRA.
inscription.p:
Records that on the representation made by Kariyamāṇikka Āḻvār,
the Śrīkāriyam, and the other temple executives, 3645 kuḻi of temple land
were made over towards sites for the formation of a colony named Ravivarma-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam after Ravivarmaṉ alias Vēṇāṭṭaḍigaḷ and for a temple
for Ravinārāyaṇap-perumāḷ. The land for the colony is said to have
been purchased for 4,000 paṇam out of which 1,800 were collected at the rate
of 10 paṇam per kōl for private building sites and at 5 per kōl for deserted
sites, the rest going towards the cost of the temple lands. Among the peti-
tioners for the approval of the transaction figures the accountant Kōdai Uḷkka-
ṇakkappiḷḷai of Vēṇāṭṭaḍigaḷ. The petition was presented to the Lord on the
occasion of Chittirai-Vishu in the 8+1st year of Perumāḷ Sundara-Pāṇḍ-
yadēva. Since the inscription mentions Kaliyugarāmaṉ-tiruvīdi among the
details of the boundaries of the sites, which must have been evidently named
after Kaliyugarāmaṉ, a title of Jaṭāvarmaṉ Vīrapāṇḍya who ascended the
throne in 1296-97 A.D., the king of this record may be identified with Jaṭā-
varmaṉ Sundarapāṇḍya of 1303 A.D., whose 9th regnal year therefore, will
correspond to A.D. 1311-12.

l.gr:
svasti śrī [||*] ētat sarvvāmarādhīśam maulimālā vibhūṣaṇam śrīmat śrīraṅga

āṟṟaṅkaraikku teṟkum

ṭṭarkaḷ tuṟaiyil nāyaṉār ātikecavapperumāḷ koyi
lukku melaimatiḷ ellaikku mekkum teṟkum naṭuvupaṭṭa teṉvaṭalkol nāṟ-

pattaiñcukku kiḻmel kol eṇpattoṉṟiṉāl kuḻi muvāyirattaṟunūṟṟu nāṟ
pattaiñcum ivvakarattil eḻuntaruḷivikkiṟa nāyaṉār koyilu[kkum]

ṭṭarkaḷ
per nūṟṟirupatukku maṉai nūṟṟirupatukku veṇṭum iṭattukkum kāṇiyākak koḷḷa
vum [|*] ivvakarattu

ttukku āṟṟukkup pokiṟa muṭukkuttuṟai
kiḻai velikkuk kiḻakku i-

ṭṭarkaḷ tuṟaiyil
nāyaṉār (r) ātikecavapperumāḷ koyilellaikku meṟkum teṟkum kāveri
niroṭu kāluṟa uḷḷa iṭamu(ma)m

[paṇ]ṭuṭaiyārum paḻaṅkāṇiyāḷarumāy maṉaiyuṭaiyavarkaḷukku eṭuppumaṉaikkuk kol
oṉṟukkup paṇam pattum pāḻmaṉaikkuk kol oṉṟukkup paṇam añcum āka
vanta paṇam āyiratteṇṇūṟu kuṭuttu ivarkaḷ eḻutippaṇittu koṇṭa
namum taṅkaḷukkut tarukaiyil ivveṭuppu maṉaiyum pāḻmaṉaiyum nikki tiru-

nāmattuk kāṇiyāṉa nilattu uḷppaṭṭa nilattukkuk kāṇi vilaikku iraṇṭāyirat
tirunūṟu paṇam śrī paṇṭārattile mutaliṭṭu śrī paṇṭārattaravum taruvikkaiyil ip-
paṭikkuk kallilum cempilum veṭṭik koṇṭu kāṇiyum iṟaiyiliyumāka

r eḻuttu § ivai koyil kātta perumāḷ nampi eḻuttu §
ivai celvappirampu

r eḻuttu § tiruppaṇi ceyvārkaḷil āḻvār eḻuttu §
śrī pātantāṅkuvāril śrī parā[nta]ka

jaṉ eḻuttu § ivai tirumāliruñ
colaip piccar eḻuttu § ivai tiruvaraṅka māḷikaippaṭṭar eḻuttu § ivai
teṉṉavaṉ

ivai paṇiyāl koyil kaṇakkup pu ••••• ṭaiyāṉ pallavaṉ viḻupparaiyaṉ
eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 223.
(A.R. No. 18 of 1936-37.)
NORTH WALL OF THE FOURTH PRĀKĀRA, OPPOSITE THE MUDALĀḺVĀR
SHRINE.
inscription.p:
Registers a tax-free gift of 700 mā of land to 208 bhaṭṭas colonising the
agaram called Kōdaṇḍarāma-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, newly founded and named
after the king in Tiruvaraṅgam-Tiruppati in Viḷā-nāḍu, a sub-division of Pāṇḍi-
kulapati-vaḷanāḍu on the southern-bank, and for offerings and worship of
god Lakshmīnārāyaṇa-Perumāḷ consecrated in that colony. The record is
dated in the 10th year and 184th day of Tribhuvaṉachakravartin Kōnērin-
maikoṇḍān who may be identified with Jaṭāvarman Sundara Pāṇḍya III who
is well-known to have had the title Kōdaṇḍarāma. The date of the
record thus corresponds to A.D. 1312.

l.ta:
koṉeriṉmai koṇṭāṉ teṉkarai pāṇṭi
kulapati vaḷanāṭṭu viḷānāṭṭut tiruvaraṅkan tiruppatiyil nām vaitta akaram
kotaṇṭarāmac catu

nārāyaṇapperumāḷ koyilt tiruppati śrī vaiṣṇavarkaḷukkum rā[ca]
rā[ca] vaḷanāṭṭu mel kūṟṟil alakaṟai nāṭṭil cūriya tevanallūrk kaṭṭaḷaiyilum

coḻanallūrk kaṭṭaḷaiyilum
kulottuṅkacoḻanallūrk kaṭṭaḷaiyilum oṉpatāvatu varaiyum kaḻintu ponta
tevatā-

rkaḷ iṟaiyili aiyyaṉ pātti piṭāri
viḷākam tukkai pāttiyum tirunārāyaṇapurattu nāyaṉār vetanāyakapperumāḷ
tiruviṭaiyāṭṭattukkum innāyaṉār koyil cūḻnta akara

rkaḷ iṟaiyili
kkum (m) kalveṭṭiṉapaṭi uḷḷa nilamum nikki nikki niṉṟa nilattile innāya
ṉārkku amutu paṭi cāttuppaṭi uḷḷiṭṭu veṇṭum nitta nimantaṅkaḷukkum
uṭalāka paṅku pattukku nilam muppatu māvum

rkaḷ per irunūṟṟeṭṭukku
per oṉṟukku nilam muṉṟu māvāka nilam aṟunūṟṟu i-

nilam patiṉaiñcu māvum āka nilam eḻunūṟu
māvum innilattāl varum nattamum nattap payirum pattāvatu taimmātam mutal
taṅkaḷukkup pūtāṉamākat tantom iṉṉilam eḻunūṟu māvum innāḷ mutal
kārum pacānamum maṟṟum koḻuntum oru pūvum kaṭaippūvum mañcaḷiñci
vāḻai kamuku puṉpayir maṟṟum eṟpeṟpaṭṭa payirkaḷu

vittup potavum innilattāl varum kaṭamai antarāyam
kāriya ārāycci nelvari ponvari kācu kaṭamai māvaṭai puṉpayir maṉai iṟai
cekkiṟai taṭṭoli tāṭṭārappāṭṭam ērivari iṉavari iṭaivari maṉai vari oḻukku nīr
mīṉ pāṭṭam kāṇikkai kāttikaip paccai pañcu pili olai eḻuttu viṉiyokam
vācalp peṟu ilāñciṉaip peṟu āṉaiccālai kutiraip panti āḷttāḻvu taricut
tāḻvu veṭṭi nirāṇi maṟṟum eṟpeṟpaṭṭa iṟaikaḷum innāḷ mutal variyilār
kaṇakkilum kaḻittu muṉṉuṭaiyārum paḻam pe[ya*]rum mutalaṟat tavirttu muta-
laṭaṅka iṟaiyiliyākat tantom [|*] ippaṭikku ivvolai piṭipāṭāka koṇṭu

vaṟ celvatāka kallilum cempilum veṭṭikkoṇṭu mutalaṭaṅka iṟai
yiliyāka aṉupavittup potuka § ivai miḻalaik kūṟṟattuk kiḻkūṟṟuc ceyyāṉa
māṉa parā

ma pāṇṭiya nallūr ālainallūruṭaiyāṉ veṇṇeyk kūttaṉāṉa
cuntarap pāṇṭiyap pallavarāyaṉeḻuttu § ivai miḻalaik kūṟṟattu naṭuviṟ kūṟṟuk
kuḷattūruṭaiyāṉ tirumāliruñcolai niṉṟāṉāṉa pallavarāya ṉeḻuttu § yāṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 224.
(A.R. No. 360 of 1953-54.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, WALL BEHIND ĀCHCHĀVAYYAṈ SHRINE, EAST OF THE
MĒṬṬAḺAGIYAŚIṄGAR SHRINE.
inscription.p:
Partly covered up by the flooring slabs and by the intercepting walls.
Seems to give a list of the shareholders of the gift village Kōdaṇḍarāma-chatur-
vēdimaṅgalam among whom figure Irāyūr Śoṭṭai Ēkāyaṉap-Periyaperumāḷ,
Śoṭṭai Śellappiḷḷai, Kandāḍai-Bhagavad-Rāmānuja-Bhaṭṭar, etc. Regarding the
formation of Kōdaṇḍarāma-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, see No 223 above.

l.ta:
maṅkalam kaiyyolaippaṭi pattā
vatu paṅkuḷavaril nāyaṉār ko
irāyūṟ coṭṭai ekāyaṉap periya perumāḷ paṅku
⎯⎯iraṇṭām variyil iṭṭa cākkuvukku viḻunta ātaṉūr ciraṅkanā

inscription.head:
No. 225.
(A.R. No. 29 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a grant of land to the sabhā of Jagadēkavīra-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
in exchange for 3 vēli of land required for the temple of Kōdaṇḍarāma-perumāḷ
in the new colony Kōdaṇḍarāma-chaturvēdimaṅgalam (vide No. 223). The
record is dated in the 11th year and the 170th day of the king, corresponding
to A.D. 1313. Sundara Pāṇḍya Kāḷiṅgarāyar figures as one of the signatories
in the record.

l.ta:
yārkkup perumāḷ aḻakiya maṇavāḷap perumāḷ koyilil nam perāl vaitta
akaram ko

ceṣattukkuñ cuṟṟuk kulaikkum
perumāḷ tirumuṭikkuṟaikku eḻuntaruḷukiṟa tiruvitikkum uṭpaṭṭa nilattil
tirukkuṟaippaṟṟilum pāṭakat tāḻvāṉ paṟṟilum palarpakkalilum vilaikoṇṭanilam

niṅkalāka maṉṟacca viḷākattu tirunāmattukkāṇi paṟṟilum oṭamuṭaiyār veṭṭip
(p)eṟṟilum pukunta nilam muvelikkut talaimāṟāka taṅkaḷurppiṭākai jayaṅ
koṇṭacoḻanallūrk kaṭṭaḷaiyil araiyar [va]yaliṟ pantaṟkā(l)lukkut teṟku
avaṉinārāyaṇa vāykkālukkuk kiḻakku uṭpaṭṭa nilattil kār maṟuviḷaiyum
nilattile muveli nilam patiṉoṉṟāvatu kārmutaluk kuṭuttom [|*] innilam

viṭṭu aḻakiyamaṇavāḷap perumāḷ koyilil tiruppatiyārkkicaiyak kaṭamaiyum
iṟuttuk kuṭimaiyuñ ceyyum innilam muvelikkum oṭṭuppaṭiyāluḷḷa nel
lum pacāṉamun taṅkaḷ peril kaḻittapaṭiye celavu koḷḷavum ippaṭikku iv
volai piṭipāṭāka koṇṭu ca

vaṟ cellak kalliluñ cempilum veṭ
ṭuvittuk kuṭukka § ivai neṭṭuruṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu ivai cuntarapāṇṭiyak
kāḷiṅkarāyaṉ eḻuttu § yāṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 226.
(A.R. No. 19 of 1936-37.)
IV PRĀKĀRA, NORTH WALL, OPPOSITE TO THE SVARGA-VĀŚAL.
inscription.p:
Registers a tax-free gift of 106 mā of land by the king in Kōdaṇḍa-
rāma-chaturvēdimaṅgalam to the deity named Kōdaṇḍarāma-perumāḷ after
the king, and the bhaṭṭas of the colony (vide No. 223). The record
is dated in the 12th year and 102nd day of the king's reign and is engraved
in continution of No. 223. God Kōdaṇḍarāma-perumāḷ is perhaps
identical with god Lakshmīnārāyaṇa-perumāḷ of No. 223. Neṭṭūr-Uḍaiyaṉ
Kāliṅgarāyaṉ figures as a signatory in this and two following numbers. The
date of the record will be A.D. 1314 in the reign of Sundara-Pāṇḍya III.

l.ta:
kulapati vaḷanāṭṭu viḷā nāṭṭut tiruvaraṅkan tiruppatiyil nām nam perāl vaitta
akaram kotaṇṭarāma

rkaḷukkum ivvakarattu nāya
nār kotaṇṭarāmap perumāḷukkum alakaṟai nāṭṭup pala kaṭṭaḷaikaḷilum taṇṭu
yiṟaiyili aṭaippu iṟaiyiliyāy pattāvatu mutal tiṟantu palarkkum jivitamāka
vaitta kār maṟunilam ai[m*]pattu muṉṟumāvum ikkaṭṭaḷaikaḷil taricu pāḻāṉa
nilattil [tiru]ttik koḷḷa aṭaitta nilam aimpattu muṉṟumāvum āka nilam
nūṟṟu āṟumā innilam nūṟṟu āṟu māvum uḻavu kuṭimakkaḷukku kuṭi iruppu
nattamāka tanta

ciṟṟūrum ivvūr erikkuḷamum araṅkūrum pūṉaina[t*]tamum mahentiramaṅkalattukku
vaṭakkil iluppaik kuḷamum alakaṟai ūrukku terkku ceṇṭu veḷikkuḷamum
ivvūrkaḷukku ellaiyāṉa puñcekaḷum āka ivai pūtāṉa iṟaiyili ākavum
ivvūrkaḷil nāyaṉmār tevatāṉam tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam

paḷḷic
cantam ai[y*]yaṉ tukkai pātti piṭāri viḷākam uḷḷiṭṭa ivaiyum āka ivai
ka

ṉa iṟaiyiliyākavum tantom [|*] innilaṅkaḷ kārum maṟuvum oru
pūvum kaṭaip pūvum vampum puṉpayirum kamuku koḻuntu vāḻai mañcaḷiñci karumpu
ceṅkaḻuniruḷḷiṭṭu veṇṭum payirc ce[y*]tu koḷḷavum ivaiyiṟṟāl

varum kaṭamai antarāyam kāriya ārāṭci nelvari poṉvari kācu kaṭamai māvaṭai
puṉpayir kaṭamai taṟiiṟai cekkiṟai taṭṭoli taṭṭārp pāṭṭam m[au]ṉam pāṭ
ṭam pācip pāṭṭam oḻukku nirp pāṭṭam iṭaivari yiṉavari kāṇikkai kāttikaip
paccai pañcupili olai eḻuttu viniyokam vācalp peṟu ilāñciṉaip peṟu āḷt
tāḻvu taccut tāḻvu veṭṭi nirāṇi uṟai nāḻi kāverik karai āṉaic cālai
kutiraip panti toloṭṭup paṭṭaṭaiyum maṟṟum epperpaṭṭa iṟaikaḷum iṉṉāḷ
mutal variyilār kaṇakkilum kaḻittu muṉṉuṭaiyārum paḻam perum mutalil
tavirttu mutalaṭaṅka iṟaiyi
liyākat tantom ippaṭikku ivvolai piṭipāṭāka koṇṭu

varai cel
lak kallilum cempilum veṭṭik koṇṭu anupavittuk potuka § nilam nūṟ
ṟāṟu māvukkum ivai neṭṭuruṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu yāṇṭu paṉṉiraṇṭu nāḷ
nūṟṟiraṇṭu § ivai neṭṭuruṭaiyāṉ kāliṅkarāyaṉ eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 227.
(A.R. No. 20 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a further gift of 20 mā of tax-free land by the king to the same
bhaṭṭas. This record engraved in continuation of the previous inscription (No. 226)
is dated in the 12th year and 102nd day of the king.

l.ta:
koṉeriṉmaikoṇṭāṉ teṉkarai pāṇṭikulapati
vaḷanāṭṭu viḷānāṭṭut tiruvaraṅkan tiruppatiyil nām nam perāl vaitta akaram
kotaṇṭarāmac catu

rkaḷukku taṅkaḷukku alakaṟai nāṭ
ṭilp pala kaṭṭaḷaikaḷilum pattāvatum patiṉoṉṟāvatum viṭṭa nilamum nattaṅkaḷum
niṅkalāka paṉṉiraṇṭāvatu kār mutalukku ala-

kaṟai kiḻvayalil nāṭṭup potu koṇṭik kūttaṉ nilam oṉpatu māvum cūriyateva
nallūrk kaṭṭaḷai pavittiramāṇikkavatikku meṟkku ceyaṅkoṇṭa coḻa
vāykkālukku vaṭakku [muta]l kaṇṇāṟṟup paṭṭappāḻ nilam nālumāvum
pala kaṭṭaḷaikaḷilum taricu nilattilum [ma*]ṉai nilat tūrvai kāṭu pāḻilum taṅka-
ḷukku ām eṉṟa nilattile ēḻumā nilamum āka ni[la*]m irupatu māvum toṭ

nallūril
kiḻaikkaḷamum āka ikkaḷaṅkaḷum nilamum taṅkaḷukku

iṟaiyili āka
tantu ivaiyiṟṟāl varum nelk kaṭa

mai kācu kaṭamai poṉvari kāṇikkai kāttikaip paccai vācal viṉiyokam pañcu
pili cantu vikkirakap peṟu taṟi iṟai cekkiṟai taṭṭoli taṭṭārap pāṭṭam pāci
pāṭṭam oḻukkunir [miṉ*]pāṭṭam āṉaic cālai kutiraip panti ērivari yiṉavari iṭai
vari nirāṇi maṟṟum epperpaṭṭa iṟaikaḷum upātikaḷum uṭpaṭa paṉṉiraṇṭāvatu
kār mutalukku

iṟaiyiliyāka tantu vari[yi*]lār kaṇakkilum kaḻippatākac
coṉṉom ippaṭikku ivvolai piṭipāṭākak koṇṭu innilamum kaḷaṅkaḷum
kaikkoṇṭu

vaṟ cella kallilum cempilum veṭṭikkoṇṭu
aṉu

inscription.head:
No. 228.
(A.R. No. 21 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers another gift of 34(1/2) (vēli ?) of land by the king in Tuṟaiyūr in
Maḻa-nāḍu alias Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, to the bhaṭṭas and the god of the same
agaram. Engraved in continuation of No. 227. This is also dated in
the 12th year and 102nd day of the king (vide. No. 227).

l.ta:
koneriṉmaikoṇṭāṉ teṉkarai pāṇṭikulapati vaḷa
nāṭṭut tiruvaraṅkan tiruppatiyil nām nam perāl vaitta akaram kotaṇṭarāma

rkaḷukkum ivvakarattu nāyiṉār kotaṇṭarāmap
perumāḷukkum maḻa nāṭāṉa irācairāca vaḷanāṭṭu melai muṟiyil tuṟaiyūril
taṅkaḷukku tanta nilam mu[ppa*]ttu nālaraiyum inṉilattāl varum nattamaṉai
kaḷum nir nilaikaḷum tiṭarum kulaiyum varampum āṭṭaippāḻum uṭ(p)paṭa pati
ṉoṉṟā

māka tantom iṉṉilam muppattu nālarai
yum iṉṉāḷ mutal kārum maṟuvum oru pūvum kaṭaippūvum vampum koḻuntum
mañcaḷ iñci vāḻai kamuku uḷḷiṭṭa puṉpayirum karumpum ceṅkaḻunirum uḷ
ḷiṭṭu maṟṟum epperpaṭṭa payirkaḷum ceytu koṇṭu anu

vittup potavum
iṉṉilattāl varum kaṭamai antarāyam kāriya ārāṭci nelvari kācu kaṭamai
māvaṭai maravaṭai puṉpayir taṟi iṟai cekku iṟai taṭṭoli taṭṭārap pāṭṭam
mauṉam pāṭṭam pāci pāṭṭam oḻukku nir pāṭṭam iṭaivari iṉavari erivari kāṇikkai
kāttikai paccai pañ

cupili olai eḻuttu viṉiyokam vācalppeṟu lāñcaṉaip peṟu āḷ tāḻvu taccut
tāḻvu peṭṭi nirāṇi kāverik karai āṉaiccālai kutiraippanti toṭṭap
puṟavāyam maṟṟu epperpaṭṭa iṟaikaḷum iṉṉāḷ mutal variyilār kaṇakkilum
kaḻittu muṉṉuṭaiyārum paḻamperum mutalaṟat tavirttu mutalaṭaṅka iṟaiyiliyāka
tantom ippaṭikku ivvolai piṭipāṭākak koṇṭu

vaṟ cel(la)
vatāka kallilum cempilum veṭṭikkoṇṭu aṉu

vittup potuka § ivai
neṭṭuruṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu § yāṇṭu
tu nālaraikkum ivai neṭṭuruṭaiyāṉ kāliṅkarāyaṉ eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 229.
(A.R. No. 24 of 1936-37.)
NORTH WALL OF THE FOURTH PRĀKĀRA.
inscription.p:
Registers sale of a plot of garden-land to the bhaṭṭas of Kōdaṇḍarāma-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam by Śrīvaikundadāsaṉ of Tirumeyam. The record
belongs to the 2nd year of Kōnērinmaikoṇḍāṉ Tribhuvanachakravartiṉ Kula-
śēkharadēva. Since the bhaṭṭas of Kōdaṇḍarāma-chaturvēdimaṅgalam (vide
No. 228 above) figure in the transactions recorded in this and the following
numbers, Kulaśēkhara of this inscription who was a Māṟavarmaṉ, according to
the following inscriptions must be identified with Māṟavarmaṉ Kulaśēkhara
who ascended the throne in 1314 A.D., and who was therefore a junior contem-
porary of Jaṭāvarmaṉ Sundarapāṇḍya (accession 1302 A.D.) in whose name
the above mentioned village was formed. A comparison of the details of date
given in this inscription with the same details occurring in the following inscrip-
tions will show that the expression ‘edir-ām-āṇḍu’ meaning ‘the year opposite
to’ was inadvertently omitted in this inscription. Hence the details of date
given in this and the following numbers (230-34) would correspond to
A.D. 1316, January 13, Tuesday; ·53; f.d.n. ·93.

l.ta:
vaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīkulacekaratevaṟku yāṇṭu iraṇṭāvatu
[etirāmāṇṭu*] makaraṉāyaṟṟu a

yaiyum ce[v*]vāykiḻamaiyum
peṟṟa uttirattu ṉāḷ teṉkarai pāṇṭikulapati viḷāṉāṭṭut tiruvaraṅkan

kiṉa maramuṅ kiḻṉokkiṉa kiṇaṟum bhāgamum bhāgāśrayaṅkaḷum maṟṟum ep

aññūṟṟu aṟupatukkum viṟṟuk kuṭuttamaikku ivai śrī vai[ku*]nta

inscription.head:
No. 230.
(A.R. No. 23 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Records the sale of garden-land by the officials of the Eḍuttakai-Aḻagiya-
Nāyaṉār shrine at Tiruvaraṅgam to the bhaṭṭas of Kōdaṇḍarāma-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam founded in the name of Perumāḷ Sundara-Pāṇḍyadēva. The inscri-
ption is dated in the (2+1st) year of the king's reign in Makara, ba. 3, Uttiram
Tuesday; which details are identical with those of the previous record but
for the omission in the former of the ‘opposite’ year quoted.

l.ta:
yum ce[v*]
vāyk kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa uttir(āṭa)ttu nāḷ teṉkarai pāṇṭiyakulapati vaḷanāṭṭu
veḷā nā

ṭṭu tiruvaraṅkan tiruppatiyil nāyaṉār eṭuttakaiyaḻakiya nāyaṉār koyil śrīvaiṣṇava
kaṇkāṇi ceyvārkaḷum mutaṟ karaṇa[t*]tomum tirunantavaṉa nilavilai

māṇam [|*] perumāḷ cuntarapāṇṭiya tevar tirunāmattāl ivvūril vaitta
akaram kotaṇṭarāmac catu

maṅkalattup paṭṭarkaḷukku iṟṟai nāḷāl viṟṟuk
kuṭutta tirunantavaṉamāvatu tirukkuṟaippaṟṟil teṉṉoḻukil innāyiṉār tiru
nāmattukkāṇi āṉa tirunantavaṉattukku icainta kiḻpārk[e]llai irāmāṉucaṉ
cuṟṟukkulaikku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai maṉṟacca viḷākattu ellaikku vaṭakkum
melpāṟ

edition.l:
kellai tiruppati śrīvaiṣṇavarkaḷil tirumaiyyattu śrīvaikuntanātar tirunanta
vaṉattukku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai tirumuṭikkuṟaikku perumāḷ eḻuntaruḷukiṟa

viḷainilamu muḷpaṭa kiḻmel kol 16nya ittirunantavaṉam kol patiṉāṟe

viṟṟu vilai pramāṇam

muṉṟu māvil meṉokkiṉa maramum kiḻṉokkiṉa kiṇaṟum

inscription.head:
No. 231.
(A.R. No. 25 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Also records a similar transaction by Piṟaguvāḷi Nittaṉ alias Kōyilpoṉ-
mēyndaperumāḷdāsaṉ, a dāsanambi of Tiruvaraṅgam, to the bhaṭṭas of Kōdaṇ-
ḍarāma-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 232.
(A.R. No. 26 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Also records the sale of a garden-land by the officials of the shrine of the
goddess Śrīraṅganāyakī to the same bhaṭṭas.

l.note: This and the following inscriptions are engraved one below the other.

inscription.head:
No. 233.
(A.R. No. 27 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Records the sale of a garden-land to the bhaṭṭas by Aruḷāḷap-perumāḷ
alias Piṟaguvāḷi Aḻagiyaperumāḷdāsaṉ, a dāsanambi of Tiruvaraṅgam-Tirup-
pati.

ttikaḷ śrīkulacekaratevarkku yāṇṭu varuṣam
iraṇṭāvatiṉ etirāmāṇṭu makaranāyaṟṟu aparapakṣattu

inscription.head:
No. 234.
(A.R. No. 28 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a similar sale of garden-land to the bhaṭṭas by Karumāṇikkāḻvāṉ
alias Aṇukkavillidāsaṉ, a dāsanambi of Tiruvaraṅgam-Tiruppati. Among
the boundaries of the land sold is mentioned the garden of Kulaśēkhara Brah-
mārāyar alias Chollāppiḷḷai, a Śrīpādaṁtāṅgum-nambi.

l.ta:
tiyaiyum cevvāyk

nāṭṭut tiruvaraṅkan tiruppatiyil
pāṇṭiya tevar tirunāmattāl ivvūril vaitta akaram

inscription.head:
No. 235.
(A.R. No. 98 of 1937-38.)
WEST WALL OF THE FOURTH PRĀKĀRA.
inscription.p:
Registers a sale of garden-land to the bhaṭṭas of Kōdaṇḍarāma-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam by a number of persons. The record belongs to the 2+1st year of
Māṟavarmaṉ Tribhuvanachakravartin Kulaśēkhara and is dated in Mēsha,
ba. 7, Thursday, Sadayam. The details of date correspond to A.D. 1316
April 15, Thursday. Since the combination of Sadaiyam with the other details
given is impossible, it may be considered to be a mistake for Uttirāḍam or
Śravaṇam.

inscription.head:
No. 236.
(A.R. No. 97 of 1937-38.)
WEST WALL OF THE FOURTH PRĀKĀRA.
inscription.p:
Registers a sale of land to the bhaṭṭas of Kōdaṇḍarāma-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam by Kuravaiśēri Nārāyaṇa-bhaṭṭa Sarvvakratu of Nāgalamahādēvi-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam. The record belongs to the 2+1st regnal year of
the king and the details of the date viz., Mēsha, ba. 10, Sunday, Rēvati,
correspond to A.D. 1316, April 18. Here also the combination of the nak-
shatra Rēvatī with the other details is impossible and therefore it must be
taken as a mistake for Pūraṭṭādi.

inscription.head:
No. 237.
(A.R. No. 95 of 1937-38.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers a sale of land to the bhaṭṭas of Kōdaṇḍarāma-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
by Ālikkoṉṟai Dēvarāja-Bhaṭṭa of Nāgalamahādēvi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
an agrahāra in Viḷā-nāḍu, a sub-division of Pāṇḍikulapati-vaḷanāḍu. The
record is dated in the 2+1st year (vide No. 230 of the king's reign)
and the details of the date i.e., Mēsha, ba. 11 Sunday, Uttiraṭṭādi correspond
regularly to A.D. 1316, April 18, f.d.t. ·09; f.d.n. ·83. The king's name
Jaṭāvarmaṉ seems to be wrongly quoted for Māṟavarmaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 238.
(A.R. No. 98-A of 1937-38.)
WEST WALL OF THE FOURTH PRĀKĀRA.
inscription.p:
Records the sale-deed of the lands mentioned in No. 235 on the 23rd
of Vaikāśi in the same year i.e., on May 17, 1316 A.D., a month later
than the date quoted in No. 235 above.

inscription.head:
No. 239.
(A.R. No. 41 of 1936-37.)
EAST WALL OF THE FOURTH PRĀKĀRA, IN CONTINUATION OF No. 222 ABOVE.
inscription.p:
Records a tax-free gift of lands in Toḍaiyūr, Naṭṭaṇaṅguḍi, Maḻavaṉūr
and several other villages to the bhaṭṭas of Ravivarma-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
and the god Ravinārāyaṇaperumāḷ (vide No. 222) by the king Tri-
bhuvanachakravartin Kōnerinmaikoṇḍāṉ (vide No. 240 following) while he
was camping at Kaṇṇaṉūr on the 24th day of his 2+1st regnal year. Since
the village was formed in the year opposite to the eighth year of Perumāḷ
Sundarapāṇḍya identified with Jaṭāvarmaṉ Sundarapāṇḍya III (No. 222 above)
the king of this record may be identified with Māṟavarmaṉ Kulaśēkhara II.

inscription.head:
No. 240.
(A.R. No. 22 of 1936-37.)
EAST WALL OF THE FOURTH PRĀKĀRA.
inscription.p:
Engraved in continuation of No. 228 Registers a remission of
taxes on certain lands purchased by the bhaṭṭas of Kōdaṇḍarāma-chatur
vēdimaṅgalam towards temple site, garden and sites for the watchmen.
The record is dated the 2+1st year and 213th day of Tribhuvanachakravartin
Kōnērinmaikoṇḍāṉ who may be identified with Māṟavarmaṉ Kulasēkhara
II. Nārāyaṇaṉ Aṉantaṉ, a signatory in this record also figures in the
record No. 241 below.

vukkum ivvakarattil eḻun-
taruḷuvikkiṟa nāyaṉār tiruviruppukkum tiru[na*]ntavaṉattukkum nilamum

inscription.head:
No. 241.
(A.R. No. 39 of 1936-37.)
NORTH WALL OF THE FOURTH PRĀKĀRA.
inscription.p:
Records the assignment of the vṛittis to the temple, the Brāhmaṇas and
services out of the lands referred to in the previous record. The village Ravi-
varma-chaturvēdimaṅgalam is stated to have been founded in the name of the
king described as Tribhuvanachakravartin Kōnēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ by whom the
record is issued. The date at the end of the record makes it assignable to the
reign of Māṟavarmaṉ Kulaśēkkhara II (compare the numbers above). Nārā-
yaṇaṉ Aṉantaṉ, a signatory of this record figures also in No. 240 above.

inscription.head:
No. 242.
(A.R. No. 38 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Records the order (terippu) issued in the 4th regnal year of the king from
his camp at Kaṇṇaṉūr communicating the royal sanction to the remission of
taxes on 5 vēli of land left out of the 30 vēli of which 25 had already been assi
gned in favour of the bhaṭṭas of Ravivarma-chaturvēdimaṅgalam (No. 241).

l.note: Engraved below the following inscription in palimpsest.
l.ta:
nālāvatu kaṇṇaṉūril eḻuntaruḷi irukka [tiru]varaṅkaṉ tiruppatiyil
tirunāmattāl vait[ta aka]ram iravi
vitta nāyaṉār iravi nārāyaṇap perumāḷukkum pala

inscription.head:
No. 243.
(A.R. No. 37 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Registers the assignment of the 5 vēli of land referred to in the previous
record to the bhaṭṭas of the agaram on the same day specified in the previous
record.

l.ta:

inscription.head:
No. 244.
(A.R. No. 36 of 1936-37.)
SAME WALL OPPOSITE THE CHANDRA-PUSHKARAṆI.
inscription.p:
Records the remittance into the temple treasury by Śrīvaikundadāsaṉ a
sum of 2,200 paṇam, part of which was realised as cost of lands in Jagadēka-
vīra-chaturvēdimaṅgalam sold to certain bhaṭṭas of Ravivarma-chaturvēdimaṅ-
galam, part towards temple services and the rest by way of fines collected from
the Āriyar and the Kaikkōḷas. The amount so remitted was earmarked towards
the purchase of a palanquin, an aureole, etc. for the temple.

The record is dated in the 4th year, Āḍi 10, of Māṟavarmaṉ Tribhuvana-
chakravartin Kulaśēkharadēva who may be identical with Kulaśēkhara II of
the previous numbers. The date may correspond to A.D. 1317, July 7.

l.ta:
tiruppati śrīvaiṣṇavarkaḷil śrī vaikuntatātareṉ paṟṟumuṟi kuṭutta paricāvatu
[|*] ivvakara nattattukku

ttukkum tavarācaṉ paṭukaikkum vilaiyāka
niccayittu tirukkoṭṭakārattil

tiyati tirupperil piṟi-

ttut tantu paṟṟiṉa paṇam 200- m tirumuṟṟappaṟṟu jagadēcavira caturvēti
maṅkalattu paṟṟiṉa paṇam 829- m ākap paṇam āyirattirupattoṉpatum
āṉi mātam iraṇṭāntiyati ā-

teṇṭam niccayitta paṇattil kulacekaratātar tantu paṟṟiṉa paṇam 41- m
āka paṇam 1171 āka paṇam 2200

etirāmāṇṭu cittirai mātam eḻāntiya[ti*] nāḷ tiruccivikaikkum tiruvācikai[yu*]m
uḷḷiṭṭavaiyiṟṟukku nāṉ
poṟpaṇṭārattil oṭukkiṉa poṉṉile ivvāṭṭai mārkaḻi mātam

rkaḷukku śrī vaikuṉtatātareṉ [|*] ippaṇam poṟ
paṇṭārattile mutaliṭṭu kuṭuttamaikku §
l.note: One line is hidden by a masonry drain: read in situ.

inscription.head:
No. 245.
(A.R. No. 101 of 1937-38)
WEST WALL OF THE FOURTH PRĀKĀRA.
inscription.p:
Registers another sale of land to the bhaṭṭas of Kōdaṇḍarāma-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam by Dēvapperumāḷ-sāni, wife of Tiruvēṅgaḍadāsaṉ, a dāsanaṁbi
of Tiruvaraṅgam. The record is dated in the fourth regnal year and the details
quoted of the date viz., Siṁha, ba. 13, Monday, Pūśam, seem to correspond
to A.D. 1317, August 5, Friday (not Monday); ·87; f.d.n. ·13.

edition.l:
śrīmatcittikku Read ‘śrīmeykkirttikku’ mel [komā]ṟa [paṉmarā]ṉa tri[ bhuvaṉaccakrava] ttikaḷ śrī kulaśēkhara
dēvaṟku yāṇṭu 4- vatu nālāvatu siṅhanāyaṟṟu aparapakṣattu trayōda-

yum tiṅkaḷkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa pūcattunāḷ teṉkarai pāṇṭi kulapati vaḷanāṭṭu

viḷainilam naṭṭuppāḻākaiyil ne[l] kaṭamaikku vaṭaceri aḻakiya pāṇṭiya tevar
