inscription.head:
No. 605.
(A.R. No. 145 of 1938-39.)
SLAB SET UP NEAR THE NĀḺIKĒṬṬĀṈ-GŌPURA
inscription.p:
Announces the order of the Hindu sabhā of Chennapaṭṭaṇam appointed as
trustees by the Suprme Court giving publicity to the benefaction in the shape of
an endowment of one lakh of varāhaṉ made by Pachchayappa Mudaliyār of
Kāñchīpuram for feeding itinerant brāhmaṇas in the temple for 240 varāhaṉ
annually out of the interest accrued on the endowed amount and utilise the
excess interest over this amount for engaging a tutor on a salary of 5 varāhaṉ
per month for teaching English to Hindu boys at Śrīraṅgam. The charity was
to take effect form the year Śaka 1764 corresponding to 1842 A.D.

edition.l: kāptam 1764- m varuṣam kkuc cariyā-
ṉa cupakirutu varuṣam mutal varuṣam 1- kku 240 va-

l.ta: ṭi lakṣam varākaṉ poka maṟṟa mikutip

edition.l: kaṟpikkaṟataṟku mātam 1- kku 5- varākaṉ campa-

inscription.head:
No. 606.
(A.R. No. 150 of 1938-39.)
SOUTH WALL OF THE CHAKKARATTĀḺVĀR SHRINE.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Seems to register some assignment to Tiruvāḻiāḻvār i.e.,
Chakkarattāḻvār.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] nāyaṉār tiruvāḻi āḻvāṟku

inscription.head:
No. 607.
(A.R. No. 29-A of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH WALL, WEST OF THE NĀḺIKĒṬṬĀṈ-VĀŚAL
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Records a gift of land for the merit of Kōnammaṅgāru, the
mother of Kōnēṭi Timmayadēvamahārāja. The donor's name and the details
of the date are lost.

l.ta:
••••• nañcey puñcey nattam paṭukaittiṭar ••• ••• ••••
patti nikki niṉṟa rūpāy ••••• puṉcey rūpāy ṉāreṉaka

inscription.head:
No. 608.
(A.R. No. 47-of 1948-49.)
III PRĀKĀRA, TIERS OF THE PAVITRA-MAṆḌAPA
inscription.p:
This inscription consisting of six fragments appears to record some
provisions made for conducting a festival ending with the tīrtha on the day
of Punarpūśam. It is engraved in characters of about the 16th century.

inscription.head:
No. 609.
(A.R. No. 392 of 1949-50.)
BASE OF BALI-PĪṬHA.
inscription.p:
Contains an inscription in two lines of which the first reads ‘Aḻagiyama-
ṇavā ••••• Vaṅgai [p]perumāḷ Āyushmān and the second line is damaged.
In late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 610.
(A.R. No. 287 of 1950-51.)
II PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH SIDE, WALL OF THE JEWEL ROOM FACING THE
SOUTH WALL OF THE CHANDANA-MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
Contains the labels Sāḷuva Rāmarāju, Sāḷuva Kōnapparaju, Sāḷuva Kom-
marāju, Vēmūri Varadarāju and Mūrtirāju. They are in characters of the 16th
century.

inscription.head:
No. 611.
(A.R. No. 292 of 1950-51)
II PRĀKĀRA, SOUTH SIDE, BASE TIERS OF THE WALL, 2ND TIER
inscription.p:
Seems to record an endowment of 200 poṉ for offerings to the god and to
refer to the Bhūpati-uḍaiyār-tirunāḷ.

inscription.head:
No. 612.
(A.R. No. 297 of 1950-51.)
II PRĀKĀRA, TIERS OF THE VERANDAH, WEST SIDE, BELOW THE NĀYAKA IMAGES.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Records the provision made for several services for the god. The
details of the date etc., are lost. In characters of about the 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 613.
(A.R. No. 302 of 1950-51.)
II PRĀKĀRA, NORTH SIDE, TIERS OF THE YĀGAŚĀLA-MAṆḌAPA, WEST END.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Seems to register a poliyūṭṭu grant the details of which
are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 614.
(A.R. No. 303 of 1950-51.)
II PRĀKĀRA, NORTH SIDE, TIERS OF THE YĀGAŚĀLA-MAṆḌAPA, WEST END.
inscription.p: Fragmentary and badly damaged. Seems to refer to a gift by some lady.

Records the obeisance of Duṅgarāju Ayya of Pulindula (Pulivendula) to
god Raṅganātha. In late characters.

Records the obeisance of Vallabhayya (son ?) of Kummāra Ulirāyadēva.
The record is in Kannaḍa engraved in late characters.

Records the obeisance of Veṅgalaya and Sūraya, sons of Daḷavāyi Malla-
rasu of Gummaḍipunḍi. In late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 618-619.
(A.R. Nos. 327 and 328 of 1950-51.)
II PRĀKĀRA, ŚĒṈAIMUDALIYĀR SHRINE, PROPER RIGHT
OF ENTRANCE INTO THE INNER SHRINE
inscription.p:
Records the obeisance of Piḷḷai Kōnēri-Appaṉ and of Tavantīrtta Kumāraṉ
Chakkaram of Uttamankōyil. In late characters.

l.note: r is written above the line.

inscription.head:
No. 620-621.
(A.R. No. 329 and 330 of 1950-51.)
II PRĀKĀRA, ŚĒṈAIMUDALIYĀR SHRINE, PROPER RIGHT AND LEFT
PILLARS AT THE ENTRANCE INTO THE INNER SHRINE
inscription.p:
Records the obeisance of Tavatīrtta Kōmāra Chakkaram of Uttaman-
kōyil and Appi, the brother of Rāmānujaṉ and the son of Kōnēri Appaṉ.
In late characters.

edition.p: 2 Below this is written in Grantha.

inscription.head:
No. 622.
(A.R. No. 331 of 1950-51.)
II PRĀKĀRA, CHANDANA-MAṆḌAPA, PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA
inscription.p:
Mentions Nārāyaṇabhaṭṭaṉ Śrīraṅgarājan as the foremost amongst the
nambis worshipping Periya Perumāḷ (i.e. god Raṅganātha). In late charac-
ters.

Records the obeisance of Virūparāja, son-in-law of Achyutappa-nāyini
Murāri ••• ••• •••• Sūrama and Kōnēṭamma. The same incription is repeated
on all the pillars. In late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 624.
(A.R. No. 333 of 1950-51.)
II PRĀKĀRA, EAST WALL, NEAR THE GŌMUKHA
inscription.p:
Records the obeisance of Aṇṇāvi, son of Sarasvati who is called Paṅga-
yachchelvi. In late characters.

Gives the names Appamma, Śītamma, Chinnamma, Raṅga-Veṅkaṭamma
Nārāyaṇamma, Chinna-Veṅkaṭamma etc. in characters assignable to about the
16th century.

inscription.head:
No. 626.
(A.R. No. 345 of 1950-51.)
II PRĀKĀRA, STEPS OF THE VERANDAH, WEST SIDE, NEAR THE IVORY
IMAGES
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Refers to an endowment of 27 poṉ for specified offerings
to the deity. The details pertaining to the donor and the gift are lost.

edition.l: ••••• 3pu2kku puḷiyokaram taḷikai 4-kku pon_ 3u 2 ituvum inta •••••

l.ta: ••••• tu ceytaruḷi viṭṭavaṉ viḻukkāṭu uḷpaṭa viṉiyokam ākak kaṭa •••

inscription.head:
No. 627.
(A.R. No. 346 of 1950-51.)
II PRĀKĀRA, AROUND THE EDGE OF THE SANDAL-STONE KEPT NEAR
THE PERFUMERY ROOM
inscription.p: Seems to record the obeisance of an Ayyaṅgār. In late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 628.
(A.R. No. 163 of 1951-52.)
III PRĀKĀRA, ĀRYABHAṬṬĀḶ-VĀŚAL, INNER WALL, TIERS, PROPER LEFT
OF ENTRANCE
inscription.p:
Fragmentary and partly built in. Seems to refer to some endowment for
lamps, various offerings, such as dishes, betel leaves, etc., and also (jewels inset
with) rubies and diamonds. The date of the record and other details are lost.
In late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 629.
(A.R. No. 165 of 1951-52.)
III PRĀKĀRA, ĀRYABHAṬṬĀḶ-VĀŚAL, INNER TIERS, PROPER RIGHT OF ENTRANCE.
inscription.p:
This inscription, in three fragments engraved in characters of about the
16th century, seems to record a gift of land by purchase to provide for worship
and food offerings to the god at night, by Tunakaṇḍi Siṅgama-nāyaka,
grandson of Naragiri-nāyaka (cf. No. 642).

edition.l: ku[ṭi] rācavipāṭaṉāl rūpāy 2- li innila •••••
edition.p:
Another disconnected fragment near this in slightly larger characters
reads:

inscription.p:
Records the obeisance of Koṁḍamma, daughter of Bommarāju. In
characters of the 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 631.
(A.R. No. 173 of 1951-52.)
III PRĀKĀRA, NĀḺIKĒṬṬĀṈVĀŚAL, NICHE TO THE PROPER LEFT IN THE
PASSAGE, NORTH WALL.
inscription.p:
Seems to refer to a gift and the elaborate provisions of offerings made to
the temple. In late characters. The writing is very faint and illegible.

inscription.head:
No. 632.
(A.R. No. 177 of 1951-52)
III PRĀKĀRA, NĀḺIKĒṬṬĀṈ-VĀŚAL, STONE JAMB TO THE PROPER LEFT IN THE
PASSAGE, BEARING THE GAṆḌABHĒRUṆḌA CREST
inscription.p:
Badly damaged. Mentions Kaṇṇaṉūr and Pugaḷūr. The characters are
bold and assignable to the 17th century A.D. The purport of the inscription
is not clear. Above the inscription is engraved the figure of a Gaṇḍabhēruṇḍa
with its left arm raised and lower limbs greatly obliterated. The record is
not dated.

l.note: The record is highly damaged and worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 633.
(A.R. No. 184 of 1951-52.)
III PRĀKĀRA NĀḺIKĒṬṬĀṈVĀŚAL OF THE INNER WALL, PROPER LEFT.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Seems to refer to a gift of land as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam by
Bhōjanappalli Tirumalaiyaṅgār and to a gift of a village (name lost) as a sar-
vamānya for the merit of a nāyakkar (name lost). The date of the record
is lost.

l.ta: vittuk koṇṭu inta utayam koṇṭe intap poliyū—

inscription.head:
No. 634.
(A.R. No. 186 of 1951-52.)
III PRĀKĀRA, TIERS BELOW THE VĒṄKAṬĒŚA PERUMĀḶ PAINTING, PROPER
LEFT OF THE NĀḺIKEṬṬĀṈ-VĀŚAL
inscription.p:
Damaged and fragmentary. Refers to a poliyūṭṭu gift and to Vēdavyāsar.
Records the interest at 1 paṇam per cent. In characters of about the 15th
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 635.
(A.R. No. 188 of 1951-52.)
II PRĀKĀRA, TIERS OF THE CHANDANA-MAṆḌAPA, WEST SIDE,
COMMENCING FROM THE STEPS AT THE ENTRANCE.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary and built in. Seems to register a gift of the village
Kūḍalūr in Uṟaiyūr-kūṟṟam as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam to the deity. In character
of the 16th century A.D.

l.ta: •• nāṭṭu uṟaiyūr kūṟṟattu melpālāṟṟu tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam kūṭalūr

edition.p: 2. Plastered up by cement at a number of places.

inscription.head:
No. 636.
(A.R. No. 189 of 1951-52.)
II PRĀKĀRA, TIERS OF THE CHANDANA-MAṆḌAPA, WEST SIDE,
COMMENCING FROM THE STEPS AT THE ENTRANCE
inscription.p:
Partly built in. Refers to a gift of some vēli of land as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam
The details of the gift are lost. In characters of the 16th century A.D.

l.ta:
oṉpatān tirunāḷ tirutteril aruḷa iruntu amutu
ceytaruḷu vaṭai

edition.l:
••••• rulil tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam añcu iraṇṭukku rūpāy 6- li nikki maṭappuṟamāka
naṭantu vantu cetta rūpāy 1- likku irekaippaṭi poṉ •••••

inscription.head:
No. 637.
(A.R. No. 200 of 1951-52.)
II PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL OF THE CHANDANA-MAṆḌAPA (INSIDE), SOUTH END
inscription.p:
The record is very faintly engraved and no details either of the contents
or of the date can be made out.

inscription.head:
No. 638.
(A.R. No. 204 of 1951-52.)
II PRĀKĀRA, WEST WALL OF THE CHANDANA-MAṆḌAPA (INSIDE)
inscription.p:
The record is highly damaged and illegible. The details of the date are
also lost.

Fragmentary and damaged. Refers to some provision made for worship
in the temple of Kūrattu Āḻvār. The details of date are lost except for the
Śaka year 1803.

inscription.head:
No. 640.
(A.R. No. 347 of 1952-53.)
STONE-SET PENDANT IN THE TEMPLE
inscription.p:
This inscription in Marāṭhī language in Nāgarī script records apparently
the gift of the object by Rājā Śrī Śarphōjī-sāheb.

inscription.head:
No. 641.
(A.R. No. 348 of 1952-53.)
GOLD (GILDED) PALANQUIN
inscription.p:
States that Tōḷiktniyāṉ (the palanquin) which was the offering of Vijaya-
raṅga-Chokkanātha-nāyaka to god Raṅganātha, having become dilapidated
was repaired by Mr. George Francis Trevers, the Collector. The weight of gold
used is 39(3/4) seers and that of Silver 70(1/2) seers. The record is dated Śaka 1735,
Śrīmukha, Kārttikka śu. 11, Bhṛiguvāra. The details of date correspond to
1813, A.D., November 4, Thursday.

inscription.p:
Records that Kanekaṁṭi Vallānēniṁgāru of Dāsūla-gōtra sent a certain
amount of money (not specified) as an endowment for daily offerings of one
dish (taḷige) to the god. A verse in Sanskrit gives the genealogy of the chief
thus: Siṁga; his son, Mācha; Mācha's son Śrīvallabha, the lord of Gāndhārī-
giri and Gaṅgā-taṭa.

The details of the date viz., Śaka 1411, Saumya, Srāvaṇa, śu. 11, Friday
regularly correspond to 1489 A.D., August 7.

inscription.head:
No. 643.
(A.R. No. 369 of 1953-54.)
KOṬṬĀRAM, SLABS PAVED ON THE FLOOR
inscription.p:
Badly worn out. The inscription is in late Oriya characters and mentions
Bisva (Viśva)nāthadēva of Aṭhagaḍa and his queen.
The record is not dated.
1 The letter is written below the line.
2 is wrongly written for .

inscription.head:
No. 644.
(A.R. No. 443 of 1954-55.)
STONE NEAR THE BUS STOP CLOSE TO THE RĀYAGŌPURAM LINTEL.
AT THE MAIN ENTRANCE INTO THE CHOULTRY
inscription.p:
Records that the eleven free Rāmānujakūṭam in the Mūṅgikkuttu street
at Śrīraṅgam were endowed by the 24 families of the Erumaikāra-Nāyakkar
from the eleven places, namely, Kaṇṇanūr, Kuḍagu, Māṉantavāḍi, Nīlagiri,
Kaḷḷikkōṭṭai, Pāḷakkāḍu, Paḻani, Tiṇḍukkallu, Madurai, Sevagaṅgai and Kollam
The record is in late characters and bears no date.

l.ta: kaṇṇaṉūr kuṭaku māṉantavāṭi nīlakiṟi kaḷḷikkoṭṭai pālakkāṭu pa-
ḻaṇi tiṇṭukkallu marutai cevakaṅkai kollam yinta
ṭam yitukku yātāmoruvaṉ yiṭaru caiyitāl makattākiya [toṣa]tti-
